def_en: the act of admitting someone to enter
an acknowledgment of the truth of something
def_zh: 准许进入, 入场费, 录用, 承认
examples: Admission to the university depends on both exam results and an interview.

gloss_en: the act of allowing someone to enter a place or join an institution; also, a statement admitting that something is true

examples: You'll need to contact the admissions office directly to find out whether your transcripts have arrived.

gloss_en: The university department that handles applications and decides which students are accepted.

def_en: the act of enrolling
the body of people (such as students) who register or enroll at the same time
a document certifying an act of registering
(music) the sound property resulting from a combination of organ stops used to perform a particular piece of music; the technique of selecting and adjusting organ stops
def_zh: 登记, 挂号, 注册
examples: Registration for the conference closes at the end of this month.

def_zh: 登记; 定位; 对齐; 记录

gloss_en: the act of putting a name or details on an official list or record.

def_en: a fee paid for instruction (especially for higher education)
examples: The tuition fees at private universities are often very high.

gloss_en: the cost of being taught, especially at a college or university

examples: If your work hours were cut, you should apply for additional financial aid before the semester starts.

gloss_en: Money such as grants, loans, or scholarships given to students to help pay for their education.

def_zh: 保证人, 赞助者, 发起者, 倡议者, 教父
examples: The tennis tournament found a new sponsor whose logo appeared on the players' shirts.

def_en: assume sponsorship of
assume responsibility for or leadership of
def_zh: 发起, 赞助, 倡议
examples: A local bank agreed to sponsor the youth football team by paying for their new uniforms.

gloss_en: a person or organization that pays for or supports an event, activity or person, often in return for advertising.

examples: As a freshman, you're required to live in one of the on-campus dormitories.

gloss_en: A student in the first year of college or university.

examples: Registration opens a week earlier for continuing students than for new admits.

gloss_en: Students who are already enrolled at the school and are returning for another term, as opposed to new applicants.

def_en: a second-year undergraduate
examples: As a sophomore, she already knew which subject she wanted to major in.

def_en: used of the second year in United States high school or college
def_zh: 二年级学生的, 二年级的
examples: The band's sophomore album was even more successful than their first.

gloss_en: a student in the second year of a high school or college.

def_en: term of address for a disrespectful and annoying male
a third-year undergraduate
the younger of two persons
a son who has the same first name as his father
def_zh: 年少者, 地位较低者, 大学三年级学生
examples: By the time you're a junior, you should have declared a major and started planning your senior thesis.

gloss_en: A student in the third year of a four-year college program.

def_en: an undergraduate student during the year preceding graduation
def_zh: 年长者, 资深者, 毕业班学生
examples: The senior manager is responsible for making important decisions.

def_en: older; higher in rank; longer in length of tenure or service
used of the fourth and final year in United States high school or college
def_zh: 年长的, 高级的, 资深的
examples: The senior students are mentoring the junior students.

gloss_en: older or more experienced, especially in a particular job or position

def_en: a university student who has not yet received a first degree
def_zh: 大学本科生, 大学肄业生
examples: As an undergraduate, she spent her summers working in the university's research lab.
The department offers a wide range of undergraduate courses in history and politics.

gloss_en: a university student who is studying for a first degree and has not yet graduated.

examples: As a postgraduate, she spent most of her time in the lab working on her research thesis.

def_zh: 大学毕业后的, 大学研究院的
examples: He decided to pursue a postgraduate degree in engineering after finishing his bachelor's.

def_en: n a student who continues studies after graduation
s of or relating to studies beyond a bachelor's degree

gloss_en: relating to study done after completing a first university degree, or a student doing such study.

examples: Professor Reed thinks my research experience would strengthen my application to graduate school.

gloss_en: A division of a university offering advanced degrees, such as master's or doctoral programs, beyond the bachelor's degree.

examples: She had to quit school for a semester to take care of a family emergency, but she plans to re-enroll in the fall.

gloss_en: To stop attending school for a period of time; to take a leave from one's studies.

examples: He almost dropped out of school when his tuition went up, but a last-minute scholarship kept him enrolled.

gloss_en: To leave school permanently without finishing one's degree.

examples: I'm thinking of transferring to another school because my current university doesn't offer a marine biology major.

gloss_en: To move from one school or university to a different one and continue one's studies there.

examples: The commencement will be held on the main quad, and a well-known author is giving the address.

gloss_en: The graduation ceremony at which students formally receive their degrees.

def_en: a document certifying the successful completion of a course of study
examples: After two years of evening classes, she finally earned her diploma in business administration.

gloss_en: an official document showing that someone has successfully finished a course of study.

def_en: a position on a scale of intensity or amount or quality
a specific identifiable position in a continuum or series or especially in a process
a measure for arcs and angles
the highest power of a term or variable
def_zh: 程度, 度数, 学位, 度
examples: She earned a degree in engineering from a prestigious university.

gloss_en: a level or amount of something, especially a level of temperature or a stage in a process

def_en: a document attesting to the truth of certain stated facts
examples: After finishing the six-week workshop, participants receive a certificate they can list on their résumés.

gloss_en: An official document proving that someone has completed a course or program or achieved a qualification.

examples: Since I started my internship downtown, I've switched from full-time to part-time student status.

gloss_en: A student taking a full course load versus one taking fewer courses, often while working.

examples: The students' union is holding a meeting tonight to discuss the proposed increase in parking fees.

gloss_en: The organization run by students that represents their interests and organizes campus activities; also the building where it is based.

def_en: financial aid provided to a student on the basis of academic merit
def_zh: 学问, 学术成就, 奖学金
examples: She won a full scholarship to study engineering at a top university.

gloss_en: an amount of money given to a student to help pay for their education, usually because of academic ability.

examples: She funded her master's degree with a teaching assistantship in the biology department.

gloss_en: a paid position for a graduate student who assists with teaching or research, usually in exchange for tuition or a stipend.

def_en: money granted (by a university or foundation or other agency) for advanced study or research
def_zh: 伙伴关系, 共同参与, 团体, 奖学金, 友谊
examples: She won a prestigious fellowship that funded two years of research abroad.

def_zh: 合伙关系, 研究员薪金, 研究员基金

gloss_en: a sum of money awarded to a student or scholar to support advanced study or research.

def_zh: 大学生, 大学教师, 学者, 学会会员
examples: Many academics believe that interdisciplinary research is crucial for future breakthroughs.

def_en: associated with academia or an academy
hypothetical or theoretical and not expected to produce an immediate or practical result
marked by a narrow focus on or display of learning especially its trivial aspects
def_zh: 学院的, 学术的, 不切实际的
examples: She has an impressive academic record, having graduated with honors from a top university.

gloss_en: Relating to education and scholarship.

def_en: very good; of the highest quality
def_zh: 优良的, 杰出的, 出色的
examples: The chef prepared an excellent meal that delighted all the guests.

gloss_en: extremely good or of very high quality

def_en: a statistic describing the location of a distribution
(sports) the ratio of successful performances to opportunities
an intermediate scale value regarded as normal or usual
def_zh: 平均, 平均数, 一般水平, 海损
examples: The average age of students in the class is 20.

def_en: amount to or come to an average, without loss or gain
def_zh: 算出...平均数, 平均做, 均分, 使平衡
examples: The company averages 50 sales calls per day.

def_zh: 平均的, 中等的, 平常的
examples: An average student might find this course challenging.

gloss_en: The result obtained by adding several amounts together and then dividing the total by the number of amounts.

def_zh: 低于平均水平的, 差的
examples: Your midterm score was below average, so I'd suggest coming to office hours before the final.

gloss_en: Lower than the usual or expected standard, especially of grades or performance.

def_en: farther along in physical or mental development
comparatively late in a course of development
ahead of the times
at a higher level in training or knowledge or skill
def_zh: 在前的, 高级的, 先进的, 年老的
examples: You can't register for the advanced statistics seminar until you've passed the introductory course.

gloss_en: At a higher or more difficult level than the basic one.

def_en: money available for a client to borrow
an accounting entry acknowledging income or capital items
used in the phrase `to your credit' in order to indicate an achievement deserving praise;
arrangement for deferred payment for goods and services
def_zh: 信用, 信任, 荣誉, 贷款, 学分
examples: Many students rely on student loans and credit to finance their education.

def_zh: 归功于, 赞颂, 信任, 相信
examples: The success of the project should be credited to the entire team's hard work.

gloss_en: The ability of a customer to obtain goods or services before payment, based on the trust that payment will be made in the future.

def_en: something that is required in advance
def_zh: 先决条件, 必备条件
examples: A basic knowledge of statistics is a prerequisite for this advanced course.

def_en: required as a prior condition or course of study
def_zh: 先决条件的, 必要的
examples: Students must pass the prerequisite exam before enrolling in the seminar.

gloss_en: something that must exist or be done first before something else can happen or be achieved; (as an adjective) required as a prior condition.

def_zh: 并修课程(与其他课程同时修习的)
examples: The chemistry lab is a corequisite, so you have to sign up for it in the same semester as the lecture.

gloss_en: A course that must be taken at the same time as another course.

examples: Introduction to Psychology is a required course for everyone in the education program, so it fills up fast.
collocations: a required course for the major

gloss_en: A course that students must take in order to complete their degree program.

examples: I'm taking film studies as an elective course just because the professor is supposed to be fantastic.

gloss_en: A course that students choose freely rather than one that is required for their degree.

examples: Since I only need the background knowledge, the professor said I could audit the course instead of enrolling.
collocations: audit a course for no credit

gloss_en: To attend a course without doing the assignments or receiving credit or a grade.

examples: I'm planning to take a course in environmental science to satisfy my general education requirement.

gloss_en: To enroll in and attend a course of study.

def_en: easy and not involved or complicated
of or pertaining to or characteristic of elementary school or elementary education
of or being the essential or basic part
examples: The course covers only the elementary principles of economics.

def_zh: 元素的, 基础的, 初级的

gloss_en: relating to the most basic and simple parts or stages of a subject.

def_en: a substance formed during a chemical process before the desired product is obtained
def_zh: 中间物, 调停者, 中级
examples: In the reaction, an unstable intermediate forms before the final product appears.
A trusted diplomat agreed to intermediate between the two rival governments.

def_en: lying between two extremes in time or space or state
examples: This course is aimed at intermediate students who already know the basics.

gloss_en: being between two levels, stages, or points; also, someone or something in a middle position.

def_en: an announcement distributed to members of the press in order to supplement or replace an oral presentation
giving money or food or clothing to a needy person
def_zh: 施舍物, 救济品, 分发的印刷品资料, 广告物品
examples: The lecturer distributed a handout summarizing the key points of the presentation.

gloss_en: a printed sheet of information given out to students or an audience; also, money or goods given free to someone in need.

examples: According to the course syllabus, the term paper counts for forty percent of your final grade.

gloss_en: The document outlining a course's topics, readings, assignments, grading policy, and schedule.

def_en: an analytic or interpretive literary composition
a tentative attempt
def_zh: 随笔, 短文, 评论, 企图
examples: For Friday, write a five-page essay comparing the two novels we discussed in class.

gloss_en: A short piece of academic writing on a particular subject, assigned as coursework.

def_en: an unproved statement put forward as a premise in an argument
examples: She spent two years gathering data before finally writing her doctoral thesis.

gloss_en: a long piece of writing based on original research, done for a university degree; also the main idea one argues for.

def_en: a treatise advancing a new point of view resulting from research; usually a requirement for an advanced academic degree
examples: She spent two years researching and writing her doctoral dissertation on medieval trade.

gloss_en: a long piece of writing based on original research, usually done for a higher university degree.

examples: I haven't even chosen a topic for my history term paper, and it's due in three weeks.

gloss_en: A long research paper written over the course of a semester, usually counting heavily toward the final grade.

def_en: preparatory school work done outside school (especially at home)
def_zh: 家庭作业, 家里做的工作
examples: She finished her maths homework before dinner so she could watch the film.

gloss_en: school work that a student is given to do at home; also preparation done before an important task.

examples: The teaching assistant said our lab report has to include a full analysis of the sources of error.

gloss_en: A written account of an experiment done in a laboratory class, describing its methods and results.

examples: Our literature professor assigns a book report every month instead of giving quizzes.
collocations: a book report on the novel

gloss_en: A written assignment summarizing and commenting on a book the student has read.

def_en: the activity of formally presenting something (as a prize or reward)
a show or display; the act of presenting something to sight or view
the act of making something publicly available; presenting news or other information by broadcasting or printing it
the act of presenting a proposal
def_zh: 赠与, 描述, 介绍
examples: Each group will give a fifteen-minute presentation on their research findings at the end of the semester.

gloss_en: An oral talk given to a class or audience, often with slides, to explain a topic or project.

def_en: a planned undertaking
def_zh: 计划, 设计, 事业
examples: The team is currently working on a new research project to explore renewable energy sources.

def_en: communicate vividly
transfer (ideas or principles) from one domain into another
project on a screen
def_zh: 计划, 设计, 投掷, 发射, 使凸出, 放映
examples: The company projects a 10% increase in sales for the next quarter.

gloss_en: A planned piece of work that is designed to achieve a particular aim.

def_en: the point in time at which something must be completed
def_zh: 最后期限, 截止期限
examples: The students worked through the night to meet the deadline for their research papers.

gloss_en: the latest time or date by which a task or piece of work must be finished.

def_en: a mutually agreed delay in the date set for the completion of a job or payment of a debt
act of expanding in scope; making more widely available
an educational opportunity provided by colleges and universities to people who are not enrolled as regular students
act of stretching or straightening out a flexed limb
def_zh: 延长, 扩充, 范围, 扩展名
examples: I was sick all week, so I asked the professor for a two-day extension on my term paper.

gloss_en: Extra time granted beyond the original deadline to finish an assignment.

examples: The writing tutor said my arguments were strong, but my essay organization made them hard to follow.
collocations: work on the organization of an essay

gloss_en: The way the ideas and paragraphs of an essay are arranged and structured.

examples: Before you draft anything, spend ten minutes brainstorming ideas for your thesis statement.

gloss_en: To generate many ideas quickly and freely, usually before writing or starting a project.

def_en: some situation or event that is thought about
def_zh: 主题, 论题, 话题
examples: The main topic of discussion at the meeting was the proposed budget cuts.

gloss_en: a subject or issue that people think about, talk about, or deal with

examples: Before reading the whole paper, she skimmed the abstract to see whether it was relevant to her research.

def_en: consider a concept without thinking of a specific example; consider abstractly or theoretically
consider apart from a particular case or instance
give an abstract (of)
def_zh: 摘要, 提炼, 使抽象化
examples: The researchers tried to abstract the key principles from dozens of individual case studies.

def_en: existing only in the mind; separated from embodiment
examples: Justice is an abstract concept that can be difficult to define in precise terms.

def_zh: 摘录; 摘要; 抽象

gloss_en: existing as a general idea or quality rather than as a concrete, physical thing; also a short summary of a longer text.

examples: The professor wants to see our first draft next week so she can give feedback before the final version is due.

gloss_en: The initial, rough version of a piece of writing, written before revision.

examples: Make sure your final draft incorporates the comments I made on your outline.
collocations: the final draft is due Friday

gloss_en: The finished, polished version of a piece of writing that is submitted for grading.

def_zh: 志谢(对曾借助的人或作品)
examples: In the acknowledgement of her thesis, she thanked her advisor and the librarians who helped with her research.

gloss_en: A section of a paper or book thanking the people and works that helped the author.

def_en: a list of writings with time and place of publication (such as the writings of a single author or the works referred to in preparing a document etc.)
examples: Every source you cite in the essay must also appear in the bibliography at the end.

gloss_en: a list of the books and articles referred to in a piece of writing or on a particular subject.

def_zh: 野外考察, 实地考察
examples: The geology class involves two weekends of field work collecting rock samples in the mountains.

gloss_en: Practical research done outside the classroom or lab, collecting data in real-world settings.

def_zh: 家庭教师, 导师, 助教, 监护人
examples: Her parents hired a private tutor to help her prepare for the exam.

def_en: be a tutor to someone; give individual instruction
act as a guardian to someone
def_zh: 当...的教师, 教, 指导, 约束, 克制
当家庭教师, 受家庭教师的指导
examples: He earns extra money by tutoring high school students in mathematics.

gloss_en: a person who teaches a single student or a small group; also, to teach in this way.

def_en: a session of intensive tuition given by a tutor to an individual or to a small number of students
def_zh: 个别指导, 论文, 学习指南, 指导手册
examples: Each week the students meet their supervisor for a one-hour tutorial on their essays.

def_en: of or relating to tutors or tutoring
def_zh: 个别指导的, 家庭教师的, 辅导的
examples: The tutorial system at the university encourages close discussion between staff and students.

gloss_en: a lesson or class in which a teacher works with one student or a small group.

examples: The instructor posted the review sheet online, so check the course website before the exam.

gloss_en: A person who teaches a course at a college or university, often below the rank of professor.

examples: The administration staff at the registrar's office can fix the error on your enrollment record.

gloss_en: The non-teaching employees who manage the day-to-day operations of a school or university.

examples: You should meet with your academic advisor before registration to make sure the courses count toward your degree.

gloss_en: A faculty or staff member who guides students in choosing courses and planning their academic path.

def_en: one who supervises or has charge and direction of
def_zh: 管理者, 监督者, 指导者, 视导员
examples: If you have a problem with your shift, you should speak to your supervisor first.

gloss_en: a person who is in charge of an activity, a place, or a group of workers and makes sure the work is done properly.

examples: The internship coordinator can put you in touch with companies that hire students for the summer.

gloss_en: A person responsible for organizing a program or activity and making sure its different parts work together.

def_en: a professional person trained in library science and engaged in library services
def_zh: 图书馆员, 图书管理员
examples: The librarian showed the students how to search the online catalogue for research articles.

def_zh: 程序库管理程序; 程序库生成程序

gloss_en: a person who works in a library, helping people find books and managing its collection.

def_en: a specialized division of a large organization
the territorial and administrative division of some countries (such as France)
a specialized sphere of knowledge
examples: The marketing department is responsible for promoting the company's products.

gloss_en: a part of a large organization, such as a university or a government, that deals with a particular subject or activity

def_en: an administrator in charge of a division of a university or college
United States film actor whose moody rebellious roles made him a cult figure (1931-1955)
a man who is the senior member of a group
(Roman Catholic Church) the head of the College of Cardinals
examples: The dean of the faculty welcomed the new students at the opening ceremony.

def_zh: 外交团长, 学院院长, 教务长

gloss_en: a senior official at a university or college who is in charge of a particular department or division.

def_en: someone who is a member of the faculty at a college or university
examples: The professor of economics was known for his insightful lectures and publications.

gloss_en: a teacher of high rank in a university or college

examples: Dr. Alvarez was just promoted to associate professor after her latest research was published.

gloss_en: A mid-rank university teacher, above assistant professor and below full professor.

examples: The department hired a new lecturer to cover the introductory writing sections this fall.

gloss_en: A person who teaches courses at a university, typically hired to teach rather than to do research.

examples: If you have questions about the homework, the assistant teacher holds a help session on Thursdays.
collocations: the assistant teacher for the course
grading done by the assistant teacher

gloss_en: A person who helps the main teacher of a course, for example by leading discussion sections or grading.

def_zh: 过期的, 未兑的, 迟到的, 早就成熟的
examples: She had to pay a small fine because the library books were three weeks overdue.

def_en: s past due; not paid at the scheduled time

gloss_en: not done, paid, or returned by the expected or required time; late

examples: The book you need isn't on reserve, so you'll have to find it in the stacks on the fourth floor.
collocations: retrieve a book from the stacks

gloss_en: The rows of shelves in a library where most of the books are stored.

def_en: a request by the manufacturer of a defective product to return the product (as for replacement or repair)
a call to return
a bugle call that signals troops to return
the process of remembering (especially the process of recovering information by mental effort)
def_zh: 回忆, 召回, 撤消
examples: The company issued a product recall due to a safety defect.

def_zh: 回想, 回忆, 召回, 撤消, 使恢复
examples: I can't recall exactly where I left my keys this morning.

gloss_en: To remember something from the past; also, to officially order the return of a product.

examples: You can't check anything out until you've activated your library card at the front desk.

gloss_en: The card that identifies a user and allows them to borrow materials from the library.

def_en: a literary work based on the imagination and not necessarily on fact
examples: He prefers reading fiction to biography because he enjoys getting lost in imaginary worlds.

def_zh: 虚构的事实, 捏造, 拟制

gloss_en: stories, novels, and other writing about imaginary people and events rather than real ones

def_zh: 非小说类(如词典、参考书)
examples: The non-fiction section on the second floor has everything from biographies to science writing.

gloss_en: Books and writing about real facts and events rather than invented stories, such as reference works.

examples: Reference books can't leave the library, so you'll have to photocopy the pages you need.

gloss_en: A book, such as a dictionary or encyclopedia, that is consulted for information and usually cannot be borrowed.

def_en: a reference work (often in several volumes) containing articles on various topics (often arranged in alphabetical order) dealing with the entire range of human knowledge or with some particular specialty
examples: The encyclopedia is a valuable resource for students and researchers.

gloss_en: a book or online resource that contains a wide range of information on many subjects

def_en: a publication that appears at fixed intervals
examples: The library subscribes to dozens of scientific periodicals from around the world.

def_zh: 定期出版的, (有关)期刊的, 间歇(性的), 周期的, 定期的
examples: The committee holds periodical reviews to check that targets are being met.

gloss_en: a magazine or journal published at regular intervals; (adjective) happening or appearing regularly.

def_en: a periodic publication containing pictures and stories and articles of interest to those who purchase it or subscribe to it
product consisting of a paperback periodic publication as a physical object
a business firm that publishes magazines
a light-tight supply chamber holding the film and supplying it for exposure as required
def_zh: 杂志, 仓库, 弹盒, 胶卷盒
examples: I subscribe to a magazine that focuses on travel and culture.

def_zh: 卡片箱, 介质装卸程序

gloss_en: a publication that is issued regularly, usually weekly or monthly, and contains articles, stories, and pictures

examples: The current issue of the journal is on the display rack; older ones are shelved in the periodicals room.
collocations: the current issue of a journal
on display in the current issue rack

gloss_en: The most recent edition of a magazine or journal.

examples: You can request back issues of the magazine from the basement archive at the circulation desk.

gloss_en: An earlier edition of a magazine or journal, published before the most recent one.

def_en: a periodical dedicated to a particular subject
a record book as a physical object
the part of the axle contained by a bearing
def_zh: 日记, 杂志, 日报
examples: Her research was published in a leading scientific journal last month.

gloss_en: a serious magazine or newspaper that deals with a particular subject or profession.

def_en: (Greek mythology) a Titan who was forced by Zeus to bear the sky on his shoulders
a collection of maps in book form
the 1st cervical vertebra
a figure of a man used as a supporting column
examples: She pulled a world atlas off the shelf to find the small country mentioned in the article.

def_zh: 自动测试和负载分析系统

gloss_en: a book of maps.

examples: For last year's rainfall statistics, just check the almanac in the reference section.
collocations: look it up in the almanac

gloss_en: A reference book published yearly containing facts, statistics, and a calendar of information.

def_en: the visible part of a television transmission
(computer science) the appearance of text and graphics on a video display
examples: I watched a video on YouTube about how to cook a new recipe.
The video store has a wide selection of movies to rent.

gloss_en: a recording of moving images and sound, or a device for playing back such recordings

examples: The media room still has a VCR, since some of the older lecture recordings are only on tape.
collocations: play a tape on the VCR
a VCR in the media room

gloss_en: A machine for playing and recording videotapes.

examples: You can find peer-reviewed articles for your paper through the library's online databases.

gloss_en: A searchable electronic collection of articles, journals, or records accessed through the internet.

def_en: a photographic copy of written or printed or graphic work
examples: Please bring a photocopy of your passport when you register for the course.

def_en: reproduce by xerography
examples: The assistant photocopied the report so that everyone at the meeting had a copy.

gloss_en: a copy of a document made by a machine that photographs it; to make such a copy.

examples: The printer on the first floor is out of order, so send your document to the one in the computer lab.
collocations: the printer is out of paper
send a file to the printer

gloss_en: A machine that produces paper copies of documents from a computer.

examples: Write down the call number from the catalogue so you can find the book in the stacks.

examples: Let's grab coffee at the student union after the lecture; there's a study lounge upstairs.

examples: They signed a contract for the purchase of the new property.

def_en: enter into a contractual arrangement
def_zh: 使皱缩, 使缩短, 感染, 订约, 缔结
皱缩, 订约, 收缩
examples: The muscles in your leg contract and relax as you walk.

gloss_en: A written or spoken agreement, especially one concerning employment, sales, or tenancy, that is intended to be enforceable by law.

def_zh: 使合并, 使消失, 吞没
examples: The two small companies decided to merge in order to compete with larger rivals.

def_en: v become one
v mix together different elements
v join or combine

gloss_en: to combine or join together to form a single thing.

def_en: the act of contracting or assuming or acquiring possession of something
something acquired
examples: The company's recent acquisition of a smaller rival doubled its share of the market.

def_zh: 收购, 招揽, 取得

gloss_en: the act of gaining or obtaining something, such as a skill, an object, or a company.

def_en: (politics) granting favors or giving contracts or making appointments to office in return for political support
def_zh: 赞助, 恩赐态度, 光顾, 任免权, 保护人的身分
examples: The young painter flourished under the patronage of a wealthy merchant.

def_en: support by being a patron of
examples: Loyal customers continued to patronage the little shop for decades.

def_zh: 光顾, 资助, 赞助

gloss_en: the support, especially financial, that a person or organization gives to another.

def_en: the state of owing something (especially money)
money or goods or services owed by one person to another
an obligation to pay or do something
examples: The company is struggling to manage its mounting debt and avoid bankruptcy.

def_zh: 借款, 欠款, 债务

gloss_en: a sum of money that is owed or due

examples: Each purchase is recorded as a debit in the company's accounts.

gloss_en: In accounting, an entry recording money owed or paid out, typically shown on the left side of a ledger.

def_zh: 收入, 岁入, 税收, 税务局
examples: The company's annual revenue increased by 15% last year.

def_en: n the entire amount of income before any deductions are made
n government income due to taxation
def_zh: 岁入, 税收, 税务局

gloss_en: the income that a government or company receives from the taxes and other money that it collects

def_en: the advantageous quality of being beneficial
def_zh: 利润, 赢利, 利益
examples: The company reported a significant profit in the last quarter due to increased sales.

def_en: derive a benefit from
make a profit; gain money or materially
def_zh: 有益, 获利, 赚钱
examples: The small business managed to profit from the surge in demand for its products.

gloss_en: an amount of money that is gained in business or by selling something, especially more than is spent

examples: We can meet at the shopping mall near campus; there's a bookstore on the second floor.

gloss_en: A large enclosed building or complex containing many shops, restaurants, and services under one roof.

def_zh: 出口, 发泄方法, 市场
examples: The factory outlet on the highway sells last season's clothing at half the usual price.

def_en: n a place of business for retailing goods
n receptacle providing a place in a wiring system where current can be taken to run electrical devices
n an opening that permits escape or release
n activity that frees or expresses creative energy or emotion

gloss_en: a shop or place of business that sells goods, often at reduced prices; also a way of expressing or releasing something.

examples: The department store downtown carries everything from clothing to kitchenware.
collocations: the ground floor of a department store

gloss_en: A large retail store selling many kinds of goods organized into separate departments.

def_en: a large self-service grocery store selling groceries and dairy products and household goods
examples: She pushed her trolley through the supermarket, filling it with fruit and vegetables.

def_zh: 超级市场, 自助售货商店

gloss_en: a large shop where people buy food and household goods by serving themselves.

def_en: a storehouse for goods and merchandise
def_zh: 仓库, 货栈, 大商店
examples: The company rents a huge warehouse on the edge of town to store its imported furniture.

def_en: store in a warehouse
examples: The retailer warehouses its seasonal stock for months before shipping it to shops.

gloss_en: a large building where goods or materials are stored before being sold or distributed.

examples: There's a convenience store right beside the dorm that stays open all night.

gloss_en: A small shop open long hours selling everyday items such as snacks, drinks, and basic groceries.

examples: Many students now buy their textbooks from an online store to save money.

gloss_en: A retail business that sells goods to customers over the internet rather than in a physical shop.

examples: The lecture on service management focused on how firms handle customer complaints efficiently.

gloss_en: The business discipline of planning, delivering, and improving the services a company offers to customers.

examples: The intangibility of a haircut means customers cannot inspect it before they buy it.

gloss_en: The quality of a service of not being a physical object, so it cannot be seen, touched, or stored before purchase.

examples: The perishability of airline seats means an empty seat represents revenue the airline can never recover.

gloss_en: The characteristic of a service that it cannot be stored for later use; an unsold service, like an empty seat, is lost forever.

examples: Inseparability explains why a concert cannot be enjoyed unless the audience and the performers are together.
collocations: the inseparability of production and consumption

gloss_en: The feature of a service that its production and consumption happen at the same time, so provider and customer must both be present.

def_en: the quality of being subject to variation
def_zh: 易变, 变化性, 变异性
examples: Scientists studied the variability in temperature across the different seasons.

gloss_en: the quality of being likely to change or of differing from one case to another

examples: A long wait at the front desk is a common service failure that hotels try to avoid.

gloss_en: An instance in which a service falls short of what the customer expected or was promised.

examples: Effective service recovery, such as a sincere apology and a refund, can turn an angry customer into a loyal one.

gloss_en: The actions a company takes to make things right for a customer after a service failure.

examples: Good service design maps out every step a customer takes, from booking to checkout.

gloss_en: The activity of planning and arranging the parts of a service so that it works well for both customers and providers.

examples: The restaurant sets a service standard of seating every guest within five minutes.

gloss_en: A defined level of quality or performance that a service is expected to meet.

examples: Surveys show that satisfied customers are far more likely to return.

gloss_en: Feeling pleased because a need or expectation has been met.

def_en: affording pleasure; being in harmony with your taste or likings
(of persons) having pleasing manners or behavior
def_zh: 愉快的, 可爱的, 活泼的, 亲切的
examples: The staff created a pleasant atmosphere that made shoppers want to stay longer.

gloss_en: Enjoyable, agreeable, or giving a feeling of comfort and satisfaction.

examples: In the experience economy, a coffee shop sells not just coffee but a relaxing place to spend an afternoon.
collocations: the rise of the experience economy

gloss_en: An economic model in which businesses sell memorable experiences to customers rather than just goods or services.

def_en: belief about (or mental picture of) the future
the feeling that something is about to happen
def_zh: 期待, 指望, 展望
examples: The hotel exceeded my expectations by upgrading our room for free.

gloss_en: A belief about how good a product or service will be, against which the actual outcome is judged.

def_en: the quality of being loyal
feelings of allegiance
def_zh: 忠贞, 忠诚, 忠实
examples: Her loyalty to the company was rewarded with a promotion after ten years of service.

gloss_en: the quality of being faithful and giving firm support to a person, group, or cause.

examples: Before launching the product, the team ran a SWOT analysis to weigh its chances of success.

gloss_en: A planning method that evaluates an organization's internal Strengths and Weaknesses and its external Opportunities and Threats.

examples: The firm's main strengths were its skilled staff and strong brand.

gloss_en: In a SWOT analysis, the internal qualities or advantages that help an organization succeed.

examples: One of the company's weaknesses was its outdated computer system.

gloss_en: In a SWOT analysis, the internal shortcomings that put an organization at a disadvantage.

def_zh: 机会( opportunity的名词复数 ); 适当的时机; 条件; 就业（或晋升、进步等）的良机
examples: Studying abroad gave her many opportunities to improve her language skills.

def_en: n a possibility due to a favorable combination of circumstances

gloss_en: favorable situations or times that make it possible to do something.

def_zh: 威胁, 恐吓( threat的名词复数 ); 可能造成威胁的人[事, 想法]; 恶兆, 兆头
examples: Rising sea levels pose serious threats to low-lying coastal cities.

def_en: n something that is a source of danger
n a warning that something unpleasant is imminent
n declaration of an intention or a determination to inflict harm on another
n a person who inspires fear or dread

gloss_en: statements or situations that show possible danger, harm, or trouble.

def_en: a medicine that has a mitigating effect on a specific disease
examples: The report failed to provide the specifics of how the budget cuts would affect public services.

def_en: (sometimes followed by `to') applying to or characterized by or distinguishing something particular or special or unique
stated explicitly or in detail
relating to or distinguishing or constituting a taxonomic species
def_zh: 特殊的, 明确的, 具有特效的, 特定地, 具体地
examples: Could you be more specific about the problems you encountered with the software?

gloss_en: Clearly defined or identified; relating to one particular thing and not others.

examples: A measurable goal, such as increasing sales by ten percent, lets a team track its progress.

gloss_en: Able to be quantified or judged against a clear figure; a key trait of a well-set goal (the M in SMART goals).

examples: Setting an attainable target keeps employees motivated rather than discouraged.

gloss_en: Realistic and possible to achieve with the resources available; the A in SMART goals.

def_en: having a bearing on or connection with the subject at issue
def_zh: 有关联的, 有关系的, 适当的, 相应的
examples: Make sure the training is relevant to the skills your staff actually need.

gloss_en: Closely connected or appropriate to the matter at hand; in SMART goals, aligned with the larger objective.

examples: A time-bound goal specifies exactly when the target must be reached.

gloss_en: Having a fixed deadline or time frame for completion; the T in SMART goals.

examples: Despite its bulk, the hippopotamus can move surprisingly fast on land.

gloss_en: A large plant-eating African mammal that spends much of its time in rivers and lakes.

def_en: any of the larger cetacean mammals having a streamlined body and breathing through a blowhole on the head
examples: The tourists watched in awe as a blue whale surfaced near their boat.

def_en: hunt for whales
examples: For generations, the coastal villagers went out to whale each summer.

gloss_en: a very large sea mammal that breathes air through a hole on top of its head.

def_en: the remains (or an impression) of a plant or animal that existed in a past geological age and that has been excavated from the soil
examples: Palaeontologists discovered the fossil of a dinosaur that had been buried in the cliff for millions of years.

def_en: characteristic of a fossil
def_zh: 化石的, 陈腐的, 守旧的
examples: Burning fossil fuels such as coal and oil releases large amounts of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.

gloss_en: the preserved remains or impression of a plant or animal from millions of years ago, found in rock.

def_en: any object that is left unused or still extant
def_zh: 剩余物, 废墟, 残余
examples: Archaeologists discovered the remains of an ancient temple beneath the modern city.

def_zh: 遗体, 尸体, 遗骸

gloss_en: the parts of something that are left after the rest has gone, been used, or been destroyed

def_en: a terrestrial or aquatic flesh-eating mammal
any animal that feeds on flesh
examples: As a carnivore, the wolf depends almost entirely on hunting other animals for food.

def_zh: 食肉动物, 食虫植物

gloss_en: an animal that feeds on the flesh of other animals.

examples: As a herbivore, the deer spends much of the day grazing on grasses and leaves.

gloss_en: An animal that feeds mainly or entirely on plants.

examples: Bears are omnivores, feeding on berries, fish, and small mammals alike.
collocations: omnivores eat both plants and meat

gloss_en: An animal that eats both plants and other animals.

examples: The kangaroo is a marsupial that carries its newborn in a pouch for several months.

gloss_en: A mammal, such as a kangaroo or opossum, whose young are born very undeveloped and continue growing in a pouch on the mother's body.

def_en: a flat-bottomed motor vehicle that can travel on land or water
an airplane designed to take off and land on water
cold-blooded vertebrate typically living on land but breeding in water; aquatic larvae undergo metamorphosis into adult form
def_zh: 两栖动物, 水旱两生植物, 水陆两用飞机
examples: Frogs are amphibians that begin life in water as tadpoles before moving onto land.

def_zh: 两栖类的, 水陆两用的
examples: The military used an amphibian vehicle capable of driving straight from the shore into the sea.

gloss_en: a cold-blooded animal, such as a frog or newt, that can live both in water and on land.

def_en: any cold-blooded vertebrate of the class Reptilia including tortoises, turtles, snakes, lizards, alligators, crocodiles, and extinct forms
def_zh: 爬行动物, 爬虫, 卑鄙的人
examples: The zoo's new exhibit features reptiles from tropical regions around the world.

def_zh: 爬行的, 爬虫类的, 卑鄙的
examples: The museum displayed several reptile skeletons from the age of the dinosaurs.

gloss_en: a cold-blooded animal, such as a snake or lizard, that has scaly skin and lays eggs.

def_en: any warm-blooded vertebrate having the skin more or less covered with hair; young are born alive except for the small subclass of monotremes and nourished with milk
examples: The whale is the largest mammal on Earth, yet it lives entirely in the sea.

gloss_en: a warm-blooded animal that has hair or fur and feeds its young with milk.

def_en: animals having a bony or cartilaginous skeleton with a segmented spinal column and a large brain enclosed in a skull or cranium
examples: Fish were the earliest vertebrates, appearing in the fossil record long before animals moved onto land.

def_en: having a backbone or spinal column
def_zh: 有椎骨的, 有脊椎的
examples: The course examines the evolution of the vertebrate skeleton from fish to mammals.

gloss_en: an animal that has a backbone, such as a fish, bird, reptile, or mammal; having a backbone.

examples: As a cold-blooded animal, the lizard basks in the sun to warm itself before hunting.

gloss_en: An animal, such as a reptile or fish, whose body temperature changes with the surrounding environment rather than staying constant.

examples: A warm-blooded animal must eat frequently to fuel the energy it uses maintaining its body heat.

gloss_en: An animal, such as a mammal or bird, that keeps a steady internal body temperature regardless of its surroundings.

def_en: the organic processes (in a cell or organism) that are necessary for life
examples: Regular exercise can gradually raise your metabolism and help you burn more energy at rest.

gloss_en: the chemical processes in a living body that turn food into energy and keep it alive.

def_en: a body of people who settle far from home but maintain ties with their homeland; inhabitants remain nationals of their home state but are not literally under the home state's system of government
a group of organisms of the same type living or growing together
one of the 13 British colonies that formed the original states of the United States
a place where a group of people with the same interest or occupation are concentrated
examples: For over two centuries the region was a colony ruled from a capital thousands of miles away.

def_zh: 菌(集)落, 菌丛; 移民区

gloss_en: a country or area controlled by another, more powerful country and settled by its people.

def_en: a teeming multitude
def_zh: 蜂房, 闹区, 蜜蜂群
examples: The newsroom was a hive of activity as the deadline approached.

def_en: store, like bees
move together in a hive or as if in a hive
gather into a hive
def_zh: 进入蜂房, 储藏蜜于蜂房, 如蜂般群居
examples: The busy workshop seemed to hive with energy all day long.

gloss_en: a structure in which bees live; also a place full of busy activity.

def_en: the only fertile female in a colony of social insects such as bees and ants and termites; its function is to lay eggs
a female sovereign ruler
the wife or widow of a king
something personified as a woman who is considered the best or most important of her kind
examples: The entire hive depends on a single queen to lay thousands of eggs each day.

gloss_en: In a bee, ant, or termite colony, the single fertile female that lays all the eggs.

examples: Unlike worker bees, drones neither gather nectar nor defend the hive.

gloss_en: A male bee whose sole role is to mate with the queen; drones do no work in the hive.

examples: Worker bees fly out to gather nectar and return to feed the developing larvae.

gloss_en: The sterile female bees that do most of the hive's labor, such as collecting nectar, building comb, and tending the young.

def_en: the officer below the master on a commercial ship
the partner of an animal (especially a sexual partner)
one of a pair
South American holly; leaves used in making a drink like tea
def_zh: 配偶, 对手, 助手, (象棋)将死
examples: During spring, the male bird sings loudly to attract a mate.

def_zh: 使配对, 使一致, 结伴, (象棋)将死
examples: These birds usually mate in early spring and raise their young by summer.

gloss_en: one of a pair of animals that breed together; also, informally, a friend or companion.

def_en: a sweet liquid secretion that is attractive to pollinators
fruit juice especially when undiluted
def_zh: 神酒, 甜美饮料, 甘露
examples: Bees collect nectar from flowers and turn it into honey.

gloss_en: the sweet liquid produced by flowers that attracts insects and birds.

def_en: the fine spores that contain male gametes and that are borne by an anther in a flowering plant
examples: In spring, high levels of pollen in the air trigger allergies in many people.
Bees pollen the crops as they move from blossom to blossom gathering nectar.

gloss_en: the fine, usually yellow powder produced by flowers, which is carried to other flowers to allow them to produce seeds.

examples: Through the bee dance, a returning forager signals the direction and distance of a flower patch.

gloss_en: A patterned movement, such as the waggle dance, by which a honeybee tells other workers where to find food.

examples: The sea otter is a keystone species because it keeps sea-urchin numbers in check.
collocations: the loss of a keystone species

gloss_en: A species whose presence has a disproportionately large effect on its ecosystem, so that its loss dramatically changes the community.

examples: Once the otters were extirpated from the coast, the kelp forests quickly disappeared.

gloss_en: Completely wiped out from a particular area, though the species may survive elsewhere.

def_en: (of flora or fauna) in imminent danger of extinction
def_zh: 有生命危险的；濒临灭绝的
examples: The giant panda is one of the world's best-known endangered species.

gloss_en: at serious risk of disappearing completely, especially a species in danger of extinction

def_en: a person who is the aim of an attack (especially a victim of ridicule or exploitation) by some hostile person or influence
animal hunted or caught for food
examples: The owl silently swooped down on its unsuspecting prey.

def_en: profit from in an exploitatory manner
examples: Wolves typically prey on weak or injured deer.

gloss_en: an animal that is hunted and eaten by another animal.

def_en: any animal that lives by preying on other animals
examples: The lion is a powerful predator that hunts across the open grasslands.

gloss_en: an animal that hunts, kills, and eats other animals to survive.

examples: The sea otter cracks open shellfish by pounding them against a rock balanced on its chest.

gloss_en: A marine mammal of the North Pacific that feeds on sea urchins and shellfish and helps protect kelp forests.

def_en: the soft brown fur of the beaver
a full beard
a movable piece of armor on a medieval helmet used to protect the lower face
a hat made with the fur of a beaver (or similar material)
examples: The beaver used branches and mud to build a dam across the stream.

gloss_en: a large rodent with strong teeth and a broad flat tail that builds dams across rivers.

def_en: plant growing in the sea, especially marine algae
examples: Strands of green seaweed clung to the rocks exposed by the low tide.

gloss_en: a plant or alga that grows in the sea, often found washed up on beaches.

def_en: large brown seaweeds having fluted leathery fronds
def_zh: 海草, 巨藻, 海草灰
examples: Sea otters often wrap themselves in kelp to keep from drifting while they sleep.

gloss_en: a large brown seaweed that grows in cold coastal waters.

def_en: a structure that provides privacy and protection from danger
protective covering that provides protection from the weather
temporary housing for homeless or displaced persons
def_zh: 庇护所, 避难所, 庇护, 隐蔽处, 掩蔽
examples: The dense kelp gives small fish shelter from larger predators.

gloss_en: A place or structure that provides protection from danger, weather, or predators.

examples: Without otters to eat them, sea urchins multiply and strip the seabed of kelp.

gloss_en: Small spiny sea animals that graze on kelp; their numbers are controlled by predators such as sea otters.

examples: Divers once harvested abalone from the same reefs where otters feed.

gloss_en: An edible marine snail with a flattened, ear-shaped shell, often found clinging to rocks.

examples: The squid escaped the predator by releasing a cloud of dark ink.

gloss_en: A fast-swimming marine mollusk with a long soft body, eight arms, and two tentacles.

examples: Water passes over the fish's gills, allowing it to extract dissolved oxygen.

gloss_en: The organs that fish and many other water animals use to breathe by taking oxygen from water.

examples: The squid stores its dark pigment in a small ink sac.

gloss_en: A bag-like part or pouch inside a plant or animal, often holding a fluid or gas.

examples: The eye of the octopus and the eye of a mammal are a classic case of convergent evolution.

gloss_en: The process by which unrelated species independently develop similar features because they live in similar environments.

def_en: tentacles of octopus prepared as food
bottom-living cephalopod having a soft oval body with eight long tentacles
examples: An octopus can squeeze its whole body through a gap barely wider than its eye.

gloss_en: a soft-bodied sea creature with eight long arms covered in suckers.

def_en: the doctrine that representations of nature or human behavior should be accurate imitations
something copied or derived from an original
copying (or trying to copy) the actions of someone else
def_zh: 模仿, 效法, 冒充, 赝品
examples: Children learn a great deal about language through imitation of the adults around them.

def_zh: 模仿, 仿制, 仿制品

gloss_en: the act of copying the way someone or something looks, sounds, or behaves; also a copy of something.

examples: By expanding and contracting its chromatophores, the octopus blends into the reef.

gloss_en: Pigment-containing cells in the skin of animals such as squid and octopuses that allow them to change color.

examples: Old photographs often have a sepia tone, a color originally derived from cuttlefish ink.

gloss_en: The brownish-black ink produced by cuttlefish; also the reddish-brown color named after it.

def_en: a variable color averaging a deep pink
the hard stony skeleton of a Mediterranean coral that has a delicate red or pink color and is used for jewelry
unfertilized lobster roe; reddens in cooking; used as garnish or to color sauces
marine colonial polyp characterized by a calcareous skeleton; masses in a variety of shapes often forming reefs
examples: Divers were amazed by the bright colours of the coral growing along the reef.

gloss_en: a hard, often colourful substance formed from the skeletons of tiny sea animals, found in warm oceans.

def_en: the feel of a surface or a fabric
the essential quality of something
the musical pattern created by parts being played or sung together
the characteristic appearance of a surface having a tactile quality
def_zh: (织物的)密度, (材料等的)结构, 纹理
examples: The wool sweater had a rough texture that felt scratchy against her skin.

gloss_en: the way a surface or material feels when you touch it, or how it looks in terms of its structure.

examples: Some squid achieve a kind of cloaking by matching the light coming from above.

gloss_en: The act of hiding something from view, as when an animal disguises itself to avoid being seen.

examples: Transparency gives certain deep-sea creatures near-total invisibility to predators.

gloss_en: The state of not being able to be seen.

def_en: fabric dyed with splotches of green and brown and black and tan; intended to make the wearer of a garment made of this fabric hard to distinguish from the background
device or stratagem for concealment or deceit
def_zh: 伪装, 伪装物, 幌子
examples: The insect's green color provides perfect camouflage among the leaves.

def_en: disguise by camouflaging; exploit the natural surroundings to disguise something
examples: The soldiers camouflaged their vehicles with branches and netting.

gloss_en: a way of hiding something by making it blend in with its surroundings; also, to disguise in this way.

def_en: a written work (as a novel) that has been recast in a new form
the process of adapting to something (such as environmental conditions)
(physiology) the responsive adjustment of a sense organ (as the eye) to varying conditions (as of light)
def_zh: 适应, 改编, 改编本
examples: The film is a faithful adaptation of the best-selling novel by the same author.

gloss_en: the process of changing something to suit new conditions, or a version of a work changed into a new form such as a film.

def_en: the dressed skin of an animal (especially a large animal)
body covering of a living animal
def_zh: 兽皮, 迹象, 躲藏处
examples: The ancient tribes used animal hide to make clothing and shelters.

def_en: prevent from being seen or discovered
be or go into hiding; keep out of sight, as for protection and safety
def_zh: 藏, 隐瞒, 遮避, 剥...的皮, 隐藏
examples: The children tried to hide from their parents during the game of hide-and-seek.

gloss_en: To put something in a place where it cannot be seen or found.

def_en: the dressed hairy coat of a mammal
dense coat of fine silky hairs on mammals (e.g., cat or seal or weasel)
a garment made of the dressed hairy coat of a mammal
examples: The rabbit's thick fur keeps it warm through the cold winter months.

def_zh: 以毛皮制作, 使生苔, 使生水垢
examples: Over time, limescale had furred the inside of the old kettle.

gloss_en: the soft, thick hair that covers the bodies of many mammals, or this hair used to make clothing.

def_en: an outer garment that has sleeves and covers the body from shoulder down; worn outdoors
growth of hair or wool or fur covering the body of an animal
examples: In winter the fox grows a thicker coat to keep out the cold.

gloss_en: An animal's outer covering of fur, hair, or a similar protective layer.

examples: A waxy coating on the leaf helps the plant retain water.

gloss_en: A thin layer of a substance covering the surface of something.

def_en: the light horny waterproof structure forming the external covering of birds
turning an oar parallel to the water between pulls
examples: A single white feather drifted slowly down from the nest onto the garden path.

def_en: join tongue and groove, in carpentry
cover or fit with feathers
examples: The rowers feathered their oars smoothly to reduce resistance against the wind.

gloss_en: one of the light, soft structures that grow from and cover a bird's body.

examples: The researchers studied a mutant strain that could survive without light.

gloss_en: An organism carrying a genetic change that makes it differ from the normal form of its species.

examples: The bird's distraction display, dragging one wing as if broken, lures the fox away from the nest.
collocations: a distraction display draws predators away

gloss_en: A behavior in which an animal draws a predator's attention away from its young or nest, often by pretending to be injured.

examples: The bird began preening its feathers, a displacement activity triggered by the standoff.

gloss_en: An irrelevant behavior an animal performs when caught in conflict or stress, such as grooming when it can neither fight nor flee.

def_en: the production of young from an egg
shading consisting of multiple crossing lines
a movable barrier covering a hatchway
examples: The sailors climbed down through the hatch into the lower deck of the ship.

def_en: emerge from the eggs
examples: The chicks began to hatch early in the morning, one by one breaking free of their shells.

gloss_en: to break out of an egg, or to cause an egg to break open; also, a small door or opening.

def_en: the movement of persons from one country or locality to another
a group of people migrating together (especially in some given time period)
(chemistry) the nonrandom movement of an atom or radical from one place to another within a molecule
def_zh: 移民, 移往, 移动
examples: The seasonal migration of birds to warmer regions fascinates scientists every autumn.

gloss_en: the movement of people or animals from one place or region to another.

examples: Engineers use aerodynamics to design cars that use less fuel at high speeds.

def_en: n the branch of mechanics that deals with the motion of gases (especially air) and their effects on bodies in the flow

gloss_en: the branch of science that studies how air and other gases move and how they affect objects moving through them.

examples: Using echolocation, the bat can catch a flying insect in complete darkness.

gloss_en: The ability of animals such as bats and dolphins to locate objects by emitting sounds and interpreting the returning echoes.

examples: Loud noises can damage the delicate eardrum over time.

gloss_en: The thin membrane in the ear that vibrates in response to sound waves.

def_en: of or relating to the process of hearing
def_zh: 听觉的, 听觉器官的
examples: Some students learn better through auditory instruction, remembering more of what they hear than what they read.

gloss_en: relating to hearing or to the sense and organs by which we hear

def_en: (astronomy) an indistinct surface feature of Mars once thought to be a system of channels; they are now believed to be an optical illusion
long and narrow strip of water made for boats or for irrigation
def_zh: 运河, 水道, 管, 沟渠
examples: The canal linked the two rivers, allowing barges to carry coal to the coast.

def_en: provide (a city) with a canal
examples: Engineers planned to canal the marshland to drain it for farming.

gloss_en: a long, narrow, artificial waterway built for boats or for carrying water.

def_en: a medium that disseminates moving pictures
photographic material consisting of a base of celluloid covered with a photographic emulsion; used to make negatives or transparencies
a thin coating or layer
a thin sheet of (usually plastic and usually transparent) material used to wrap or cover things
def_zh: 软片, 薄膜, 胶卷, 电影
examples: The film won several awards at the festival.
The director will film the scene again tomorrow.

gloss_en: a series of moving images that tell a story

def_en: the relation between two different species of organisms that are interdependent; each gains benefits from the other
examples: The relationship between the clownfish and the sea anemone is a classic example of symbiosis.

gloss_en: a close relationship between two different kinds of living thing in which each benefits from the other.

examples: The bee and the flower are a classic example of mutualism: the bee gets nectar and the flower gets pollinated.

gloss_en: A relationship between two species in which both partners benefit.

def_en: the relation between two different kinds of organisms when one receives benefits from the other without damaging it
examples: The barnacles attached to a whale's skin are an example of commensalism, gaining transport without affecting the whale.

gloss_en: a relationship between two kinds of organisms in which one benefits while the other is neither helped nor harmed.

def_en: the relation between two different kinds of organisms in which one receives benefits from the other by causing damage to it (usually not fatal damage)
def_zh: 寄生, 寄生习惯, 寄生状态, 寄生现象
examples: The tapeworm's parasitism drains nutrients from its host without immediately killing it.

gloss_en: a relationship in which one organism lives on or in another and benefits at the host's expense.

examples: Some seabirds practice kleptoparasitism, harassing others until they drop their catch.

gloss_en: A feeding strategy in which one animal steals food that another animal has caught or gathered.

examples: The remote island is famous for its unique flora, including several species found nowhere else on Earth.

def_en: n all the plant life in a particular region or period
n (botany) a living organism lacking the power of locomotion
def_zh: 植物区系, 植物丛, 植物群, 菌丛

gloss_en: all the plants that grow in a particular region or during a particular period of time.

def_en: all the animal life in a particular region or period
examples: The island's unique fauna includes several bird species found nowhere else on Earth.

gloss_en: all the animals that live in a particular region or during a particular period.

def_en: the branch of biology that studies plants
examples: Her lifelong interest in botany began with cataloguing wildflowers in her garden.

gloss_en: the scientific study of plants and their structure, growth, and classification.

def_en: (botany) the usually underground organ that lacks buds or leaves or nodes; absorbs water and mineral salts; usually it anchors the plant to the ground
(linguistics) the form of a word after all affixes are removed
a number that, when multiplied by itself some number of times, equals a given number
the part of a tooth that is embedded in the jaw and serves as support
def_zh: 根, 根本, 根源, 基础, 底部
examples: Deep roots let the desert shrub reach water far below the surface.

gloss_en: The part of a plant, usually underground, that anchors it and absorbs water and nutrients from the soil.

def_en: the tube of a tobacco pipe
def_zh: 茎, 干, 柄, 船首, 血统, 堵塞物
examples: She cut the flower's stem at an angle before placing it in the vase.

def_en: grow out of, have roots in, originate in
cause to point inward
stop the flow of a liquid
def_zh: 摘掉茎, 装柄于, 阻止, 堵住, 逆行
examples: The government introduced new measures to stem the flow of illegal goods.

gloss_en: the long, thin part of a plant that grows upward and supports the leaves and flowers.

def_en: a slender or elongated structure that supports a plant or fungus or a plant part or plant organ
a hunt for game carried on by following it stealthily or waiting in ambush
the act of following prey stealthily
a stiff or threatening gait
def_zh: 茎, 追踪, 高视阔步
examples: She cut each rose with a long stalk so the flowers would fit neatly in the tall vase.

def_zh: 悄悄靠近, 蹑手蹑脚地走近, 蔓延, 高视阔步
examples: The lion crouched low in the grass and stalked the herd of antelope for nearly an hour.

gloss_en: the main stem of a plant, or the thin part that supports a leaf, flower, or fruit.

def_en: a division of some larger or more complex organization
a division of a stem, or secondary stem arising from the main stem of a plant
a part of a forked or branching shape
a stream or river connected to a larger one
def_zh: 树枝, 支店, 支流, 分部
examples: The tree's lower branches were heavy with fruit.

gloss_en: A woody limb that grows outward from the trunk or a larger stem of a tree or shrub.

def_en: the main stem of a tree; usually covered with bark; the bole is usually the part that is commercially useful for lumber
luggage consisting of a large strong case used when traveling or for storage
def_zh: 树干, 干线, 躯干, 主干, 象鼻, 箱子
examples: The old oak had a trunk so wide that three people could not reach around it.
The workers trunked the fallen logs down to the sawmill by the river.

def_zh: 树干的, 躯干的, 干线的, 箱形的
examples: The engineers upgraded the trunk line that carries most of the region's phone traffic.

gloss_en: the thick main stem of a tree, from which the branches grow.

examples: The bird built its nest from dry twigs and bits of grass.

def_en: branch out in a twiglike manner
examples: The main stem twigs into dozens of slender shoots near the top of the plant.

gloss_en: a small thin branch that grows from a larger branch of a tree or bush

def_en: tough protective covering of the woody stems and roots of trees and other woody plants
a noise resembling the bark of a dog
a sailing ship with 3 (or more) masts
the sound made by a dog
examples: The bark of the tree was rough and weathered.
The dog barked loudly at the stranger.

gloss_en: the outer layer of a tree's trunk, branches, and roots, or the sound a dog makes

def_en: a small hard fruit
a mature fertilized plant ovule consisting of an embryo and its food source and having a protective coat or testa
def_zh: 种子, 籽, 萌芽, 子孙, 精液
examples: Each seed contains a tiny embryo and a store of food to fuel early growth.

gloss_en: The small reproductive part of a plant from which a new plant can grow.

examples: In early spring the trees begin budding as the days grow warmer.

gloss_en: The process of a plant producing buds, the early shoots from which leaves, flowers, or stems develop.

def_en: fertilize by transfering pollen
examples: Bees pollinate the fruit trees as they move from blossom to blossom collecting nectar.

gloss_en: to transfer pollen to a flower or plant so that it can produce seeds.

examples: Ferns thrive in the shaded, damp floor of the forest.

gloss_en: A flowerless green plant that reproduces by spores and typically has feathery, divided leaves called fronds.

def_en: synthesis of compounds with the aid of radiant energy (especially in plants)
examples: During photosynthesis, plants absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen into the air.

gloss_en: the process by which green plants use sunlight to make food from carbon dioxide and water.

examples: Aquatic plants release oxygen into the pond as they photosynthesize.

gloss_en: A plant that grows in or on water rather than on dry land.

def_zh: 使凋谢, 使消亡, 使畏缩
examples: Without rain, the crops began to wither in the fields.

def_en: v wither, as with a loss of moisture
v lose freshness, vigor, or vitality

gloss_en: to become dry and weak, so that a plant shrinks and dies; also, to weaken or fade away.

def_en: a low woody perennial plant usually having several major stems
def_zh: 矮树, 灌木, 果汁甜酒
examples: They planted a row of flowering shrubs along the garden path.

gloss_en: a low woody plant that is smaller than a tree and usually has several stems.

def_en: a large wilderness area
43rd President of the United States; son of George Herbert Walker Bush (born in 1946)
United States electrical engineer who designed an early analogue computer and who led the scientific program of the United States during World War II (1890-1974)
vice president under Reagan and 41st President of the United States (born in 1924)
examples: Birds built their nest deep inside the thorny bush for protection.

gloss_en: A low woody plant with many stems branching from near the ground, smaller than a tree.

def_en: a plant lacking a permanent woody stem; many are flowering garden plants or potherbs; some having medicinal properties; some are pests
aromatic potherb used in cookery for its savory qualities
examples: The chef added fresh herbs such as basil and thyme to bring out the flavour of the sauce.

gloss_en: a plant whose leaves or seeds are used to add flavour to food or to make medicine.

def_zh: 花, 花开的状态, 兴旺期
examples: In spring, the cherry trees are covered in pink blossom.

def_en: develop or come to a promising stage
def_zh: 开花, 兴旺, 发展
examples: Their casual friendship gradually blossomed into a lasting romance.

gloss_en: a flower on a tree or plant; to produce flowers or to develop successfully.

def_en: (botany) a plant lasting for three seasons or more
examples: Unlike annual flowers, this perennial will come back every spring for years.

def_en: lasting three seasons or more
lasting an indefinitely long time; suggesting self-renewal
recurring again and again
def_zh: 常年的, 长期的, 反复的
examples: Traffic congestion is a perennial problem in most large cities.

gloss_en: lasting or happening again and again for a long time; (of a plant) living for several years

def_en: (botany) a plant that completes its entire life cycle within the space of a year
a reference book that is published regularly once every year
examples: The company publishes an annual detailing its financial performance and future plans.

def_en: completing its life cycle within a year
occurring or payable every year
def_zh: 每年的, 一年一次的, 全年的, 一年生的
examples: The annual conference attracts delegates from all over the world.

gloss_en: Happening once every year; or a plant that lives for one year.

def_en: a tropical tree or shrub bearing fruit that germinates while still on the tree and having numerous prop roots that eventually form an impenetrable mass and are important in land building
examples: The mangroves along the shore protect the coast from storm damage and erosion.

gloss_en: a tropical tree or shrub that grows in muddy coastal water and has many tangled roots above the ground

def_en: a shortage of rainfall
a prolonged shortage
examples: The severe drought destroyed most of the region's crops and left reservoirs almost empty.

gloss_en: a long period of time with little or no rain, causing a shortage of water

examples: Rising salinity in the estuary threatened the survival of the freshwater fish.

def_en: n the taste experience when common salt is taken into the mouth
n the relative proportion of salt in a solution

gloss_en: the amount of salt contained in water or soil

def_en: cause to grow or sprout
def_zh: 发芽, 萌芽, 开始发育
examples: These seeds will germinate only when the soil is both warm and moist enough.

gloss_en: (of a seed) to begin to grow and put out shoots; to cause a seed to do this.

def_en: a partially opened flower
a swelling on a plant stem consisting of overlapping immature leaves or petals
examples: In early spring, tiny green buds began to appear on the branches of the cherry tree.

def_en: develop buds
start to grow or develop
examples: The roses started to bud as soon as the weather turned warmer.

gloss_en: a small, tightly closed part of a plant that will grow into a leaf or flower.

examples: In autumn, anthocyanins give many leaves their brilliant red color.

gloss_en: A natural plant pigment that produces red, purple, and blue colors in leaves, flowers, and fruit.

examples: The pond water teemed with protozoa visible only under the microscope.

gloss_en: Single-celled microscopic organisms, more complex than bacteria, that often live in water or as parasites.

examples: A bacterium is a unicellular organism that can nonetheless survive in extreme conditions.

gloss_en: A living thing made up of only one cell that carries out all its life functions.

examples: In a multicellular organism, different cells take on distinct roles such as absorbing food or sensing light.

gloss_en: A living thing made up of many cells that are specialized for different functions.

examples: A prokaryotic cell keeps its DNA loose in the cytoplasm rather than inside a nucleus.

gloss_en: A simple cell, such as a bacterium, that lacks a nucleus enclosed by a membrane.

examples: Unlike bacteria, a eukaryotic cell stores its genetic material inside a distinct nucleus.
collocations: the nucleus of a eukaryotic cell

gloss_en: A cell that keeps its DNA inside a membrane-bound nucleus and contains other specialized structures called organelles.

examples: In a bacterium, the genetic material sits in an open region called the nucleoid.

gloss_en: The irregular region in a prokaryotic cell where the cell's DNA is concentrated, not enclosed by a membrane.

examples: The mitochondrion breaks down nutrients to release the energy the cell needs.

gloss_en: The organelle in a eukaryotic cell that produces most of the cell's energy; often called the powerhouse of the cell.

examples: In today's lecture we'll see how the chloroplast captures light energy and turns it into the sugars the cell needs to survive.

gloss_en: The organelle inside a plant or algal cell that carries out photosynthesis, using chlorophyll to convert sunlight into chemical energy.

def_en: any of a group of green pigments found in photosynthetic organisms; there are four naturally occurring forms
def_zh: 叶绿素, 含有叶绿素的绿色蜡状物质
examples: Leaves appear green because chlorophyll absorbs red and blue light while reflecting green.

gloss_en: the green pigment in plants that absorbs light and makes photosynthesis possible.

examples: Unlike animal cells, bacteria have a tough cell wall that helps them keep their shape under pressure.

gloss_en: The rigid outer layer surrounding the membrane of plant, fungal, and bacterial cells that gives the cell its shape and protection.

examples: The textbook uses the older term cytoderm, but it simply means the cell wall you already know.

gloss_en: A technical synonym for the cell wall, the rigid protective layer enclosing a plant or microbial cell.

def_en: (genetics) a segment of DNA that is involved in producing a polypeptide chain; it can include regions preceding and following the coding DNA as well as introns between the exons; it is considered a unit of heredity
examples: Researchers identified the gene that is responsible for the plant's resistance to drought.

gloss_en: a unit of material inside a cell that is passed from parent to child and controls a particular characteristic.

examples: The professor explained that each microbe's DNA carries all the information needed to reproduce.

gloss_en: Deoxyribonucleic acid, the molecule that stores the genetic instructions used to build and run a living organism.

def_en: a threadlike strand of DNA in the cell nucleus that carries the genes in a linear order
examples: Human beings normally have twenty-three pairs of chromosomes in each body cell.

gloss_en: a threadlike structure in a cell nucleus that carries genetic information in the form of genes.

examples: During mitosis the chromosomes line up and are pulled evenly into the two new cells.

gloss_en: The process by which one cell divides its nucleus into two identical daughter nuclei, using spindle fibers.

examples: Some single-celled organisms reproduce by amitosis, dividing directly without the spindle we see in mitosis.

gloss_en: A simpler form of cell division in which the nucleus splits directly without forming spindle fibers or visible chromosomes.

def_en: (microbiology) single-celled or noncellular spherical or spiral or rod-shaped organisms lacking chlorophyll that reproduce by fission; important as pathogens and for biochemical properties; taxonomy is difficult; often considered to be plants
examples: Certain bacteria in the gut actually help us digest our food.

gloss_en: very small living things, each made of a single cell, some of which cause disease and some of which are useful.

def_en: a small apparently simple structure (as a fertilized egg) from which new tissue can develop into a complete organism
def_zh: 细菌, 种子, 生殖细胞, 根源
examples: Washing your hands regularly helps stop the spread of germs.

def_zh: 芽胞, 胚, 胚芽, 病菌

gloss_en: a very small living thing that can cause disease; also, the earliest form of something from which it develops.

def_en: (virology) ultramicroscopic infectious agent that replicates itself only within cells of living hosts; many are pathogenic; a piece of nucleic acid (DNA or RNA) wrapped in a thin coat of protein
a harmful or corrupting agency
a software program capable of reproducing itself and usually capable of causing great harm to files or other programs on the same computer
def_zh: 病毒, 滤过性病毒, 毒害
examples: A virus is not fully alive on its own; it must hijack a host cell in order to multiply.

gloss_en: A tiny infectious agent that can only reproduce inside the living cells of a host organism.

examples: The lecture showed how amino acids join in a chain to form a functional protein.

gloss_en: One of the small molecules that link together to form proteins, the building blocks of living cells.

def_en: an organism of the kingdom Fungi lacking chlorophyll and feeding on organic matter; ranging from unicellular or multicellular organisms to spore-bearing syncytia
