examples: A grey fungus had begun to grow on the damp wall of the cellar.

def_zh: 真菌, 霉菌, , 蕈, 海绵肿

gloss_en: a type of organism, such as a mushroom or mold, that grows on plants, food, or other surfaces and lives on organic matter.

examples: Under the microscope you can see the fungal filaments branching out to absorb nutrients.

gloss_en: The long thread-like strands that make up the body of a fungus or a filamentous microbe.

examples: Chitin gives the fungal cell wall its strength, much as it hardens an insect's shell.

gloss_en: The tough structural material that forms fungal cell walls and the exoskeletons of insects.

def_en: generate and separate from cells or bodily fluids
place out of sight; keep secret
def_zh: 隐秘, 隐藏, 隐匿, 分泌
examples: The pancreas secretes enzymes that help the body digest food.

gloss_en: to produce and release a substance from a cell, gland, or organ; also, to hide something.

def_en: any of several complex proteins that are produced by cells and act as catalysts in specific biochemical reactions
examples: This enzyme helps the body break down food into nutrients it can absorb.

gloss_en: a protein produced by living cells that speeds up chemical reactions in the body.

examples: Doctors warn that overusing antibiotics can lead to drug-resistant bacteria.

def_en: n a chemical substance derivable from a mold or bacterium that can kill microorganisms and cure bacterial infections

gloss_en: medicines that kill or stop the growth of bacteria and are used to treat infections.

def_en: any of various antibiotics obtained from Penicillium molds (or produced synthetically) and used in the treatment of various infections and diseases
examples: The doctor prescribed penicillin to clear up the throat infection within a week.

gloss_en: an antibiotic drug, originally obtained from mould, used to treat many bacterial infections.

def_en: the acetylated derivative of salicylic acid; used as an analgesic anti-inflammatory drug (trade names Bayer, Empirin, and St. Joseph) usually taken in tablet form; used as an antipyretic; slows clotting of the blood by poisoning platelets
examples: She took two aspirin to ease the throbbing pain in her head.

def_zh: 乙酰水杨酸; 阿司匹灵; 邻乙酸基苯甲酸

gloss_en: a common drug used to relieve pain, reduce fever, and lessen inflammation.

def_en: part of an organism consisting of an aggregate of cells having a similar structure and function
a soft thin (usually translucent) paper
def_zh: 薄的织物, 薄纱, 棉纸, 组织, 一套
examples: The surgeon carefully removed the damaged tissue from around the wound.

gloss_en: a group of connected cells in an animal or plant that perform a similar function; also, a piece of soft paper used for wiping.

def_en: a fully differentiated structural and functional unit in an animal that is specialized for some particular function
a government agency or instrument devoted to the performance of some specific function
a periodical that is published by a special interest group
wind instrument whose sound is produced by means of pipes arranged in sets supplied with air from a bellows and controlled from a large complex musical keyboard
def_zh: 风琴, 器官, 元件, 机构, 机关
examples: The liver is one of the largest organs in the human body.

gloss_en: a part of the body, such as the heart or liver, that has a particular function; also a large musical keyboard instrument.

def_en: a person who is immune to a particular infection
examples: The immune system plays a crucial role in fighting off infections.

def_en: relating to the condition of immunity
secure against
relating to or conferring immunity (to disease or infection)
def_zh: 免疫的, 免除的, 不受影响的
examples: Some people are immune to certain allergies.

gloss_en: not affected by a particular disease or not influenced by something

def_en: a unit of heat equal to the amount of heat required to raise the temperature of one kilogram of water by one degree at one atmosphere pressure; used by nutritionists to characterize the energy-producing potential in food
unit of heat defined as the quantity of heat required to raise the temperature of 1 gram of water by 1 degree centigrade at atmospheric pressure
examples: To lose weight steadily, she tracked how many calories she ate each day.

gloss_en: a unit for measuring the energy that food provides to the body.

def_en: an essential structural component of living cells and source of energy for animals; includes simple sugars with small molecules as well as macromolecular substances; are classified according to the number of monosaccharide groups they contain
examples: Runners often eat foods rich in carbohydrate the night before a race.

gloss_en: a substance such as sugar or starch found in foods that gives the body energy.

def_en: transmit from one generation to the next
travel through the air
transmit
transmit or cause to broaden or spread
def_zh: 繁殖, 传播, 传送
examples: Gardeners often propagate roses by taking cuttings rather than growing them from seed.

gloss_en: to cause something such as a plant, an animal species, or an idea to spread, multiply, or be passed on from one generation to the next.

def_en: the branch of biology that deals with the structure of animals and plants
studies of the rules for forming admissible words
the admissible arrangement of sounds in words
def_zh: 形态学, 词法, 形态
examples: By comparing the morphology of the two fossils, researchers concluded they belonged to the same species.

gloss_en: the study of the form and structure of something, such as living organisms in biology or words in linguistics.

examples: Our solar system formed from a spinning cloud of gas and dust about 4.6 billion years ago.

gloss_en: The Sun together with all the planets, moons, and smaller bodies that orbit it under its gravity.

examples: The astronomer pointed to a star cluster where hundreds of young stars were born together.

gloss_en: A group of stars that are physically bound together by gravity and formed from the same cloud of gas.

examples: The Big Dipper is technically an asterism, a familiar star pattern within a larger constellation.

gloss_en: A recognizable pattern of stars in the sky that is not one of the official constellations.

def_en: a splendid assemblage (especially of famous people)
(astronomy) a collection of star systems; any of the billions of systems each having many stars and nebulae and dust
def_zh: 银河, 星系, 一群(显赫的人物)
examples: Our solar system lies in a spiral galaxy called the Milky Way.

gloss_en: a very large group of stars, gas, and dust held together by gravity.

examples: A single cluster of galaxies can contain thousands of galaxies bound by their mutual gravity.

gloss_en: A large group of galaxies held together by gravity, forming one of the biggest structures in the universe.

def_en: everything that exists anywhere
everything stated or assumed in a given discussion
def_zh: 宇宙, 星系, (思想等)范围
examples: Scientists continue to explore the vastness and mysteries of the universe.

gloss_en: all the stars, planets, and space that exist

def_en: the unlimited expanse in which everything is located
an empty area (usually bounded in some way between things)
an area reserved for some particular purpose
a blank character used to separate successive words in writing or printing
def_zh: 位置, 空间, 距离, 太空, 空白, 间隔, (期刊等的)篇幅
examples: The astronauts traveled through space to reach the moon.
The company spaced out the meetings to avoid conflicts.

gloss_en: a large, empty area, either outside the Earth's atmosphere or inside a building or room

def_en: the absence of matter
a region that is devoid of matter
an electrical home appliance that cleans by suction
def_zh: 真空, 空间, 真空吸尘器
examples: Sound cannot travel through the vacuum of outer space.

def_en: clean with a vacuum cleaner
examples: He vacuumed the carpet thoroughly before the guests arrived.

def_zh: 真空的, 产生真空的, 利用真空的
examples: The coffee is stored in a vacuum container to keep it fresh.

gloss_en: a space that is completely empty of all matter, including air.

examples: The lecture invited us to think of the cosmos as a vast, orderly system governed by physical laws.

gloss_en: The universe considered as a whole, viewed as an ordered and harmonious system.

def_en: a configuration of stars as seen from the earth
examples: On clear nights, the ancient sailors used the constellation of the Great Bear to find their way.

gloss_en: a group of stars that form a recognizable pattern in the night sky.

examples: On a clear night you can use the Big Dipper to find the North Star.
collocations: the Big Dipper points to Polaris

gloss_en: A well-known group of seven bright stars whose shape resembles a ladle or dipper.

def_en: a medicinal liquid preparation intended for use in an atomizer
cloudiness of the urine
an immense cloud of gas (mainly hydrogen) and dust in interstellar space
examples: The telescope captured a stunning image of a distant nebula glowing in red and blue.

def_zh: 薄翳; 角膜翳, 角膜云翳; 喷雾剂

gloss_en: a huge cloud of gas and dust in space, often seen as a bright or dark patch among the stars.

examples: During the eclipse we could finally see the Sun's corona glowing around the darkened disk.

gloss_en: The outermost layer of the Sun's atmosphere, a glowing halo visible during a total solar eclipse.

def_en: a gaseous layer of the sun's atmosphere (extending from the photosphere to the corona) that is visible during a total eclipse of the sun
examples: During the eclipse, astronomers observed the thin, glowing chromosphere surrounding the darkened disc of the sun.

gloss_en: a reddish layer of the sun's atmosphere lying just above the visible surface, seen especially during a total eclipse.

def_en: the intensely luminous surface of a star (especially the sun)
examples: Sunspots appear as dark patches on the Sun's photosphere where the temperature is lower.

gloss_en: the bright visible surface layer of a star, especially the Sun, from which light is given off.

def_en: one celestial body obscures another
def_zh: 日食, 月食, (地位名誉等的)消失
examples: Thousands of people gathered in the park to watch the total eclipse of the sun.

def_en: cause an eclipse of (a celestial body) by intervention
def_zh: 引起日食, 引起月食, 使黯然失色
examples: Her early success was soon eclipsed by that of her younger and more talented sister.

gloss_en: an occasion when the sun or moon cannot be seen because one is blocked by the other.

examples: Solar radiation drives Earth's climate by warming the surface and the atmosphere.

gloss_en: The energy given off by the Sun in the form of light and other electromagnetic waves.

def_en: exchange on a regular basis
perform a job or duty on a rotating basis
cause to turn on an axis or center
def_zh: 使旋转, 使转动, 使轮流
examples: The Earth rotates once every twenty-four hours, giving us day and night.
The botanist labelled the plant's rotate flowers, whose petals spread flat like a wheel.

gloss_en: to turn around a central point or axis; also to take turns doing something in a regular order.

examples: When a massive star runs out of fuel, it can end its life in a spectacular supernova.

gloss_en: The powerful explosion of a dying star that briefly shines as brightly as an entire galaxy.

examples: A neutron star packs more mass than the Sun into a sphere only a few kilometers across.

gloss_en: The extremely dense collapsed core left behind after certain stars explode as supernovae.

examples: Our Sun will eventually shrink into a white dwarf and slowly cool over billions of years.
collocations: the star becomes a white dwarf

gloss_en: The small, dense, hot remnant left when a low-mass star like the Sun exhausts its fuel.

def_en: movement downward resulting from gravitational attraction
a figurative movement toward some attraction
def_zh: 引力, 重力, 沉下
examples: Newton's law of gravitation describes how any two masses attract each other.

gloss_en: the force that attracts objects toward one another, especially the pull of a planet on nearby bodies

examples: At the heart of a black hole lies a singularity where gravity becomes infinitely strong.

gloss_en: A point of infinite density at the center of a black hole where the known laws of physics break down.

def_en: an abrupt failure of function or complete physical exhaustion
a natural event caused by something suddenly falling down or caving in
def_zh: 崩溃, 倒塌, 虚脱
examples: The collapse of the bridge left thousands of commuters without a route to work.

def_en: break down, literally or metaphorically
fold or close up
def_zh: 倒塌, 崩溃, 瓦解
examples: The old warehouse roof collapsed under the weight of the heavy snow.

gloss_en: to fall down or inward suddenly; the sudden failure or breakdown of a structure, system, or person.

examples: An emission nebula glows red because its hydrogen gas is being excited by hot young stars.

gloss_en: A cloud of glowing gas in space that gives off its own light after being energized by nearby stars.

examples: A reflection nebula appears blue because its dust scatters the light of the stars beside it.

gloss_en: A cloud of dust in space that shines by reflecting the light of nearby stars rather than emitting its own.

examples: A dark nebula shows up as a black gap in the sky where its thick dust hides the stars beyond.

gloss_en: A dense cloud of gas and dust in space that blocks the light of stars behind it, appearing as a dark patch.

examples: Ancient astronomers called them fixed stars because they seemed not to move against each other.

gloss_en: A star that appears to stay in the same position relative to others, unlike the wandering planets.

def_en: (astronomy) any of the nine large celestial bodies in the solar system that revolve around the sun and shine by reflected light; Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and Pluto in order of their proximity to the sun; viewed from the constellation Hercules, all the planets rotate around the sun in a counterclockwise direction
any celestial body (other than comets or satellites) that revolves around a star
def_zh: 行星, 命运星辰, 杰出的人, 重大影响的事
examples: Scientists are constantly searching for new planets that could potentially support life.

gloss_en: A large, spherical object in space that moves around a star, such as the Earth.

def_en: a person who is markedly small
a plant or animal that is atypically small
examples: In the fairy tale, a friendly dwarf shows the travellers the way through the forest.

def_en: check the growth of
examples: The new skyscraper dwarfs every other building in the old city centre.

gloss_en: a person, animal, or plant that is much smaller than the usual size.

def_en: any of numerous small celestial bodies composed of rock and metal that move around the sun (mainly between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter)
examples: Scientists tracked the asteroid as it passed harmlessly near the Earth.

def_en: shaped like a star
examples: The mission was designed to collect samples of asteroid dust for study.

gloss_en: one of the many small rocky objects that orbit the sun, mostly between Mars and Jupiter.

examples: Ceres was first classified as a minor planet before being called a dwarf planet.
collocations: minor planet in the asteroid belt

gloss_en: A small rocky body that orbits the Sun but is much smaller than a true planet, such as an asteroid.

def_en: a heavy silvery toxic univalent and bivalent metallic element; the only metal that is liquid at ordinary temperatures
(Roman mythology) messenger of Jupiter and god of commerce; counterpart of Greek Hermes
the smallest planet and the nearest to the sun
temperature measured by a mercury thermometer
examples: Old thermometers contained mercury, which rose up the tube as the temperature increased.

gloss_en: a heavy, silvery metal that is liquid at ordinary temperatures and is poisonous, used in thermometers.

examples: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system because its dense atmosphere traps heat.

gloss_en: The second planet from the Sun, known for its thick atmosphere and extremely high surface temperature.

examples: Earth's distance from the Sun allows liquid water to exist on its surface.

gloss_en: The third planet from the Sun and the only one known to support life.

examples: Scientists study Mars for signs that liquid water once flowed across its surface.

gloss_en: The fourth planet from the Sun, a cold, dusty world often called the Red Planet.

examples: Ceres, the biggest body in the asteroid belt, is now considered a dwarf planet.

gloss_en: The largest object in the asteroid belt, classified as a dwarf planet.

examples: Between Mars and Jupiter lies the asteroid belt, home to millions of rocky bodies.

gloss_en: The region between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter where most of the solar system's asteroids orbit the Sun.

examples: Jupiter is so massive that all the other planets combined would fit inside it several times over.
collocations: the Great Red Spot on Jupiter

gloss_en: The fifth and largest planet in the solar system, a giant made mostly of gas.

examples: Saturn's spectacular rings are made of countless pieces of ice and rock.

gloss_en: The sixth planet from the Sun, a gas giant famous for its bright system of rings.

examples: Uranus is unusual because it spins on its side, unlike any other planet.

gloss_en: The seventh planet from the Sun, an ice giant that rotates tilted almost on its side.

examples: Neptune, the most distant planet, has the fastest winds recorded in the solar system.

gloss_en: The eighth and farthest major planet from the Sun, a cold, windy ice giant.

examples: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 after new objects like it were found.

gloss_en: A dwarf planet in the outer solar system, once counted as the ninth planet.

def_en: (astronomy) a relatively small extraterrestrial body consisting of a frozen mass that travels around the sun in a highly elliptical orbit
examples: Astronomers observed the comet as it passed close to the sun and grew a long tail.

gloss_en: a bright object in space made of ice and dust that travels around the sun and often has a glowing tail.

def_en: (astronomy) any of the small solid extraterrestrial bodies that hits the earth's atmosphere
examples: A meteoroid becomes a meteor when it burns up as it enters the earth's atmosphere.

gloss_en: a small rocky or metallic body traveling through space, smaller than an asteroid.

def_en: a streak of light in the sky at night that results when a meteoroid hits the earth's atmosphere and air friction causes the meteoroid to melt or vaporize or explode
examples: We lay on the grass at midnight and watched a bright meteor streak across the summer sky.

gloss_en: a piece of rock or metal from space that burns brightly as it enters the earth's atmosphere; a shooting star.

examples: What we call a shooting star is really a tiny meteor burning up as it enters the atmosphere.
collocations: a shooting star streaks across the sky
make a wish on a shooting star

gloss_en: A brief streak of light in the night sky made by a small space rock burning up in the atmosphere.

def_en: stony or metallic object that is the remains of a meteoroid that has reached the earth's surface
examples: Scientists believe a giant meteorite impact may have contributed to the extinction of the dinosaurs.

gloss_en: a piece of rock or metal from space that has fallen to the earth's surface.

def_en: half of the terrestrial globe
half of a sphere
either half of the cerebrum
def_zh: 半球, 地球的半面, 大脑半球
examples: When it is summer in the northern hemisphere, it is winter in the southern hemisphere.
collocations: the left hemisphere of the brain

gloss_en: one half of the Earth, especially as divided by the equator into north and south; also either half of the brain.

examples: At the North Pole the Sun stays above the horizon for months during summer.

gloss_en: Either of the two points, north or south, where the Earth's axis of rotation meets its surface.

examples: North of the polar circle the Sun never sets during part of the summer.

gloss_en: Either of the two lines of latitude, the Arctic or Antarctic Circle, marking the regions of midnight sun.

examples: The Arctic is warming faster than almost anywhere else on the planet.

gloss_en: The cold northernmost region of the Earth surrounding the North Pole.

examples: The Antarctic holds most of the world's fresh water locked up in its ice sheet.

gloss_en: The cold southernmost region of the Earth surrounding the South Pole, including the continent of Antarctica.

def_en: arid land with little or no vegetation
def_zh: 沙漠, 应得的赏罚, 功劳
examples: The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert in the world.

def_en: leave behind
def_zh: 放弃, 遗弃, 擅离
examples: The soldiers were ordered not to desert their posts under any circumstances.
The desert landscape was harsh and unforgiving.

gloss_en: a large area of land that has very little water and very few plants

examples: The lecture traced how a mountain range rises where two tectonic plates collide.

gloss_en: A connected series of mountains that form a continuous line or chain.

def_en: a meeting of heads of governments
def_zh: 顶点, 最高阶层, 最高级会议
examples: After two days of climbing, the exhausted team finally reached the summit at dawn.

def_en: reach the summit (of a mountain)
examples: The climbers hoped to summit the peak before the afternoon storms rolled in.

def_zh: 政府首脑的, 最高级的
examples: World leaders gathered for summit talks aimed at reducing carbon emissions.

gloss_en: the highest point of something, especially a mountain; also an important meeting between leaders of governments.

def_en: the top or extreme point of something (usually a mountain or hill)
def_zh: 山峰, 巅, 山顶, 顶点, 尖峰, 帽舌
examples: The climbers reached the peak of Mount Everest after a challenging ascent.

def_zh: 到达最高点, 消瘦, 变憔悴, 逐渐缩小
使竖起, 使达到最高点
examples: Sales are expected to peak in the third quarter.
The company experienced peak demand for its products during the holiday season.

gloss_en: the pointed top of a mountain

def_en: a long narrow natural elevation or striation
any long raised strip
a long narrow natural elevation on the floor of the ocean
a long narrow range of hills
examples: The hikers followed the mountain ridge until they reached the highest peak.

gloss_en: a long, narrow raised part of a surface, especially the top of a line of hills or mountains.

def_zh: 高地, 高原, (上升后的)稳定水平
examples: After months of rapid improvement, her test scores reached a plateau and stopped rising.

def_en: n a relatively flat highland

gloss_en: a large area of flat, high ground; also a period when progress or activity stops increasing and stays at one level

def_en: extensive tract of level open land
def_zh: 平原, 草原, 朴实无华的东西, 无格式
examples: The vast plain stretched for miles, ideal for farming because of its flat, fertile soil.

gloss_en: A large area of flat, level land with few trees, as used in physical geography.

def_en: elevated (e.g., mountainous) land
examples: The remote highlands are home to hardy sheep and a scattering of small farms.

gloss_en: an area of high or mountainous land.

def_en: all the plant life in a particular region or period
the process of growth in plants
an abnormal growth or excrescence (especially a warty excrescence on the valves of the heart)
inactivity that is passive and monotonous, comparable to the inactivity of plant life
examples: Dense tropical vegetation covered the hillsides on both banks of the river.

def_zh: 贽生物, 贽疣, 增殖体; 生长, 增殖

gloss_en: the plants and trees that grow in a particular area or region.

examples: The tropical rainforest is home to more species than any other land ecosystem.

gloss_en: A dense, tall forest in a region of very high rainfall, especially in the tropics.

def_en: low land that is seasonally flooded; has more woody plants than a marsh and better drainage than a bog
a situation fraught with difficulties and imponderables
def_zh: 沼泽, 进退两难的困境
examples: The explorers waded through the swamp, careful to avoid the crocodiles.

def_en: drench or submerge or be drenched or submerged
def_zh: 淹没, 击溃, 清除, 使陷于沼泽
examples: After the advertisement aired, the office was swamped with phone calls.

gloss_en: an area of low, wet ground, often with trees and thick plants; to flood or overwhelm.

def_en: a body of water cut off from a larger body by a reef of sand or coral
def_zh: 环礁湖, 近海岸的浅水区, 泻湖, 咸水湖
examples: The resort was built beside a calm turquoise lagoon protected by a coral reef.

gloss_en: a shallow area of seawater separated from the open sea by a reef, sandbank, or strip of land.

def_en: a bowl-shaped vessel; usually used for holding food or liquids
the quantity that a basin will hold
a natural depression in the surface of the land often with a lake at the bottom of it
examples: She filled the basin with warm water and left the dishes to soak overnight.

gloss_en: a round open container used for holding water or food, or for washing

def_en: a long depression in the surface of the land that usually contains a river
def_zh: 山谷, 溪谷, 流域, 凹地
examples: A river valley forms as flowing water slowly carves a channel through the land.

gloss_en: A low area of land between hills or mountains, often with a river running through it.

def_en: a deep ravine (usually with a river running through it)
def_zh: 峡谷, 凹槽, 塞饱, 咽喉
examples: The river had carved a spectacular gorge through the limestone over millions of years.

def_en: overeat or eat immodestly; make a pig of oneself
def_zh: 塞饱, 狼吞虎咽地吃
examples: The children gorged themselves on cake until they could barely move.

gloss_en: a deep, narrow valley with steep sides, usually with a river running through it; to eat greedily.

def_en: a ravine formed by a river in an area with little rainfall
examples: Standing at the edge of the canyon, we could barely see the river far below.

gloss_en: a deep valley with steep sides, usually carved by a river.

def_en: a mountain formed by volcanic material
examples: When the volcano erupted, thick ash covered the surrounding villages for miles.

gloss_en: a mountain with an opening through which hot lava, ash, and gas can burst out.

def_en: (geology) a crack in the earth's crust resulting from the displacement of one side with respect to the other
(electronics) equipment failure attributable to some defect in a circuit (loose connection or insulation failure or short circuit etc.)
responsibility for a bad situation or event
(sports) a serve that is illegal (e.g., that lands outside the prescribed area)
def_zh: 过错, 故障, 毛病
examples: Earthquakes often occur along a fault, where two blocks of the crust grind past each other.

gloss_en: A crack or fracture in the Earth's crust along which blocks of rock have moved.

examples: Most volcanoes and earthquakes are concentrated along a plate boundary.

gloss_en: The edge where two of the Earth's tectonic plates meet and interact.

examples: Hot Spot Theory explains why a chain of volcanic islands like Hawaii forms far from any plate boundary.

gloss_en: The idea that some volcanoes form over fixed plumes of hot material rising deep from within the mantle.

examples: An active volcano is closely monitored because it may erupt at any time.

gloss_en: A volcano that is currently erupting or is likely to erupt in the future.

examples: A dormant volcano may look quiet for centuries before suddenly waking up.

gloss_en: A volcano that is not erupting now but could become active again in the future.

examples: An extinct volcano has lost its source of magma and is not expected to erupt again.

gloss_en: A volcano that is not expected to erupt again because its supply of magma has ended.

def_en: rock that in its molten form (as magma) issues from volcanos; lava is what magma is called when it reaches the surface
examples: Rivers of molten lava poured down the sides of the erupting volcano.

def_zh: 火山石(一种顺势疗法药)

gloss_en: hot melted rock that flows out of a volcano and hardens as it cools.

def_en: molten rock in the earth's crust
def_zh: 岩浆, 糊剂, 乳浆剂
examples: As pressure builds, magma rises through cracks in the crust and may erupt as lava.

gloss_en: hot molten or semi-molten rock beneath the earth's surface

examples: During an El Niño year, unusual ocean warming can bring floods to some regions and drought to others.

gloss_en: A recurring warming of surface waters in the eastern Pacific Ocean that disrupts weather worldwide.

examples: La Niña, the cooling phase of the cycle, often brings wetter weather to parts of the Pacific.

gloss_en: A recurring cooling of surface waters in the eastern Pacific Ocean, the counterpart to El Niño.

examples: The sudden rainstorm dropped several centimeters of rain in less than an hour.

gloss_en: A storm with heavy rain, often accompanied by strong wind.

def_en: the rising of a body of water and its overflowing onto normally dry land
an overwhelming number or amount
light that is a source of artificial illumination having a broad beam; used in photography
a large flow
def_zh: 洪水, 大量之水, 涨潮
examples: The heavy rains caused a flood that forced hundreds of families to leave their homes.

def_zh: 淹没, 使泛滥, 注满
被淹, 溢出, 涌进
examples: The river burst its banks and flooded the surrounding farmland overnight.

gloss_en: a large amount of water that covers land which is usually dry; also to cover an area with water.

examples: After the heavy rain, a debris flow of mud and rock swept down the mountainside.

gloss_en: A fast-moving mixture of water, mud, rock, and other material that rushes down a slope.

def_en: a slowly moving mass of ice
examples: The hikers had to navigate through the glacier to reach the mountain peak.

gloss_en: a large, slow-moving river of ice and snow

examples: Over the years, snow packs into neve, or firn, the grainy stage before it hardens into glacial ice.

gloss_en: Granular, partly compacted snow that has survived a summer and is on its way to becoming glacial ice.

examples: Under its own weight, buried snow slowly turns into dense glacial ice.

gloss_en: The dense, solid ice that forms when layers of snow are compressed over many years within a glacier.

examples: As glaciers slowly advance, they carve valleys and grind rock into fine powder.

gloss_en: Large masses of ice that form on land and move slowly downhill under their own weight.

def_en: a colorless gas (O3) soluble in alkalis and cold water; a strong oxidizing agent; can be produced by electric discharge in oxygen or by the action of ultraviolet radiation on oxygen in the stratosphere (where it acts as a screen for ultraviolet radiation)
def_zh: 新鲜的空气, 臭氧, 使人愉快的影响
examples: Scientists warned that certain chemicals were destroying the ozone in the stratosphere.

gloss_en: a form of oxygen (O3) present in the upper atmosphere, where it shields the earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation.

examples: The ozone layer shields life on Earth by absorbing dangerous ultraviolet rays from the Sun.
collocations: a hole in the ozone layer

gloss_en: The region of the upper atmosphere rich in ozone gas that absorbs most of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet radiation.

examples: Too much ultraviolet radiation can harm skin and eyes, which is why the ozone layer matters.

gloss_en: Invisible, high-energy light from the Sun that can damage living tissue and is largely absorbed by the ozone layer.

examples: Wetlands act like natural sponges, storing floodwater and filtering pollutants from it.

gloss_en: Areas of land, such as marshes or swamps, that are saturated with water and support distinctive plant and animal life.

examples: Plate tectonics explains why earthquakes, mountains, and volcanoes tend to occur where plates meet.

gloss_en: The theory that the Earth's outer shell is broken into large plates that slowly move and interact.

examples: Continental drift was the first proposal that today's continents were once a single landmass.

gloss_en: The early idea that the continents were once joined and have slowly moved apart over geological time.

def_en: the outer layer of the Earth
a hard outer layer that covers something
the trait of being rude and impertinent; inclined to take liberties
def_zh: 外壳, 坚硬外皮, 面包皮
examples: The pie had a golden, crispy crust that broke apart at the first bite.

def_en: form a crust or form into a crust
examples: After the storm, a thin layer of ice had crusted over the surface of the pond.

gloss_en: the hard outer layer of something such as bread, pastry, or the Earth.

def_en: the cloak as a symbol of authority
United States baseball player (1931-1997)
the layer of the earth between the crust and the core
(zoology) a protective layer of epidermis in mollusks or brachiopods that secretes a substance forming the shell
def_zh: 斗篷, 罩子, 披风
examples: After her father retired, she took on the mantle of leadership at the family firm.
A thick layer of snow mantled the rooftops and silent streets of the town.

gloss_en: an important role or responsibility that passes from one person to another; also a loose cloak worn over other clothes.

def_en: the solid part of the earth consisting of the crust and outer mantle
examples: Tectonic plates are large sections of the earth's lithosphere that slowly move over time.

gloss_en: the solid outer layer of the earth, made up of the crust and the upper part of the mantle

examples: The Earth's core is so hot that its outer part is molten metal.

gloss_en: The innermost part of the Earth, a very hot, dense center made mostly of iron and nickel.

def_en: the line or plane indicating the limit or extent of something
a line determining the limits of an area
examples: The river forms a natural boundary between the two neighboring countries.

gloss_en: a real or imagined line that marks the edge or limit of an area or of something abstract.

examples: At a mid-ocean ridge, magma rises to create fresh sea floor as the plates spread.

gloss_en: A long underwater mountain chain where new ocean floor is created as plates pull apart.

examples: At a subduction zone, one plate dives beneath another, often triggering powerful earthquakes.
collocations: plate sinks at the subduction zone

gloss_en: A region where one tectonic plate slides beneath another and sinks into the mantle.

def_en: a sheet of metal or wood or glass or plastic
a full-page illustration (usually on slick paper)
dish on which food is served or from which food is eaten
the quantity contained in a plate
def_zh: 碟, 盘子, 盆中物, 金属板, 图版, 金银餐具, 印版, 金属牌(照)
examples: Please put your dirty plates in the dishwasher after dinner.

def_zh: 镀金, 电镀, 用金属板固定, 给...装钢板, 为...制印版
examples: The chef carefully plated the gourmet meal, ensuring each element was perfectly arranged.

gloss_en: A flat, typically round, dish from which food is eaten or served, or a flat piece of metal or other hard material.

def_en: be adjacent or come together
approach a limit as the number of terms increases without limit
move or draw together at a certain location
come together so as to form a single product
examples: Thousands of protesters converged on the main square from every direction.

gloss_en: to move towards and meet at the same point.

examples: Where two plates split apart, new crust forms in the gap between them.

gloss_en: To separate or move away from each other, as when two tectonic plates pull in opposite directions.

examples: At a subduction zone, the denser plate dives under the lighter one.

gloss_en: To move downward beneath something, as when one tectonic plate sinks below another.

examples: Where two plates slide past each other, the friction can build up and release as an earthquake.

gloss_en: To move sideways alongside each other, as when two tectonic plates grind horizontally in opposite directions.

def_en: predicting what the weather will be
the earth science dealing with phenomena of the atmosphere (especially weather)
examples: Advances in meteorology have made it possible to predict severe storms several days in advance.

gloss_en: the scientific study of the atmosphere, weather conditions, and how the weather can be forecast.

def_en: wetness caused by water
def_zh: 湿气, 水分, 降雨
examples: The plastic wrapping traps moisture and keeps the bread from drying out.

gloss_en: tiny drops of water present in the air, on a surface, or in a substance.

def_zh: 潮湿的, 多雨的, 感伤的, 含泪的
examples: The rich, moist soil in the garden was perfect for growing vegetables.

def_en: s slightly wet

gloss_en: slightly wet, often in a pleasant way.

def_en: lacking sufficient water or rainfall
lacking vitality or spirit; lifeless
examples: Only a few hardy plants can survive in the arid soil of the desert.

gloss_en: having very little rain or water, so that few plants can grow; extremely dry.

def_en: a severe tropical cyclone usually with heavy rains and winds moving a 73-136 knots (12 on the Beaufort scale)
def_zh: 飓风, 暴风, 暴风雨
examples: The hurricane tore roofs from houses and left thousands of families without power.

gloss_en: a violent tropical storm with very strong winds and heavy rain.

def_en: a tropical cyclone occurring in the western Pacific or Indian oceans
examples: The approaching typhoon forced authorities to evacuate thousands of people from the coastal towns.

gloss_en: a violent tropical storm with strong winds, occurring in the western Pacific or Indian Ocean.

def_en: a localized and violently destructive windstorm occurring over land characterized by a funnel-shaped cloud extending toward the ground
def_zh: 旋风, 龙卷风, 大雷雨
examples: The tornado tore the roofs off several houses before it moved back out over the fields.

gloss_en: a violent and destructive storm in which a spinning column of air shaped like a funnel moves across the land.

def_en: a slight wind (usually refreshing)
def_zh: 微风, 煤屑, 轻而易举的事
examples: A cool breeze drifted in from the sea, making the hot afternoon more bearable.

def_en: blow gently and lightly
to proceed quickly and easily
examples: She breezed into the meeting late, completely unconcerned by the waiting colleagues.

gloss_en: a light, gentle wind.

def_en: a flow of electricity through a conductor
a steady flow of a fluid (usually from natural causes)
examples: The strong ocean current made swimming difficult.

def_en: occurring in or belonging to the present time
def_zh: 流通的, 现在的, 当前的, 流行的
examples: What is your current address and phone number?

gloss_en: Happening or existing now; belonging to the present time.

def_en: the metal or paper medium of exchange that is presently used
general acceptance or use
def_zh: 货币, 通货, 流通, 通用
examples: Travelers should exchange their money for the local currency before arriving.

gloss_en: the system of money that is used in a particular country.

def_en: a storm with widespread snowfall accompanied by strong winds
examples: The blizzard dumped half a metre of snow overnight and closed every road in the city.

gloss_en: a severe snowstorm with heavy snow and strong winds.

examples: The sailors' ship was nearly lost in the sudden tempest that blew up overnight.

gloss_en: A violent storm with strong winds and heavy rain.

def_en: very light rain; stronger than mist but less than a shower
examples: A fine drizzle fell all morning, leaving the streets damp and grey.

def_en: rain lightly
moisten with fine drops
examples: She drizzled a little olive oil over the fresh salad before serving it.

gloss_en: light rain that falls in very fine drops; as a verb, to rain lightly or to pour a thin stream of liquid over food.

def_en: ice crystals forming a white deposit (especially on objects outside)
the formation of frost or ice on a surface
United States poet famous for his lyrical poems on country life in New England (1874-1963)
examples: A heavy frost covered the grass and made the whole garden sparkle in the morning light.

def_en: decorate with frosting
def_zh: 覆着霜, 冻结, 结霜
examples: Cold air had frosted the windows overnight, so she had to scrape them before driving.

gloss_en: a thin white layer of ice that forms on surfaces outdoors when the temperature falls below freezing.

def_en: a visible suspension in the air of particles of some substance
examples: Water vapor rose from the hot spring and drifted into the cold morning air.
As the sun rose, the dew began to vapor off the warm grass.

gloss_en: a mass of very small drops of liquid in the air, such as steam or mist.

def_en: devoid of warmth and cordiality; expressive of unfriendliness or disdain
def_zh: 寒冷的, 严寒的, 冷淡的
examples: The climbers faced frigid temperatures near the summit of the mountain.

def_zh: 寒冷的, 冷淡的, 性感缺失的

gloss_en: extremely cold; also, unfriendly and lacking warmth.

def_en: coldness due to a cold environment
a sensation of cold that often marks the start of an infection and the development of a fever
a sudden numbing dread
def_zh: 寒冷, 寒意, 失意
examples: There was a distinct chill in the air as autumn gave way to winter.

def_en: depress or discourage
examples: Remember to chill the white wine for a couple of hours before serving.
A chill wind swept across the empty moor as night fell.

gloss_en: an unpleasant feeling of coldness

def_en: a seasonal wind in southern Asia; blows from the southwest (bringing rain) in summer and from the northeast in winter
rainy season in southern Asia when the southwestern monsoon blows, bringing heavy rains
any wind that changes direction with the seasons
def_zh: 季风, 吹季风的季节, 雨季
examples: Farmers depend on the summer monsoon to bring the rain their rice crops need.

gloss_en: a seasonal wind of southern Asia, or the heavy rainy season it brings in summer.

def_en: a crystal of snow
def_zh: 雪片, 雪花, 雪片莲
examples: Under the microscope, each snowflake revealed a delicate six-sided pattern unlike any other.

gloss_en: a single small piece of snow, made of ice crystals, that falls from the sky.

def_en: undergo condensation; change from a gaseous to a liquid state and fall in drops
remove water from
cause a gas or vapor to change into a liquid
become more compact or concentrated
def_zh: 使浓缩, 使压缩, 缩短
examples: Warm water vapor condenses into droplets when it touches the cold glass.

gloss_en: to make something shorter or more concentrated, or to change from a gas into a liquid

def_en: (psychoanalysis) an unconscious process whereby two ideas or images combine into a single symbol; especially in dreams
the process of changing from a gaseous to a liquid or solid state
atmospheric moisture that has condensed because of cold
def_zh: 压缩, 凝缩, 冷凝
examples: In winter, condensation forms on the inside of the windows and runs down the glass.

def_zh: 冷凝; 冷凝作用; 缩合; 缩合作用; 凝聚; 凝聚作用; 凝结作用

gloss_en: the small drops of water that form when warm air or vapor cools and turns back into liquid on a cold surface.

def_en: a six-sided polygon
examples: The cells of a honeycomb form a perfect hexagon, which makes efficient use of space.

def_zh: 六角, 六角形, 六角边

gloss_en: a flat shape with six straight sides and six angles

examples: Snowflakes grow in a hexagonal pattern because of the way water molecules bond as they freeze.

gloss_en: Having the shape of a hexagon, a figure with six straight sides, as seen in snowflake crystals.

examples: The professor sketched a pentagon on the board to compare its five sides with the six of a hexagon.

gloss_en: A flat shape with five straight sides and five angles.

examples: Some crystals form pentagonal faces, though five-sided shapes are less common in nature.

gloss_en: Having the shape of a pentagon, a figure with five straight sides.

def_en: become ground down or deteriorate
remove soil or rock
examples: Years of heavy rain had eroded the hillside, exposing the roots of the old trees.

gloss_en: to gradually wear away, or to slowly weaken or destroy something over time.

def_en: (geology) the mechanical process of wearing or grinding something down (as by particles washing over it)
condition in which the earth's surface is worn away by the action of water and wind
a gradual decline of something
def_zh: 腐蚀, 冲蚀, 侵蚀
examples: Heavy rainfall accelerated the erosion of the unprotected hillside.

gloss_en: the gradual wearing away of soil, rock, or land by wind, water, or other natural forces.

def_zh: 腐烂, 腐蚀, 败坏
examples: The builders discovered serious rot in the wooden beams supporting the old roof.

def_zh: 腐烂, 烂, 堕落, 憔悴
使腐烂, 使腐朽, 使堕落
examples: If you leave the fruit in a warm room, it will soon begin to rot.

def_en: n a state of decay usually accompanied by an offensive odor
n (biology) the process of decay caused by bacterial or fungal action
n unacceptable behavior (especially ludicrously false statements)
v break down
v become physically weaker

gloss_en: the process of decaying, or the state of being decayed, usually with an unpleasant smell; as a verb, to decay or make something decay.

def_en: the process of gradually becoming inferior
a gradual decrease; as of stored charge or current
the organic phenomenon of rotting
an inferior state resulting from the process of decaying
examples: Regular brushing helps prevent tooth decay in young children.
The fallen leaves slowly decayed into rich soil on the forest floor.

gloss_en: the process of gradually being destroyed by natural rotting; as a verb, to rot or decline

def_en: the analysis of a vector field
in a decomposed state
(chemistry) separation of a substance into two or more substances that may differ from each other and from the original substance
def_zh: 分解, 腐败, 变质
examples: Bacteria speed up the decomposition of leaves, returning nutrients to the soil.

gloss_en: the process by which dead plants and animals break down and rot; also, the act of breaking something into simpler parts.

def_en: a red or brown oxide coating on iron or steel caused by the action of oxygen and moisture
a plant disease that produces a reddish-brown discoloration of leaves and stems; caused by various rust fungi
the formation of reddish-brown ferric oxides on iron by low-temperature oxidation in the presence of water
any of various fungi causing rust disease in plants
examples: The old gate was covered in rust and creaked loudly when it was opened.
If you leave your bicycle out in the rain, the chain will soon rust.

gloss_en: the reddish-brown coating that forms on iron or steel when it is exposed to air and moisture; (as a verb) to become covered with this coating.

def_en: a sedimentary rock consisting mainly of calcium that was deposited by the remains of marine animals
examples: The ancient cathedral was built from blocks of pale grey limestone.

gloss_en: a hard rock, mostly made of calcium, that is widely used in building and in making cement.

def_en: a tetravalent nonmetallic element; next to oxygen it is the most abundant element in the earth's crust; occurs in clay and feldspar and granite and quartz and sand; used as a semiconductor in transistors
examples: Modern microchips are built on tiny wafers of pure silicon.

gloss_en: a common chemical element found in sand and rocks, widely used to make computer chips and glass.

def_en: a solid formed by the solidification of a chemical and having a highly regular atomic structure
a crystalline element used as a component in various electronic devices
a rock formed by the solidification of a substance; has regularly repeating internal structure; external plane faces
glassware made of quartz
def_zh: 水晶, 水晶装饰品, 结晶
examples: As the solution cooled slowly, tiny salt crystals began to form on the sides of the glass.

def_zh: 水晶的, 水晶一样的, 透明的
examples: The mountain lake was so crystal that we could see the pebbles lying on the bottom.

gloss_en: a clear, hard mineral or a solid whose atoms are arranged in a regular, repeating pattern, often with flat surfaces.

def_en: a ductile malleable reddish-brown corrosion-resistant diamagnetic metallic element; occurs in various minerals but is the only metal that occurs abundantly in large masses; used as an electrical and thermal conductor
a copper penny
a reddish-brown color resembling the color of polished copper
any of various small butterflies of the family Lycaenidae having coppery wings
examples: Most household electrical wires are made of copper because it conducts electricity so efficiently.

gloss_en: a reddish-brown metal that conducts heat and electricity well and is widely used in wiring and coins.

def_en: an advantage held by a competitor in a race
a soft heavy toxic malleable metallic element; bluish white when freshly cut but tarnishes readily to dull grey
evidence pointing to a possible solution
a position of leadership (especially in the phrase `take the lead')
def_zh: 铅, 铅条, 领导, 超前量, 领引, 榜样, 主角, 导线
examples: The old house had lead pipes, which were a health hazard.

def_zh: 引导, 带领, 领导, 指挥, 致使, 加铅于, 用铅包
领导, 带头, 导致, 用测深锤测深, 被铅覆盖
examples: The tour guide will lead us through the museum and explain the exhibits.
The lead singer of the band is very talented and charismatic.

gloss_en: a heavy, bluish-white metal

def_en: a hard malleable ductile silvery metallic element that is resistant to corrosion; used in alloys; occurs in pentlandite and smaltite and garnierite and millerite
a United States coin worth one twentieth of a dollar
five dollars worth of a drug
def_zh: 镍, 镍币, 五分镍币
examples: The coins are made from an alloy of copper and nickel.

def_en: plate with nickel
examples: The manufacturer chose to nickel the fittings to protect them from rust.

gloss_en: a hard, silvery metal used in alloys; (in the US) a coin worth five cents.

def_en: a silvery soft waxy metallic element of the alkali metal group; occurs abundantly in natural compounds (especially in salt water); burns with a yellow flame and reacts violently in water; occurs in sea water and in the mineral halite (rock salt)
examples: Doctors advise patients with high blood pressure to reduce the amount of sodium in their diet.

gloss_en: a soft silver-white chemical element that combines with chlorine to form common salt.

def_en: a white metallic element that burns with a brilliant light; the fifth most abundant element in the earth's crust; an important component of most plants and animals
examples: Dairy products such as milk and cheese are rich sources of calcium.

gloss_en: a soft, silver-white chemical element that is important for building strong bones and teeth.

def_en: a nonmetallic bivalent element that is normally a colorless odorless tasteless nonflammable diatomic gas; constitutes 21 percent of the atmosphere by volume; the most abundant element in the earth's crust
examples: Plants release oxygen into the atmosphere during photosynthesis.

gloss_en: a colorless, odorless gas that makes up about a fifth of the air and is essential for most living things to breathe.

def_en: a common nonmetallic element that is normally a colorless odorless tasteless inert diatomic gas; constitutes 78 percent of the atmosphere by volume; a constituent of all living tissues
examples: Farmers add nitrogen to the soil as fertiliser to help their crops grow.

gloss_en: a colourless, odourless gas that makes up about 78 percent of the Earth's atmosphere and is found in all living things.

def_en: a volatile flammable mixture of hydrocarbons (hexane and heptane and octane etc.) derived from petroleum; used mainly as a fuel in internal-combustion engines
examples: Rising gasoline prices made many commuters switch to public transport.

gloss_en: a liquid fuel made from petroleum, used mainly to power car and other internal-combustion engines.

def_en: a dark oil consisting mainly of hydrocarbons
examples: Much of the country's wealth comes from exporting crude petroleum.

gloss_en: the thick, dark oil found underground that is refined into fuels such as petrol and diesel.

def_en: an organic compound containing only carbon and hydrogen
examples: Burning hydrocarbons like coal and oil releases carbon dioxide into the air.

gloss_en: a chemical compound made only of hydrogen and carbon, such as those found in oil and gas

def_en: generic name for certain synthetic or semisynthetic materials that can be molded or extruded into objects or films or filaments or used for making e.g. coatings and adhesives
def_zh: 塑料, 可塑体, 可塑性物质
examples: Many companies are trying to reduce their reliance on single-use plastic packaging.

def_en: capable of being influenced or formed
def_zh: 塑料的, 塑造的, 有可塑性的, 造型的, 易受影响的, 有创造力的
examples: The children played with their colorful plastic toys in the sandbox.

gloss_en: A synthetic material made from a wide range of organic polymers, which can be molded into various shapes while soft and then set into a rigid or slightly elastic form.

def_en: (physics and chemistry) the simplest structural unit of an element or compound
examples: A water molecule consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

gloss_en: the smallest unit of a chemical substance that can exist on its own, made up of one or more atoms.

def_en: a body having finite mass and internal structure but negligible dimensions
a function word that can be used in English to form phrasal verbs
def_zh: 颗粒, 粒子, 质点, 极小量
examples: Tiny particles of dust floated in the beam of sunlight coming through the window.

gloss_en: a very small piece or amount of something, especially a tiny unit of matter.

def_en: a homogeneous mixture of two or more substances; frequently (but not necessarily) a liquid solution
a statement that solves a problem or explains how to solve the problem
a method for solving a problem
the set of values that give a true statement when substituted into an equation
def_zh: 解决, 解答, 溶液
examples: The government is seeking a long-term solution to the housing crisis.

gloss_en: A way of solving a problem or dealing with a difficult situation.

examples: The experiment measured how the solubility of the salt increased as the water grew warmer.

gloss_en: The extent to which a substance can dissolve in a given liquid, usually water.

def_en: (film) a gradual transition from one scene to the next; the next scene is gradually superimposed as the former scene fades out
examples: The film used a slow dissolve to move from the wedding scene to the funeral.

def_en: become weaker
cause to go into a solution
come to an end
examples: Stir the mixture until the sugar dissolves completely in the water.

gloss_en: to become part of a liquid, or to make a solid do this; also, to bring something such as an organization to an end.

def_en: an occurrence of thorough mixing
a new word formed by joining two others and combining their meanings
the act of blending components together thoroughly
examples: This coffee is a smooth blend of beans from three different countries.

def_en: combine into one
examples: Blend the sugar and butter together until the mixture becomes light and creamy.

gloss_en: to mix two or more things together so that they combine well; also a mixture made in this way.

def_en: a whole formed by a union of two or more elements or parts
(chemistry) a substance formed by chemical union of two or more elements or ingredients in definite proportion by weight
an enclosure of residences and other building (especially in the Orient)
def_zh: 混合物, 复合词, 化合物, 院子
examples: Water is a chemical compound formed from two atoms of hydrogen and one of oxygen.

def_en: put or add together
def_zh: 化合, 和解, 妥协
examples: Poor communication only compounded the problems the team was already struggling to solve.

def_zh: 复合的, 混合的, 化合的
examples: A bee's compound eye is made up of thousands of tiny separate lenses.

gloss_en: something made up of two or more separate parts or substances combined together.

def_en: (physics and chemistry) the smallest component of an element having the chemical properties of the element
(nontechnical usage) a tiny piece of anything
def_zh: 原子, 核能, 微粒, 微量
examples: A water molecule is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

gloss_en: the smallest unit of a chemical element, made up of a nucleus and electrons.

def_en: an elementary particle with negative charge
examples: Electrons flow through the wire and create an electric current.

gloss_en: a very small particle with a negative electric charge that moves around the nucleus of an atom.

def_en: an elementary particle with 0 charge and mass about equal to a proton; enters into the structure of the atomic nucleus
examples: When a neutron strikes the nucleus of a uranium atom, it can trigger fission.

gloss_en: a tiny particle with no electric charge, found in the nucleus of an atom.

def_en: a part of the cell containing DNA and RNA and responsible for growth and reproduction
the positively charged dense center of an atom
(astronomy) the center of the head of a comet; consists of small solid particles of ice and frozen gas that vaporizes on approaching the sun to form the coma and tail
any histologically identifiable mass of neural cell bodies in the brain or spinal cord
def_zh: 核心, 核子, 原子核, 起点
examples: Under the microscope, the students could clearly see the dark nucleus at the centre of each cell.

gloss_en: the central part of a cell that contains its genetic material (DNA) and controls its growth and reproduction.

examples: The number of protons in the nucleus determines which element an atom is.

gloss_en: A positively charged particle found in the nucleus of an atom.

def_en: a particle that is electrically charged (positive or negative); an atom or molecule or group that has lost or gained one or more electrons
examples: When salt dissolves in water, it separates into positively and negatively charged ions.

gloss_en: an atom or molecule with an electric charge because it has lost or gained one or more electrons.

def_en: the magnitude of something in a particular direction (especially length or width or height)
one of three Cartesian coordinates that determine a position in space
def_zh: 尺寸, 次元, 面积, 维数
examples: The room has three dimensions: length, width, and height.

def_en: indicate the dimensions on
shape or form to required dimensions
examples: The artist managed to dimension the sculpture in a way that made it seem larger than it was.

gloss_en: a measure of how long, wide, or thick something is

def_en: the act of vibrating
a distinctive emotional aura experienced instinctively
examples: You could feel the vibration of the passing train through the floor of the old house.

gloss_en: a continuous, rapid, slight shaking movement.

def_en: the process of purifying a liquid by boiling it and condensing its vapors
examples: The distillation of seawater removes the salt and produces fresh, drinkable water.

gloss_en: the process of purifying or separating a liquid by heating it until it becomes vapor and then cooling it back into liquid.

def_en: the amount per unit size
examples: Because ice has a lower density than water, it floats on the surface of a lake.

gloss_en: the degree to which something is filled or crowded; in science, the amount of mass in a given volume.

def_en: the resistance encountered when one body is moved in contact with another
effort expended in moving one object over another with pressure
def_zh: 摩擦, 摩擦治疗, 不和
examples: The friction between the tyres and the road is what allows a car to slow down and stop.

gloss_en: the force that resists motion when one surface rubs against another; also, disagreement between people.

def_en: (physics) a device that attracts iron and produces a magnetic field
def_zh: 磁铁, 有吸引力之人, 有吸引力之物
examples: The children used a small magnet to pick up the scattered pins from the floor.

gloss_en: a piece of iron or other material that attracts metal objects and has a magnetic field.

def_en: an ordered array of the components of an emission or wave
a broad range of related objects or values or qualities or ideas or activities
def_zh: 光谱, 范围, 系列
examples: The conference brought together people from across the political spectrum.

gloss_en: the full range of different but related things, ideas, or wavelengths of light.

def_en: the person who leads a musical group
a substance that readily conducts e.g. electricity and heat
the person who collects fares on a public conveyance
a device designed to transmit electricity, heat, etc.
def_zh: 领导者, 指挥者, 售票员, 向导
examples: The conductor raised her baton, and the whole orchestra began to play as one.

gloss_en: a person who stands in front of an orchestra or choir and directs its performance.

def_en: a means or instrumentality for storing or communicating information
the surrounding environment
an intervening substance through which signals can travel as a means for communication
a liquid with which pigment is mixed by a painter
def_zh: 媒体, 方法, 媒介
examples: Sound needs a medium like air or water to travel, so it cannot move through empty space.

gloss_en: A substance or material through which something, such as a wave or signal, travels.

examples: In optics we learn how lenses bend light to form the images we see through a telescope.

gloss_en: The branch of physics that studies light and how it behaves and interacts with matter.

def_en: expel (gases or odors)
give off, send forth, or discharge; as of light, heat, or radiation, vapor, etc.
def_zh: 发出, 放射, 发表(意见等), 散发, 发行
examples: The old factory continued to emit thick black smoke into the sky.

gloss_en: to send out light, heat, sound, gas, or a smell.

def_en: put out or expel from a place
leave an aircraft rapidly, using an ejection seat or capsule
def_zh: 逐放, 放逐, 喷射
examples: The pilot managed to eject from the aircraft seconds before it crashed.

gloss_en: to force something or someone out of a place suddenly and often violently

def_en: a motorboat with an open deck or a half deck
def_zh: 下水, 汽艇, 发射
examples: The successful launch of the satellite marked a new era in space exploration.

def_en: propel with force
launch for the first time; launch on a maiden voyage
get going; give impetus to
def_zh: 使下水, 发射, 发动
起飞, 下水, 投入, 开始
examples: The company plans to launch a new marketing campaign next quarter.

gloss_en: to start an important plan or activity

def_en: the process whereby heat changes something from a solid to a liquid
warm weather following a freeze; snow and ice melt
a relaxation or slackening of tensions or reserve; becoming less hostile
def_zh: 融解, 融化, 解冻时期
examples: The spring thaw caused the river to overflow its banks.
Leave the frozen meat on the counter to thaw before you cook it.

gloss_en: to become soft or liquid again after being frozen.

def_zh: 收缩, 萎缩, 回避
examples: The manufacturer measured the shrink of the fabric after the first wash.

def_en: reduce in size; reduce physically
become smaller or draw together
decrease in size, range, or extent
def_zh: 收缩, 退缩, 萎缩, 缩小, 回避
examples: My wool sweater shrank two sizes after I accidentally washed it in hot water.

gloss_en: to become smaller in size or amount, or to make something smaller.

def_en: plumbing fixture consisting of a water basin fixed to a wall or floor and having a drainpipe
(technology) a process that acts to absorb or remove energy or a substance from a system
def_zh: 藏垢的场所, 沟渠, 污水槽
examples: The metal block acts as a heat sink, drawing warmth away from the circuit.

gloss_en: In physics, a place or object that absorbs and removes energy, such as heat, from a system.

examples: Infrared telescopes can peer through clouds of dust to reveal stars forming inside them.

gloss_en: Telescopes designed to detect infrared light, which lets astronomers see objects hidden by dust or too cool to shine in visible light.

examples: With a simple pair of binoculars you can already see the four largest moons of Jupiter.

gloss_en: A handheld optical instrument with two eyepieces used to magnify distant objects for both eyes at once.

examples: Light, radio, and X-rays are all electromagnetic waves that differ only in their wavelength.

gloss_en: A wave of combined electric and magnetic fields that carries energy through space, including light and radio waves.

examples: Astronomers use radio waves to study objects in space that give off little visible light.

gloss_en: A type of electromagnetic wave with a long wavelength, used to carry signals such as radio and TV broadcasts.

examples: Bats navigate in the dark by sending out ultrasonic waves and listening for the echoes.

gloss_en: A sound wave with a frequency too high for humans to hear.

examples: Through spectroscopy, astronomers can tell what a distant star is made of just from its light.

gloss_en: The technique of studying the light given off or absorbed by matter to learn about its composition.

examples: Radioactive decay causes an unstable element to slowly change into a more stable one over time.

gloss_en: The process by which an unstable atomic nucleus loses energy by emitting particles or radiation.

examples: Because carbon-14 has a known half-life, scientists can use it to estimate the age of old remains.

gloss_en: The time it takes for half of a radioactive substance to decay.

def_en: a mixture containing two or more metallic elements or metallic and nonmetallic elements usually fused together or dissolving into each other when molten
examples: Bronze is an alloy of copper and tin that is harder than either metal alone.

def_en: make an alloy of
def_zh: 将...铸成合金, 减低成色
examples: Manufacturers often alloy steel with chromium to make it resistant to rust.

gloss_en: a metal made by combining two or more metallic elements.

examples: Radiometric dating lets geologists calculate the age of a rock from the isotopes locked inside it.

gloss_en: A method of determining the age of rocks or fossils by measuring the decay of radioactive elements they contain.

examples: Radiocarbon dating showed that the wooden tool was about five thousand years old.

gloss_en: A technique that estimates the age of once-living material by measuring how much carbon-14 remains in it.

examples: With relative dating, geologists assume lower rock layers are older than the ones above them.

gloss_en: A method of determining the order of past events by comparing the position of rock layers rather than measuring exact ages.

examples: The professor explained that absolute dating of the charcoal fragments allowed archaeologists to place the settlement at around 9,000 years ago.
collocations: use absolute dating to determine the age of

gloss_en: A method of determining the actual calendar age of an archaeological find or rock layer, for example through radiocarbon analysis, rather than just its age relative to other finds.

def_en: recover through digging
find by digging in the ground
form by hollowing
remove the inner part or the core of
def_zh: 挖空, 挖出, 凿通
examples: Archaeologists excavated the ancient tomb and uncovered a wealth of golden artifacts.

gloss_en: to dig out earth carefully, especially to find buried objects or to prepare a site for building.

examples: In today's lecture we'll look at how Paleolithic hunters adapted their toolkits to the changing Ice Age climate.

gloss_en: Relating to the Old Stone Age, the earliest and longest period of human prehistory, when people made simple chipped-stone tools and lived by hunting and gathering.

examples: Mesolithic sites along the coast show that these communities relied heavily on fishing and shellfish gathering.

gloss_en: Relating to the Middle Stone Age, the transitional period between the Paleolithic and Neolithic, marked by smaller, finer stone tools and a foraging lifestyle after the last Ice Age.

examples: The Neolithic revolution transformed human society, as people shifted from hunting and gathering to settled agriculture.

gloss_en: Relating to the New Stone Age, the final Stone Age period when humans began farming, domesticating animals, and living in permanent villages.

def_en: relating to or arranged according to temporal order
def_zh: 年代学的, 按时间顺序的, 按照年月顺序的
examples: The museum displays the artifacts in chronological order, beginning with the earliest civilizations.

def_zh: 编年的, 按年月日顺序的

gloss_en: arranged in the order in which events happened, from earliest to latest.

def_zh: 剩余, 零料, 遗迹
examples: Archaeologists uncovered the remnants of an ancient stone wall.
A few remnant patches of snow still lay in the shadows of the valley.

def_en: n a small part or portion that remains after the main part no longer exists
n a piece of cloth that is left over after the rest has been used or sold

gloss_en: a small remaining part or trace of something after most of it has gone.

def_en: be left; of persons, questions, problems, results, evidence, etc.
def_zh: 保持, 逗留, 剩余
examples: I will remain in the city for a few more days.

def_zh: 停留, 居住, 继续

gloss_en: to stay in the same place or condition

def_en: ceramic ware made from clay and baked in a kiln
the craft of making earthenware
a workshop where clayware is made
examples: The museum displayed beautiful pieces of ancient pottery decorated with painted figures.

gloss_en: objects such as pots and dishes made from clay and hardened by heat; also the craft of making them.

def_en: the art of making and decorating pottery
examples: She studied ceramics at art school and now sells her handmade bowls online.

gloss_en: the art of making pots, dishes, and other objects from clay, or the objects made in this way.

def_en: any piece of furniture or decorative object or the like produced in a former period and valuable because of its beauty or rarity
examples: The clock is a genuine antique that has been in the family for over two hundred years.

def_en: shop for antiques
give an antique appearance to
examples: The craftsman antiqued the new cabinet so that it would look like a piece from the eighteenth century.

def_en: made in or typical of earlier times and valued for its age
def_zh: 古老的, 古风的, 旧式的, 过时的
examples: They furnished the living room with an antique table and a pair of matching chairs.

gloss_en: an old and often valuable object such as furniture, jewellery, or art made in an earlier period.

def_en: a man-made object taken as a whole
examples: The museum's newest exhibit features ancient artifacts recovered from a Roman shipwreck.

def_zh: 人为现象, 人工产物

gloss_en: an object made by a human being, especially one of historical or cultural interest

examples: The lecturer argued that wind power could supply a substantial share of the region's electricity if turbines were sited offshore.

gloss_en: Electricity or mechanical energy generated by using the wind, typically by turning turbine blades; a major renewable energy source.

examples: Residents initially opposed the offshore wind farm, but it now supplies clean electricity to thousands of homes.

gloss_en: A large group of wind turbines built in one area, on land or offshore, to produce electricity on a commercial scale.

examples: Early New England factories were located beside rivers so they could run their machinery on water power.

gloss_en: Energy obtained from moving or falling water and used to drive machinery or generate electricity; also called hydropower.

examples: Before steam engines, a village mill typically used a water wheel to grind grain into flour.
collocations: a water wheel powered the mill

gloss_en: A large wheel turned by flowing or falling water, historically used to power mills and other machinery.

examples: Because tides are highly predictable, tidal energy offers a more reliable output than wind or solar power.

gloss_en: Renewable energy produced by capturing the movement of ocean tides, usually with underwater turbines or barrages.

examples: Engineers proposed building a barrage across the estuary to generate electricity from the rising and falling tides.

gloss_en: A dam-like barrier built across a river or estuary to control water flow, often used to capture tidal energy for generating electricity.

def_en: a barrier constructed to contain the flow of water or to keep out the sea
female parent of an animal especially domestic livestock
examples: The huge dam supplies electricity and water to millions of people in the valley.

def_en: obstruct with, or as if with, a dam
examples: Engineers plan to dam the river in order to create a large reservoir.

gloss_en: a strong wall built across a river to hold back water and control its flow

examples: The university plans to cover the library roof with panels so that solar energy can offset part of its electricity use.

gloss_en: Energy from the sun's radiation, collected with solar panels or other devices and converted into electricity or heat.

def_en: undesirable state of the natural environment being contaminated with harmful substances as a consequence of human activities
examples: Burning fossil fuels for electricity remains a major source of air pollution in industrialized regions.

gloss_en: The contamination of air, water, or land with harmful substances, especially as a byproduct of energy production and industry.

def_en: a system formed by the interaction of a community of organisms with their physical environment
examples: Pollution from the factory disrupted the delicate ecosystem of the entire river valley.

gloss_en: all the living things in an area and the way they affect each other and their physical environment.

def_en: having the qualities or effects of a poison
not safe to eat
marked by deep ill will; deliberately harmful
def_zh: 有毒的, 恶毒的, 讨厌的
examples: Some brightly coloured frogs in the rainforest are extremely poisonous.

gloss_en: containing a substance that can cause illness or death if swallowed, breathed in, or touched.

def_en: make impure
def_zh: 污染, 弄脏, 玷污
examples: The factory was fined for dumping waste that polluted the nearby river.

gloss_en: to make air, water, or land dirty or harmful, especially with chemicals or waste.

def_en: make radioactive by adding radioactive material
def_zh: 弄污, 弄脏, 污染, 毒害
examples: Waste from the factory had contaminated the river, killing much of the fish.

gloss_en: to make something dirty, impure, or harmful by adding a dangerous or unwanted substance.

def_en: any materials unused and rejected as worthless or unwanted
useless or profitless activity; using or expending or consuming thoughtlessly or carelessly
(law) reduction in the value of an estate caused by act or neglect
def_zh: 浪费, 废物, 损耗, 消耗, 荒地, 垃圾, 地面风化物
examples: The waste management system in the city is inefficient.

def_en: spend thoughtlessly; throw away
def_zh: 浪费, 消耗, 使荒芜
浪费, 消耗, 变消瘦
examples: Don't waste your time watching TV all day.

def_zh: 废弃的, 荒芜的, 多余的
examples: The waste land was reclaimed and turned into a park.

gloss_en: to use or expend something unnecessarily or carelessly

def_en: worthless material that is to be disposed of
def_zh: 废物, 垃圾, 胡说
examples: The council collects household rubbish from every street twice a week.

def_en: attack strongly
examples: Critics rubbished the new policy before it had even been given a chance.

gloss_en: waste material or things that are no longer wanted; also nonsense or something of very poor quality.

def_en: the type of environment in which an organism or group normally lives or occurs
def_zh: 栖息地, 居留地, 自生地, 聚集处
examples: Draining the wetlands destroyed the natural habitat of many rare birds.

def_zh: 习生地, 产地, 生境, 栖所

gloss_en: the natural home or environment where a particular animal or plant normally lives.

def_en: a consecrated place where sacred objects are kept
def_zh: 圣所(指教堂、寺院等), 耶路撒冷的神殿, 避难所
examples: The injured birds were taken to a wildlife sanctuary to recover.

def_zh: 庇护所, 避难所, 教堂

gloss_en: a safe place where someone or something is protected, especially wildlife; also a sacred part of a temple or church

def_en: a composition written in metrical feet forming rhythmical lines
examples: For Friday's seminar, please analyze how the poem uses imagery of winter to convey a sense of loss.

gloss_en: A single piece of writing in verse that expresses ideas or emotions through rhythm, imagery, and often rhyme.

def_en: literature in metrical form
any communication resembling poetry in beauty or the evocation of feeling
def_zh: 诗, 韵文, 诗歌艺术
examples: The professor traced how American poetry moved away from strict rhyme schemes in the early twentieth century.

gloss_en: Literary work in verse as an art form; the body of poems or the craft of writing them.

def_en: a writer of poems (the term is usually reserved for writers of good poetry)
examples: The Romantic poet drew much of his inspiration from the natural landscape.

gloss_en: a person who writes poems.

def_en: the phonological or orthographic sound or appearance of a word that can be used to describe or identify something
a perceptual structure
the visual appearance of something or someone
a printed document with spaces in which to write
def_zh: 形状, 形体, 类型, 方式, 表格, 形式
examples: The form of the sculpture was inspired by nature.
The company will form a new department next year.

gloss_en: the shape or appearance of something, or a document with blank spaces to be filled in

examples: Shakespeare's sonnets often turn in the final couplet, where the speaker resolves or reverses the poem's argument.

gloss_en: A fourteen-line poem with a fixed rhyme scheme, traditionally written in iambic pentameter, made famous by poets like Petrarch and Shakespeare.

examples: Once you can hear the meter, you'll notice where the poet deliberately breaks the pattern for emphasis.

gloss_en: The regular rhythmic pattern of stressed and unstressed syllables in a line of poetry.

examples: The instructor had students clap out a line of iambic pentameter to feel its da-DUM da-DUM rhythm.

gloss_en: A poetic meter with five pairs of syllables per line, each pair having an unstressed syllable followed by a stressed one; the standard line of English verse.

def_en: a piece of poetry
a line of metrical text
examples: The poet is best known for the beautiful verse she wrote about the sea.

def_en: compose verses or put into verse
familiarize through thorough study or experience
examples: Over the years he became well versed in the traditions of classical music.

gloss_en: writing arranged in lines, often with a regular rhythm or rhyme; poetry.

examples: Milton chose blank verse for Paradise Lost because he felt rhyme would constrain the epic's grandeur.

gloss_en: Poetry written in regular meter, usually iambic pentameter, but without rhyme; common in English drama and epic poetry.

examples: Walt Whitman's free verse shocked readers who expected poems to rhyme and scan predictably.

gloss_en: Poetry that does not follow a regular meter or rhyme scheme, letting the rhythm follow natural speech.

def_en: the basic rhythmic unit in a piece of music
recurring at regular intervals
the arrangement of spoken words alternating stressed and unstressed elements
def_zh: 旋律, 节奏, 韵律, 匀称, 张弛节律
examples: The dancers moved in perfect time to the steady rhythm of the drums.

gloss_en: a regular, repeated pattern of sounds or movements, especially the beat in music.

examples: Notice how the poet abandons rhyme in the final stanza to signal the speaker's emotional breakdown.

gloss_en: The matching of similar end sounds in words, especially at the ends of lines of poetry; also a word that sounds like another.

def_en: a performance of music by players or singers not involving theatrical staging
def_zh: 音乐会, 和声, 一致
examples: The student orchestra is giving a free concert in the campus auditorium this Saturday evening.

gloss_en: A live public performance of music, typically by an orchestra, band, or soloist before an audience.

def_en: instrumentalists not including string players
a stripe or stripes of contrasting color
an adornment consisting of a strip of a contrasting color or material
a range of frequencies between two limits
examples: The rock band played to a sold-out stadium last night.
The villagers banded together to protect their homes from the flood.

gloss_en: A group of musicians who play popular music together.

def_en: any activity that is performed alone without assistance
