a musical composition for one voice or instrument (with or without accompaniment)
a flight in which the aircraft pilot is unaccompanied
def_zh: 独奏, 独唱, 单独表演
examples: The audience applauded loudly after the pianist finished her dazzling solo.

def_en: fly alone, without a co-pilot or passengers
examples: After months of training, the young pilot was finally allowed to solo.

def_zh: 单独的, 独奏的, 独唱的
examples: The climber made a dangerous solo ascent of the mountain without any support.

gloss_en: done alone by one person; as a noun, a piece of music or a performance done by a single person.

def_en: a long and complex sonata for symphony orchestra
examples: The orchestra performed Beethoven's Ninth Symphony to a packed concert hall.

gloss_en: a long, complex piece of music written for a full orchestra, usually in several movements.

def_en: a musical organization consisting of a group of instrumentalists including string players
seating on the main floor in a theater
def_zh: 管弦乐队, 乐队演奏处
examples: The orchestra tuned their instruments before the conductor raised his baton.

gloss_en: a large group of musicians who play many different instruments together, especially classical music, led by a conductor.

examples: The ethnomusicology lecture examined how Appalachian folk music preserved melodies brought over from the British Isles.

gloss_en: Traditional music of a community or region, passed down orally and often played on acoustic instruments, reflecting everyday life and culture.

examples: The professor traced how country music grew out of folk ballads sung by settlers in the American South.

gloss_en: A popular music genre that developed in the rural southern United States, featuring guitars, fiddles, and story-like lyrics about everyday life.

examples: The lecture compared how pop music production changed once recording moved from studios to laptops.

gloss_en: Commercial popular music aimed at a wide audience, usually with catchy melodies and simple, memorable structures.

examples: The course examines how rock and roll reshaped American youth culture in the 1950s and 60s.
collocations: the birth of rock and roll

gloss_en: A style of popular music that emerged in 1950s America, blending rhythm and blues with country music and driven by electric guitar and a strong beat.

examples: The music department hosts a chamber music series where student quartets perform Haydn and Brahms.

gloss_en: Classical music written for a small group of instruments, such as a string quartet, originally meant to be performed in a room rather than a concert hall.

def_en: the amount of 3-dimensional space occupied by an object
a publication that is one of a set of several similar publications
a relative amount
the magnitude of sound (usually in a specified direction)
def_zh: 册, 卷, 体积, 容量, 大量, 许多, 份量, 音量
examples: The volume of a sphere can be calculated using a specific mathematical formula.
Please don't volume your music so loud; it's disturbing the neighbors.
The book is a volume of essays on various literary topics.

gloss_en: the amount of space that a substance or object occupies, or that is enclosed within a container

def_en: an orderly grouping (of things or persons) considered as a unit; the result of arranging
an organized structure for arranging or classifying
the act of arranging and adapting a piece of music
def_zh: 排列, 整齐, 安排
examples: We need to finalize the seating arrangement for the wedding reception.

gloss_en: a plan or preparation for something

def_en: compatibility in opinion and action
the structure of music with respect to the composition and progression of chords
a harmonious state of things in general and of their properties (as of colors and sounds); congruity of parts with one another and with the whole
agreement of opinions
def_zh: 协调, 和睦, 调和
examples: The two communities have lived together in harmony for generations.

gloss_en: a state of peaceful agreement, or a pleasing combination of parts, sounds, or colours.

def_en: an instance or single occasion for some event
a period of time considered as a resource under your control and sufficient to accomplish something
an indefinite period (usually marked by specific attributes or activities)
a suitable moment
def_zh: 时间, 时侯, 时机, 时期, 期限, 次数, 节拍, 暂停, 规定时间
examples: What time is it?

def_zh: 测定...的时间, 记录...的时间, 计时, 定时
examples: The coach will time the runners.

def_zh: 时间的, 记时的, 定时的, 定期的, 分期的
examples: This is a time bomb waiting to happen.

def_zh: DOS内部命令:用于显示或设定系统的时间

gloss_en: a point or period in the past, present, or future

def_en: a brief written record
a short personal letter
a notation representing the pitch and duration of a musical sound
a tone of voice that shows what the speaker is feeling
def_zh: 笔记, 记录, 注解, 票据, 符号, 显要, 注重, 便笺, 照会
examples: Please make a note of the important points discussed during the meeting.

def_zh: 记录, 注解, 注意
examples: Please note that the deadline for submissions has been extended.

gloss_en: A short piece of writing that helps you remember something or gives information.

def_en: a straight line connecting two points on a curve
a combination of three or more notes that blend harmoniously when sounded together
examples: The guitarist strummed a gentle chord and the whole room fell silent.

def_en: play chords on (a string instrument)
examples: She began to chord softly on the piano to accompany the singer.

gloss_en: a group of musical notes played together to produce a harmonious sound.

def_en: any utterance produced simultaneously by a group
a group of people assembled to sing together
a body of dancers or singers who perform together
a company of actors who comment (by speaking or singing in unison) on the action in a classical Greek play
examples: The whole audience joined in singing the chorus of the famous song.
"We agree!" the students chorused in reply.

gloss_en: a large group of singers, or the part of a song that is repeated after each verse; also something said by many people at once

examples: The drama club is staging a play by Tennessee Williams, and auditions are open to all majors.

gloss_en: A dramatic work written to be performed by actors on a stage, telling a story through dialogue and action.

def_en: a drama set to music; consists of singing with orchestral accompaniment and an orchestral overture and interludes
a commercial browser
a building where musical dramas are performed
examples: They dressed formally to attend the opera at the grand old theater downtown.

gloss_en: a dramatic musical work in which the words are sung with orchestral accompaniment.

examples: According to the famous story, Archimedes leapt from his bath shouting 'Eureka!' when he grasped the principle of buoyancy.
collocations: the story of Archimedes and the crown

gloss_en: An ancient Greek mathematician, physicist, and inventor (c. 287-212 BCE), famous for discovering the principle of buoyancy and for inventions like the screw pump.

examples: Socrates left no writings of his own; we know his ideas mainly through the dialogues of his student Plato.

gloss_en: An ancient Greek philosopher (c. 470-399 BCE) who taught by asking probing questions rather than lecturing, and who was sentenced to death in Athens.

examples: In the Republic, Plato uses the allegory of the cave to explain how education leads the mind toward truth.

gloss_en: An ancient Greek philosopher (c. 428-348 BCE), student of Socrates and teacher of Aristotle, who founded the Academy and wrote influential philosophical dialogues.

examples: Aristotle classified hundreds of animal species, making him one of the earliest systematic biologists.

gloss_en: An ancient Greek philosopher and scientist (384-322 BCE), student of Plato, whose systematic writings on logic, biology, ethics, and poetics shaped Western thought.

examples: The seminar explores how William Shakespeare adapted older chronicles and tales into his history plays.

gloss_en: The English playwright and poet (1564-1616), widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language, author of plays like Hamlet and 154 sonnets.

def_en: graphic art consisting of an artistic composition made by applying paints to a surface
creating a picture with paints
the act of applying paint to a surface
the occupation of a house painter
examples: The museum acquired a rare 17th-century oil painting for its collection.

gloss_en: A picture made using paint, or the activity of creating pictures with paint.

examples: The instructor showed how Van Gogh loaded his brush with thick paint to create textured surfaces.

gloss_en: A hand tool with bristles set in a handle, used by painters to apply paint to a canvas or other surface.

examples: Up close, the portrait dissolves into individual brushstrokes, but from a distance the image looks smooth.

gloss_en: A single mark made by a painter's brush; also the characteristic way an artist applies paint, part of their technique.

def_en: a visual attribute of things that results from the light they emit or transmit or reflect
interest and variety and intensity
the timbre of a musical sound
def_zh: 颜色, 面色, 颜料, 外貌
examples: The bright color of the painting caught my eye.

def_zh: 把...涂上颜色, 粉饰, 使脸红, 歪曲
examples: The artist colored the picture with vibrant hues.

gloss_en: a quality of an object that is seen when light falls on it

def_en: a usually soluble substance for staining or coloring e.g. fabrics or hair
examples: The scarf was colored with a natural dye made from plant roots.

def_en: color with dye
examples: She decided to dye her hair a deep shade of red for the summer.

gloss_en: a substance used to change the color of cloth, hair, or other material; (as a verb) to color something in this way.

def_en: a substance used as a coating to protect or decorate a surface (especially a mixture of pigment suspended in a liquid); dries to form a hard coating
def_zh: 油漆, 颜料, 绘画作品, 涂漆

def_en: make a painting
apply paint to; coat with paint
make a painting of
def_zh: 油漆, 绘, 画, 描绘, 装饰, 点缀
examples: The walls need to be painted before we move in.

gloss_en: a colored substance used for protecting or decorating surfaces

def_en: dry coloring material (especially a powder to be mixed with a liquid to produce paint, etc.)
any substance whose presence in plant or animal tissues produces a characteristic color
def_zh: 色素, 色质, 颜料

def_en: acquire pigment; become colored or imbued
color or dye with a pigment
examples: Certain cells pigment the feathers, giving each bird its distinctive coloration.

gloss_en: a substance that gives colour to something, such as the coloured powder used to make paint, or the natural colour in skin, hair, or plants.

def_en: a diagram or picture illustrating textual material
a model of a bodily form (especially of a person)
a combination of points and lines and planes that form a visible palpable shape
an amount of money expressed numerically
def_zh: 数字, 价格, 图形, 形状

def_zh: 描绘, 表示, 演算, 认为
计算, 出现, 估计
examples: It took me a while to figure out how to assemble the new furniture.

gloss_en: A number, especially one that is part of an official report or statistic.

def_en: a heavy, closely woven fabric (used for clothing or chairs or sails or tents)
an oil painting on canvas fabric
the setting for a narrative or fictional or dramatic account
the mat that forms the floor of the ring in which boxers or professional wrestlers compete
def_zh: 帆布, 画布, 油画
examples: The artist stretched a fresh canvas over the wooden frame before beginning her portrait.

gloss_en: a strong, heavy cloth used for tents, sails, and bags, or a piece of it stretched on a frame for an artist to paint on.

def_en: the framework for a pair of eyeglasses
a single one of a series of still transparent pictures forming a cinema, television or video film
a single drawing in a comic_strip
an application that divides the user's display into two or more windows that can be scrolled independently
examples: She carefully placed the photograph in a decorative frame before hanging it on the wall.

def_zh: 构成, 设计, 制定, 使适合, 陷害
examples: The artist decided to frame his latest painting himself.

def_zh: 框架, 图文框, 帧

gloss_en: a structure that surrounds and supports something, such as a picture or a window

def_en: preliminary drawing for later elaboration
a brief literary description
short descriptive summary (of events)
def_zh: 素描, 草图, 小品
examples: The architect made a rough sketch of the building on a napkin.

def_en: make a sketch of
def_zh: 描绘略图, 画素描或速写
examples: She sat by the harbour and sketched the fishing boats in her notebook.

gloss_en: a quick, rough drawing that shows the main features of something without much detail.

examples: Oil painting lets artists rework a canvas for weeks because the paint dries so slowly.

gloss_en: The art or a work of painting with pigments mixed in slow-drying oil, a medium that allows rich color and fine blending.

def_en: any likeness of a person, in any medium
def_zh: 肖像, 人像, 半身像, 描写, 竖排格式
examples: A portrait of the university's founder hangs in the main hall.

gloss_en: a painting, drawing, or photograph of a person, especially of the face.

def_en: a particular kind (as to appearance)
(botany) the narrow elongated part of the pistil between the ovary and the stigma
editorial directions to be followed in spelling and punctuation and capitalization and typographical display
a slender bristlelike or tubular process
def_zh: 风格, 时尚, 文体, 风度, 字体, 类型
examples: The artist's unique style has been influential in the development of modern art.

def_zh: 称呼, (根据新款式)设计, 使合潮流
examples: The hairdresser will style your hair for the wedding.

gloss_en: a way of doing something, especially one that is typical of a particular person or group

def_en: an artistic movement in France beginning in 1907 that featured surfaces of geometrical planes
examples: Picasso and Braque pioneered cubism, breaking figures into overlapping planes and angles.

gloss_en: an early 20th-century art movement that shows objects as fragmented geometric shapes seen from several angles at once

examples: Critics coined the name Fauvism because the paintings' violent colors made the artists seem like wild beasts.

gloss_en: An early twentieth-century art movement, led by Matisse, known for using intense, unnatural colors and bold brushwork; its painters were nicknamed 'wild beasts.'

examples: With Abstractism, painters like Kandinsky abandoned recognizable subjects and let color and form carry the meaning.

gloss_en: Abstract art: a style of painting that does not depict recognizable objects, instead using shapes, colors, and lines for their own expressive effect.

examples: Impressionism got its name from Monet's painting 'Impression, Sunrise,' which critics initially mocked.

gloss_en: A late nineteenth-century French painting movement that captured fleeting effects of light and everyday scenes with quick, visible brushstrokes.

examples: Warhol's soup cans show how Pop Art blurred the line between commercial imagery and fine art.

gloss_en: A 1950s-60s art movement that drew imagery from popular culture, such as advertising, comic strips, and consumer products, exemplified by Andy Warhol.

examples: Modern cosmology traces the universe's history back to a fraction of a second after the Big Bang.

gloss_en: The scientific study of the origin, structure, and evolution of the universe as a whole.

def_en: of or from or pertaining to or characteristic of the cosmos or universe
inconceivably extended in space or time
def_zh: 宇宙的, 宇宙航行的, 无限的, 有秩序的
examples: Astronomers study cosmic radiation to learn how the universe began.

gloss_en: relating to the universe or outer space; also extremely large in scale or importance

def_en: relating to or derived from the sun or utilizing the energies of the sun
def_zh: 太阳的, 日光的, 源自太阳的

gloss_en: relating to, coming from, or using the energy of the sun

examples: During a total solar eclipse, the sky darkens enough for astronomers to observe the Sun's corona.
collocations: the path of a solar eclipse

gloss_en: An event in which the Moon passes between the Sun and the Earth, blocking the Sun's light partly or completely.

def_en: of or relating to or associated with the moon
def_zh: 阴历的, 月的, 微亮的(指光)
examples: The lunar surface is covered with craters formed by ancient meteor impacts.

gloss_en: relating to the moon.

examples: The astronomy club is organizing a late-night trip off campus to watch the Perseid meteor shower.
collocations: the peak of the meteor shower

gloss_en: An event in which many meteors streak across the night sky, occurring when Earth passes through debris left by a comet.

examples: Interplanetary probes like Voyager have sent back data from the outer reaches of the solar system.

gloss_en: Existing or traveling between the planets of a solar system.

def_en: (astronomy) a celestial body of hot gases that radiates energy derived from thermonuclear reactions in the interior
any celestial body visible (as a point of light) from the Earth at night
an actor who plays a principal role
a plane figure with 5 or more points; often used as an emblem
def_zh: 星, 恒星, 星形物, 运气, 明星
examples: The star of the movie walked the red carpet.

def_zh: 以星状物装饰, 用星号标, 使成为明星
examples: The famous actor will star in the new play.

gloss_en: a large ball of hot, glowing gas in space, or a person who is famous, especially in the entertainment industry

def_en: being or relating to or resembling or emanating from stars
def_zh: 星的, 似星的, 星球的, 主要的
examples: The stellar performance of the actress earned her a standing ovation.

gloss_en: relating to stars, or exceptionally good or talented

examples: The lecture explained that intergalactic space is not completely empty but contains extremely thin gas.

gloss_en: Existing or occurring in the space between galaxies.

def_en: between or among stars
examples: Scientists study interstellar dust to learn how new stars are formed.

gloss_en: existing or happening between the stars.

def_en: study of the earth's surface; includes people's responses to topography and climate and soil and vegetation
examples: In geography class we learned how mountains and rivers shape where cities grow.

gloss_en: the study of the earth's surface, including its countries, landscapes, climate, and the way people live in different places.

examples: The geographer used satellite images to map how the river delta had shifted over the past century.

gloss_en: A scientist who studies the Earth's surface, including its landforms, climates, and how humans interact with their environment.

def_en: a town in eastern Mississippi
an imaginary great circle on the surface of the earth passing through the north and south poles at right angles to the equator
def_zh: 子午线, 经线, 顶点
examples: The prime meridian passes through Greenwich and marks zero degrees of longitude.

def_en: of or happening at noon
def_zh: 子午线的, 最高点的, 顶点的
examples: The sun reaches its greatest height at the meridian altitude, when it crosses due south.

gloss_en: an imaginary line on the earth's surface running from the North Pole to the South Pole, used to measure longitude.

def_en: (mathematics) one of a set of parallel geometric figures (parallel lines or planes)
def_zh: 平行, 对比, 相匹敌之物
examples: Historians drew a parallel between the two economic crises a century apart.

def_en: be parallel to
make or place parallel to something
def_zh: 与...平行, 与...相似, 相比, 使平行
examples: The rise in sales closely paralleled the growth of the company's online store.

def_en: being everywhere equidistant and not intersecting
examples: The two railway tracks ran parallel to each other across the plain.

gloss_en: similar and happening at the same time; also, a similarity, or lines that stay the same distance apart.

def_en: the line at which the sky and Earth appear to meet
the range of interest or activity that can be anticipated
a specific layer or stratum of soil or subsoil in a vertical cross section of land
the great circle on the celestial sphere whose plane passes through the sensible horizon and the center of the Earth
def_zh: 地平线, 眼界, (天)视地平
examples: The sun slowly sank below the horizon as the sky turned orange.

gloss_en: the line far away where the sky seems to meet the land or sea; also the limit of a person's knowledge or experience.

def_en: an imaginary line around the Earth forming the great circle that is equidistant from the north and south poles
a circle dividing a sphere or other surface into two usually equal and symmetrical parts
examples: Countries near the equator tend to have hot, humid climates all year round.

gloss_en: The imaginary line around the middle of the Earth, equally distant from the North and South Poles.

def_en: the regions to the north of the Arctic Circle centered on the North Pole
a waterproof overshoe that protects shoes from water or snow
examples: Melting ice in the Arctic is a clear sign of a warming climate.

def_en: extremely cold
examples: The expedition faced arctic temperatures that dropped far below freezing.

gloss_en: the region around the North Pole, or relating to that very cold northern region.

def_en: the region around the south pole: Antarctica and surrounding waters
examples: Scientists travel to the Antarctic to study how the ice is changing.

def_zh: 南极的, 南极地带的
examples: The expedition faced fierce winds and freezing antarctic temperatures.

gloss_en: relating to the region around the South Pole; also, the cold region at the far south of the Earth.

def_en: the configuration of a surface and the relations among its man-made and natural features
precise detailed study of the surface features of a region
def_zh: 地志, 地形学, 地势, 局部解剖学
examples: The rugged topography of the region made road construction extremely difficult.

def_zh: 局部解剖, 局部记载

gloss_en: the shape and physical features of an area of land, including its hills, rivers, and other natural or man-made features.

def_en: low level country
examples: The river floods the lowlands every spring, leaving rich soil behind.

def_en: of relatively low or level country
examples: Lowland farmers grow different crops from those in the mountains.

gloss_en: an area of land that is low and fairly flat, especially compared with nearby higher ground

def_en: any large dark enclosed space
a large cave or a large chamber in a cave
def_zh: 巨穴, 洞窟, 深处
examples: The explorers switched on their lamps as they entered the vast underground cavern.

def_en: hollow out as if making a cavern
examples: Over thousands of years, the river had caverned deep hollows into the soft limestone.

gloss_en: a large cave or a large, dark, enclosed space

def_en: a piece of ground having specific characteristics or military potential
def_zh: 地带, 地区, 地形, 领域, 范围
examples: The soldiers were trained to march across rough, mountainous terrain carrying heavy packs.

gloss_en: a stretch of land, especially with regard to its physical features such as how flat, rough, or hilly it is.

def_zh: 地下室, 地下工作者
examples: The blind creatures were true subterraneans, spending their entire lives in the dark tunnels.

def_en: being or operating under the surface of the earth
lying beyond what is openly revealed or avowed (especially being kept in the background or deliberately concealed)
examples: The cave led to a vast subterranean river that no one had ever explored.

gloss_en: existing, happening, or operating below the surface of the earth; underground.

def_en: a land mass (smaller than a continent) that is surrounded by water
a zone or area resembling an island
def_zh: 岛, 岛屿, 孤立地区, 安全岛
examples: The island is a popular tourist destination.
The construction project will island the area from the rest of the city.

gloss_en: a piece of land surrounded by water

examples: Seabirds nest on the rocky islets offshore, where they are safe from most land predators.

gloss_en: A very small island, often rocky and uninhabited.

def_en: a large mass of land projecting into a body of water
def_zh: 半岛, 突出的地方, 伊比利亚半岛
examples: Spain and Portugal together occupy the Iberian Peninsula in south-western Europe.

gloss_en: a long piece of land that sticks out from a larger area of land into the sea and is almost surrounded by water.

def_en: one of the large landmasses of the earth
the European mainland
examples: Antarctica is the coldest and least populated continent on the planet.

def_en: having control over urination and defecation
examples: The doctor was pleased that the patient remained fully continent after the operation.

gloss_en: one of the seven large continuous areas of land on the earth, such as Africa or Asia.

def_en: the limits within which something can be effective
a large tract of grassy open land on which livestock can graze
a series of hills or mountains
a place for shooting (firing or driving) projectiles of various kinds
def_zh: 排, 行, 山脉, 范围, 行列, 射程
examples: The store offers a wide range of products, from electronics to groceries.

def_zh: 排列, 归类于, 使并列, 放牧
平行, 延伸, 漫游
examples: The prices for the concert tickets range from $50 to $200.

def_zh: 量程; 范围; 域; 距离

gloss_en: A set of different things of the same general type, or the amount or extent of variation between limits.

def_en: a science that deals with the history of the earth as recorded in rocks
examples: The unusual geology of the canyon reveals millions of years of the earth's history in its rock layers.

gloss_en: the scientific study of the earth, especially its rocks, soil, and physical history.

def_en: a specialist in geology
def_zh: 地质学家, 地质学者
examples: The geologist examined the rock layers to estimate how old the canyon was.

gloss_en: a scientist who studies the rocks, soil, and physical structure of the earth

def_en: single thickness of usually some homogeneous substance
a relatively thin sheetlike expanse or region lying over or under another
a hen that lays eggs
thin structure composed of a single thickness of cells
def_zh: 层, 产卵鸡, 放置者
examples: The cake had a delicious layer of chocolate frosting between the sponges.
She carefully layered the different fabrics to create a warm quilt.

gloss_en: a thickness of a substance or material covering a surface

def_en: one of several parallel layers of material arranged one on top of another (such as a layer of tissue or cells in an organism or a layer of sedimentary rock)
examples: Each stratum of rock in the cliff records a different period of geological history.

gloss_en: a single layer of one substance, especially one of several parallel layers of rock or tissue.

examples: Granite is an igneous rock that formed as magma cooled slowly deep beneath the Earth's surface.

gloss_en: Rock formed when molten magma or lava cools and solidifies, such as granite or basalt.

def_en: cause to start burning; subject to fire or great heat
def_zh: 点燃, 使灼热, 使兴奋
examples: A single spark was enough to ignite the dry grass and start a huge fire.

gloss_en: to catch fire or to set something on fire; to stir up strong feelings.

examples: Because fossils are preserved in layers, sedimentary rock gives geologists a record of past environments.

gloss_en: Rock formed from layers of sand, mud, or organic material that are compressed and cemented over time, such as sandstone or limestone.

examples: Under enormous pressure deep in the crust, limestone recrystallizes into the metamorphic rock we call marble.
collocations: heat and pressure create metamorphic rock

gloss_en: Rock that has been transformed by intense heat and pressure inside the Earth, such as marble formed from limestone.

def_en: a hard glossy mineral consisting of silicon dioxide in crystal form; present in most rocks (especially sandstone and granite); yellow sand is quartz with iron oxide impurities
examples: The beach sand sparkled because it was composed largely of tiny grains of quartz.

gloss_en: a hard, common mineral made of silicon dioxide, found in many rocks and used in glass, watches, and electronics.

def_en: a hard crystalline metamorphic rock that takes a high polish; used for sculpture and as building material
a small ball of glass that is used in various games
a sculpture carved from marble
def_zh: 大理石, 石弹, 雕刻品
examples: The palace floors were made of gleaming white marble.

def_en: paint or stain like marble
examples: The craftsman marbled the paper with swirling patterns of blue and gold.

def_zh: 大理石的, 冷酷无情的, 有大理石花纹的
examples: They admired the grand marble columns at the entrance to the museum.

gloss_en: a hard stone, often white with coloured lines, that can be polished and is used for statues and buildings.

def_en: plutonic igneous rock having visibly crystalline texture; generally composed of feldspar and mica and quartz
something having the quality of granite (unyielding firmness)
examples: The kitchen counters were made of polished granite, which resists scratches and heat.

gloss_en: a very hard, gray or pink rock used in building and for making monuments.

def_en: solid homogeneous inorganic substances occurring in nature having a definite chemical composition
def_zh: 矿物, 无机物, 苏打水
examples: The region is rich in minerals such as copper, gold, and iron ore.

def_en: relating to minerals
composed of matter other than plant or animal
examples: The spa is famous for its warm mineral springs, which are said to aid recovery.

gloss_en: a naturally occurring solid inorganic substance, such as iron, salt, or quartz, that is found in the earth.

def_en: a mineral that contains metal that is valuable enough to be mined
a monetary subunit in Denmark and Norway and Sweden; 100 ore equal 1 krona
examples: The miners extracted iron ore from deep within the mountain.

gloss_en: rock or earth from which metal can be obtained, valuable enough to be mined.

def_en: the phenomenon of sediment or gravel accumulating
money deposited in a bank or some similar institution
money given as security for an article acquired for temporary use
a payment given as a guarantee that an obligation will be met
def_zh: 存款, 定金, 堆积物
examples: The valley's thick deposits of glacial sediment tell us the ice sheet once extended this far south.

gloss_en: A layer or body of material, such as sediment or minerals, that has accumulated naturally in one place over time.

def_en: a large mass of ice floating at sea; usually broken off of a polar glacier
examples: The ship changed course sharply to avoid a huge iceberg drifting across its path.

gloss_en: a very large mass of ice floating in the sea, most of which lies hidden beneath the surface.

def_en: relating to or derived from a glacier
def_zh: 冰的, 冰川的, 冰川时代的, 冰冷的
examples: The valley was carved out by glacial movement over thousands of years.

gloss_en: relating to or caused by glaciers or ice; also extremely cold or extremely slow.

examples: During the last glacial epoch, ice sheets carved out the basins that later became the Great Lakes.
collocations: the end of the glacial epoch

gloss_en: A period in Earth's history when large ice sheets covered much of the planet; an ice age.

def_en: symptom consisting of a breaking out and becoming visible
(of volcanos) pouring out fumes or lava (or a deposit so formed)
the emergence of a tooth as it breaks through the gum
def_zh: 爆发, 喷出物, 出疹
examples: The volcanic eruption sent a huge cloud of ash high into the sky, grounding all flights.

gloss_en: a sudden, violent outburst, especially of a volcano throwing out lava, ash, and gas.

examples: Geologists classified the dark, glassy sample as a volcanic formed when lava cooled quickly.

def_en: relating to or produced by or consisting of volcanoes
explosively unstable
igneous rock produced by eruption and solidified on or near the earth's surface; rhyolite or andesite or basalt
examples: The island's rich, dark soil is volcanic, which makes it especially good for growing coffee.

gloss_en: relating to, produced by, or resembling a volcano.

def_en: a social scientist who specializes in anthropology
examples: The anthropologist spent two years living with the tribe to study its customs.

gloss_en: a scientist who studies human societies, cultures, and their development

def_en: properties attributable to your ancestry
an event that is a beginning; a first part or stage of subsequent events
the point of intersection of coordinate axes; where the values of the coordinates are all zero
the source of something's existence or from which it derives or is derived
def_zh: 起源, 起因, 出身, 开端
examples: Anthropologists still debate the origin of agriculture and why it arose independently in several regions.

gloss_en: The point or process from which something begins; in archaeology and anthropology, the beginnings of humans, cultures, or practices.

def_en: come into existence; take on form or shape
bring into being
begin a trip at a certain point, as of a plane, train, bus, etc.
def_zh: 创始, 发明, 发起
发源, 发生, 起航
examples: Many popular dishes that we eat today originate from ancient trade routes.

gloss_en: to begin to exist, or to come from a particular place or source

def_en: someone from whom you are descended (but usually more remote than a grandparent)
examples: Her ancestors emigrated from Ireland more than a century ago.

gloss_en: a person from whom one is descended, usually one who lived long ago.

def_en: all of the same or similar kind or nature
def_zh: 同种的, 同质的, 齐性的
examples: The small town had a fairly homogeneous population that shared the same language and customs.

def_zh: 同种的, 纯一的, 同质的, 均匀的

gloss_en: consisting of parts or members that are all the same kind or nature.

examples: The archeologist showed slides of the dig site, pointing out where the team had uncovered pottery shards.
collocations: an archeologist at a dig site

gloss_en: A scientist who studies past human societies by excavating and analyzing physical remains such as tools, buildings, and bones.

def_en: the act of digging
a hole in the ground made by excavating
def_zh: 挖掘, 发掘, 挖掘的洞
examples: The excavation of the ancient city took archaeologists more than a decade to complete.

gloss_en: the act of digging in the ground, especially to uncover buried remains or to prepare a site.

examples: Tourists wandered among the ancient ruins of the Roman city.

def_en: n an irrecoverable state of devastation and destruction
n a ruined building
n the process of becoming dilapidated
n an event that results in destruction
n failure that results in a loss of position or reputation
n destruction achieved by causing something to be wrecked or ruined
v destroy completely; damage irreparably
v destroy or cause to fail
v reduce to bankruptcy
v reduce to ruins
v deprive of virginity
v fall into ruin

gloss_en: the parts of a building or town that remain after it has been badly damaged or destroyed, especially long ago.

def_en: an antiquity that has survived from the distant past
def_zh: 遗物, 遗迹, 纪念物, 遗风, 残留物
examples: The museum displays ancient relics that were discovered in the ruins of the old temple.

gloss_en: an object or custom that has survived from the distant past.

examples: Stone Age communities left behind flint tools that reveal surprisingly sophisticated craftsmanship.
collocations: date back to the Stone Age

gloss_en: The earliest period of human prehistory, when tools and weapons were made mainly of stone, before the use of metal.

examples: Bronze Age traders carried tin across long distances because it was essential for making the alloy.

gloss_en: The period of prehistory following the Stone Age, when people learned to make tools and weapons from bronze, an alloy of copper and tin.

examples: Iron Age farmers could clear land far more efficiently once iron axes became widely available.
collocations: the transition to the Iron Age

gloss_en: The prehistoric period after the Bronze Age, when iron replaced bronze as the main material for tools and weapons.

def_en: the science of matter; the branch of the natural sciences dealing with the composition of substances and their properties and reactions
the chemical composition and properties of a substance or object
the way two individuals relate to each other
examples: She studied chemistry at university before becoming a research scientist.

gloss_en: the scientific study of substances, their properties, and how they react with one another.

def_en: a pseudoscientific forerunner of chemistry in medieval times
examples: Medieval scholars devoted years to the alchemy that promised to turn lead into gold.

def_zh: 炼丹术; 炼金术; 金丹术

gloss_en: a medieval form of chemistry that tried to turn ordinary metals into gold; or a seemingly magical process of transformation

def_en: the real physical matter of which a person or thing consists
material of a particular kind or constitution
a particular kind or species of matter with uniform properties
def_zh: 物质, 实质, 主旨, 资产, 本质, 牢固
examples: In lab, you'll identify each unknown substance by measuring its density and boiling point.

gloss_en: A particular kind of matter or material with definite chemical or physical properties.

examples: Mendeleev left gaps in his periodic table, correctly predicting elements that had not yet been discovered.
collocations: a group/period of the periodic table

gloss_en: The chart that arranges all chemical elements by atomic number so that elements with similar properties fall into the same columns.

def_en: the science of matter and energy and their interactions
the physical properties, phenomena, and laws of something
def_zh: 物理学, 物理过程, 物理现象
examples: She studied physics at university and later became a researcher in nuclear energy.

gloss_en: the scientific study of matter, energy, force, and motion, and the way they relate to each other.

def_en: the branch of physics concerned with the motion of bodies in a frame of reference
def_zh: 机械学, 结构, 力学
examples: Quantum mechanics describes how particles behave at extremely small scales.

gloss_en: the branch of physics that deals with the motion of objects and the forces acting on them.

examples: The concert hall was redesigned because its acoustics muffled the orchestra's higher frequencies.

gloss_en: The branch of physics that studies sound; also the sound-carrying qualities of a room or building.

examples: The optics lecture used myopia to illustrate how a diverging lens corrects where light focuses in the eye.

gloss_en: Nearsightedness: a vision condition in which the eye focuses images in front of the retina, so distant objects appear blurred.

examples: As the meteor entered the atmosphere, its enormous velocity caused it to burn brightly.

def_en: n distance travelled per unit time

gloss_en: the speed of something moving in a particular direction.

def_en: an increase in rate of change
the act of accelerating; increasing the speed
(physics) a rate of increase of velocity
def_zh: 加速, 促进, 加速度
examples: The car's rapid acceleration pushed the passengers back into their seats.

gloss_en: an increase in speed, or the rate at which something speeds up or develops.

def_en: (physics) the force of attraction between all masses in the universe; especially the attraction of the earth's mass for bodies near its surface
a solemn and dignified feeling
examples: Without gravity, the astronauts and their tools simply floated around inside the spacecraft.

gloss_en: the natural force that pulls objects toward each other, especially the force that pulls things toward the earth.

def_en: a stable situation in which forces cancel one another
a sensory system located in structures of the inner ear that registers the orientation of the head
def_zh: 平衡, 平静, 均衡
examples: After the sudden shock, prices gradually returned to equilibrium in the market.

gloss_en: a state of balance in which opposing forces or influences are equal and stable.

def_en: (physics) the tendency of a body to maintain its state of rest or uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force
def_zh: 惯性, 惰性, 迟钝
examples: Bureaucratic inertia meant that the reforms took years to be approved.

gloss_en: a lack of energy or unwillingness to change; in physics, a body's tendency to stay still or keep moving.

def_en: the repetition of a sound resulting from reflection of the sound waves
(Greek mythology) a nymph who was spurned by Narcissus and pined away until only her voice remained
a reply that repeats what has just been said
a reflected television or radio or radar beam
def_zh: 回声, 回音, 回波
examples: Our voices produced a faint echo as they bounced off the far wall of the empty cave.

def_zh: 摹仿, 重复, 反射
examples: The sound of the explosion echoed through the narrow valley for several long seconds.

def_zh: 回显; DOS批处理命令:控制MS-DOS命令是否在屏幕上显示

gloss_en: a sound that is heard again after it bounces off a surface such as a wall or hill.

def_en: an excited state of a stable particle causing a sharp maximum in the probability of absorption of electromagnetic radiation
a vibration of large amplitude produced by a relatively small vibration near the same frequency of vibration as the natural frequency of the resonating system
the quality imparted to voiced speech sounds by the action of the resonating chambers of the throat and mouth and nasal cavities
def_zh: 共鸣, 回声, 反响, 谐振, 共振
examples: The poem still has a powerful resonance for readers who have lost someone they loved.

gloss_en: a deep, full sound that continues; also the quality of having a strong emotional effect or special meaning for people.

examples: Engineers used ultrasonics to detect tiny cracks hidden inside the metal beams.
Bats navigate in the dark by emitting ultrasonic pulses and listening for the echoes.

def_en: s having frequencies above those of audible sound

gloss_en: relating to sound waves with a frequency higher than the upper limit of human hearing.

def_en: a physical phenomenon associated with stationary or moving electrons and protons
energy made available by the flow of electric charge through a conductor
keen and shared excitement
def_zh: 电, 电流, 电学, 热情, 电力供应
examples: The dorms lost electricity for two hours last night, so the exam review session was moved online.

gloss_en: A form of energy carried by the flow of charged particles, used to power lights, machines, and electronic devices.

examples: The professor rubbed a balloon on wool to show how static electricity makes it cling to the wall.

gloss_en: An electric charge that builds up on the surface of an object, often through friction, rather than flowing as a current.

examples: When you close the circuit, an electric current flows through the wire and lights the bulb.

gloss_en: The flow of electric charge, usually electrons, through a conductor such as a wire.

def_en: attraction for iron; associated with electric currents as well as magnets; characterized by fields of force
the branch of science that studies magnetism
def_zh: 磁性, 吸引力, 磁学, 催眠术
examples: The experiment demonstrated how electric currents can produce magnetism.

gloss_en: the physical property by which certain materials attract or repel one another; also, a powerful personal charm that attracts others.

examples: Rubber is a good insulator, which is why electrical wires are coated with it for safety.

gloss_en: A material, such as rubber or glass, that does not allow electricity (or heat) to pass through it easily.

def_en: protect from heat, cold, or noise by surrounding with insulating material
examples: They insulated the roof to keep the house warm during the winter.

gloss_en: to cover or surround something with a material that stops heat, cold, sound, or electricity from passing through.

def_en: a substance as germanium or silicon whose electrical conductivity is intermediate between that of a metal and an insulator; its conductivity increases with temperature and in the presence of impurities
examples: Modern computer chips are built from tiny layers of semiconductor material.

gloss_en: a material, such as silicon, whose ability to conduct electricity is between that of a metal and an insulator, widely used in electronic devices.

examples: The invention of the integrated circuit allowed computers to shrink from room-sized machines to desktop devices.

gloss_en: A tiny electronic chip containing many interconnected circuit components on a single piece of semiconductor material.

def_en: a triangular wooden float attached to the end of a log line
a piece of dried bovine dung
a thin crisp slice of potato fried in deep fat
a small disk-shaped counter used to represent money when gambling
def_zh: 屑片, 薄片, 碎片
examples: Be careful not to get a chip in your new glasses.

def_zh: 削, 切, 削成碎片, 使摔倒, 凿
examples: The hammer accidentally chipped the corner of the tile.

def_zh: 孔屑; 组件; 晶片; 芯片

gloss_en: a small piece of something that has broken off

examples: Early radios relied on electron tubes, which were bulky, fragile, and consumed a lot of power.

gloss_en: A sealed glass or metal device that controls the flow of electrons in a vacuum, used in early electronics before transistors.

examples: ENIAC, one of the first computers, used thousands of vacuum tubes and filled an entire room.

gloss_en: An electron tube from which the air has been removed, used to amplify or switch electric signals in early computers and radios.

def_en: a biologist who studies the relation between organisms and their environment
examples: The ecologist warned that draining the wetland would destroy many species' habitats.

gloss_en: a scientist who studies the relationships between living things and their environment.

examples: After the spring rains, the desert seems to effloresce overnight, carpeting the ground with wildflowers.

gloss_en: To burst into flower; figuratively, to develop and flourish, as when an ecosystem or population blooms rapidly.

examples: The demonstration showed how a tungsten filament incandesces when an electric current heats it.

gloss_en: To glow with heat; to become white-hot or give off light because of high temperature.

examples: The morning fog over the wetland evanesces as soon as the sun warms the surface.

gloss_en: To fade away gradually and disappear, like mist dissolving into the air.

examples: Acid rain from coal-burning power plants killed fish populations in lakes hundreds of miles downwind.

gloss_en: Rain made acidic by air pollutants such as sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides, which damages forests, lakes, and buildings.

examples: Without the natural greenhouse effect, Earth's average temperature would be far below freezing.
collocations: the greenhouse effect warms the planet

gloss_en: The warming of the Earth's surface caused by atmospheric gases, such as carbon dioxide, that trap heat radiated from the planet.

examples: Carbon dioxide absorbs the infrared radiation the Earth emits, which is why it acts as a greenhouse gas.

gloss_en: Invisible electromagnetic radiation with wavelengths longer than visible light, felt as heat and trapped by greenhouse gases.

def_en: a particular environment or surrounding influence
the mass of air surrounding the Earth
the weather or climate at some place
the envelope of gases surrounding any celestial body
def_zh: 大气, 空气, 气氛
examples: The lecture explained how volcanic eruptions can inject enough ash into the atmosphere to cool the climate temporarily.

gloss_en: The layer of gases surrounding the Earth, which supports life, drives weather, and regulates the planet's temperature.

examples: Stricter pollution control regulations forced factories to install scrubbers on their smokestacks.

gloss_en: Measures and technologies used to prevent or reduce the release of harmful substances into the environment.

def_en: waste matter that contaminates the water or air or soil
examples: Cars release a range of pollutants that lower the air quality in crowded cities.

gloss_en: a substance that makes air, water, or soil dirty and harmful.

def_en: injurious to physical or mental health
def_zh: 有害的, 有毒的, 使道德败坏的
examples: The factory released noxious fumes that residents said made them cough and dizzy.

gloss_en: harmful or poisonous to health or the environment.

examples: The novelist uses the unspoiled wilderness as a symbol of innocence lost to industrial development.

gloss_en: The state of being blameless or morally pure; freedom from guilt or from knowledge of harm.

def_zh: 可憎的, 使人不愉快的, 令人讨厌的
examples: His obnoxious remarks made everyone at the dinner uncomfortable.

def_en: s causing disapproval or protest

gloss_en: extremely unpleasant or offensive, especially in a way that annoys other people.

examples: Exhaust fumes from the highway have measurably degraded air quality in the neighborhoods beside it.

gloss_en: Harmful or unpleasant gases or smoke given off by chemicals, vehicles, or burning materials.

examples: The city's landfill is nearly full, so officials are pushing recycling to cut the volume of solid waste.

gloss_en: Discarded solid materials such as household garbage and industrial refuse, as opposed to sewage or gas emissions.

def_en: waste matter carried away in sewers or drains
examples: Untreated sewage was flowing into the sea, causing serious pollution along the coast.

gloss_en: the waste matter and dirty water carried away from homes and buildings through drains and sewers.

examples: The field trip to the treatment plant showed each stage of sewage purification, from filtering to bacterial digestion.

gloss_en: The process of cleaning wastewater by removing contaminants so it can be safely returned to the environment.

examples: Rapid urban growth outpaced the city's sewage disposal system, contaminating the river downstream.

gloss_en: The handling and getting rid of wastewater and human waste, usually through treatment systems or septic tanks.

def_en: a specialist who studies processes in the earth's atmosphere that cause weather conditions
examples: The meteorologist warned that a severe storm would reach the coast by evening.

gloss_en: a scientist who studies the atmosphere and weather, often in order to forecast it.

def_zh: 预想, 预测, 预报
examples: According to the weather forecast, heavy rain is expected tomorrow afternoon.

def_en: predict in advance
def_zh: 预想, 预测, 预报
examples: Analysts forecast that the economy will grow slowly over the next year.

gloss_en: a statement about what is likely to happen in the future, based on available information.

def_en: the weather in some location averaged over some long period of time
the prevailing psychological state
def_zh: 气候, 社会趋势, 气候区
examples: The company is working to create a more positive and inclusive work climate.

gloss_en: the prevailing attitudes or feelings of a person or group

def_en: lose or cause to lose liquid by vaporization leaving a more concentrated residue
cause to change into a vapor
change into a vapor
def_zh: 蒸发, 失去水分, 消失
使蒸发, 使脱水, 使消失
examples: The water will evaporate quickly in the hot desert sun.

gloss_en: to change from a liquid to a gas or vapor

def_en: a slight wetness
examples: The old cellar smelled of damp after weeks of heavy rain.

def_en: restrain or discourage
def_zh: 使潮湿, 使阻尼, 抑止
examples: She tried to damp the fire's noise by closing the heavy iron door.

def_en: slightly wet
examples: Wipe the surface with a damp cloth to remove the dust.

gloss_en: slightly wet, often in an unpleasant way; also, slight wetness in the air or on a surface.

def_en: wetness in the atmosphere
def_zh: 湿气, 潮湿, 湿度
examples: The high humidity in the tropics made the afternoon feel unbearably hot.

gloss_en: the amount of water vapor in the air, making conditions feel damp

def_zh: 饱和化合物, 饱和脂肪酸
examples: Nutritionists warn that a diet high in saturates can raise the risk of heart disease.

def_en: cause (a chemical compound, vapour, solution, magnetic material) to unite with the greatest possible amount of another substance
def_zh: 使渗透, 浸透, 使充满, 使饱和
examples: Heavy advertising has saturated the market with nearly identical products.

def_zh: 浸透的, 饱和度高的, 深颜色的
examples: The soil was completely saturate after a week of relentless rain.

gloss_en: to make something completely wet, or to fill it so full that no more can be added.

def_en: a heavy rain
examples: The picnic was cut short by a sudden downpour that soaked everyone.

gloss_en: a heavy and sudden fall of rain.

def_en: a plumbing fixture that sprays water over you
washing yourself by standing upright under water sprayed from a nozzle
a brief period of precipitation
a sudden downpour (as of tears or sparks etc) likened to a rain shower
def_zh: 阵雨, 淋浴, 一阵, 展出者, 显示者
examples: A light shower passed over the town, leaving the streets glistening in the sun.
She showered quickly and rushed out to catch the early bus.

gloss_en: a short period of rain; also a device that sprays water for washing the body, or the act of washing under it.

def_en: droplets of water vapor suspended in the air near the ground
an atmosphere in which visibility is reduced because of a cloud of some substance
def_zh: 雾, 迷惑, (割后的)最生草, 苔藓
examples: Dense fog forced the airport to cancel dozens of morning flights.
Steam from the shower quickly fogged the bathroom mirror.

gloss_en: a thick cloud of tiny water droplets near the ground that makes it hard to see.

def_en: air pollution by a mixture of smoke and fog
examples: Thick smog hung over the city, making it hard to see the tall buildings.

gloss_en: a form of air pollution that looks like a mixture of smoke and fog, common over large cities.

def_en: precipitation of ice pellets when there are strong rising air currents
many objects thrown forcefully through the air
enthusiastic greeting
def_zh: 冰雹, 致敬, 欢呼, 招呼
examples: The sudden hail battered the crops and dented several parked cars.

def_en: be a native of
def_zh: 向...欢呼, 致敬, 招呼, 使象下雹样落下
examples: Critics hailed the film as one of the finest of the decade.
Hail, noble king! the crowd cried as the ruler passed through the gates.

gloss_en: small balls of ice that fall from the sky like rain during a storm.

def_en: a slide of large masses of snow and ice and mud down a mountain
a sudden appearance of an overwhelming number of things
def_zh: 雪崩, 山崩, 大量
examples: The skiers were warned that heavy snowfall had raised the risk of an avalanche.

def_en: gather into a huge mass and roll down a mountain, of snow
examples: As spring arrived, huge slabs of snow began to avalanche down the steep slopes.

gloss_en: a large mass of snow, ice, and rock sliding rapidly down a mountain; also a sudden flood of something.

def_en: a strong wind moving 45-90 knots; force 7 to 10 on Beaufort scale
def_zh: 狂风, 大风, 定期租金
examples: A fierce gale swept across the coast, tearing branches from the trees.

gloss_en: a very strong wind.

examples: On hot afternoons, whirlwinds of dust often spin across the dry valley floor as heated air rises.

gloss_en: A small rotating column of air, such as a dust devil, that moves across land picking up dust and debris.

def_en: confused movement
the shape of something rotating rapidly
def_zh: 回旋, 旋转, 急走, 眩晕
examples: The dancers moved in a dizzying whirl of colour across the stage.

def_en: cause to spin
fly around
def_zh: 使旋转, 使回旋, 卷走
回旋, 旋转, 急走, 发晕
examples: The leaves whirled through the air as the sudden gust of wind swept the street.

gloss_en: to move or spin round and round very quickly.

def_en: a science (or group of related sciences) dealing with the logic of quantity and shape and arrangement
examples: A firm grasp of mathematics is essential for anyone who wants to study engineering.

gloss_en: the study of numbers, quantities, shapes, and the relationships between them.

examples: The lecture profiled a nineteenth-century mathematician whose work on geometry was ignored for decades.

gloss_en: A person who studies or is an expert in mathematics.

def_en: the branch of pure mathematics dealing with the theory of numerical calculations
examples: The children practiced simple arithmetic by adding up the prices in the shop.

gloss_en: the branch of mathematics that deals with adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing numbers.

def_en: the procedure of calculating; determining something by mathematical or logical methods
problem solving that involves numbers or quantities
planning something carefully and intentionally
def_zh: 计算, 考虑, 计算的结果
examples: A quick calculation showed that the trip would cost far more than they had planned.

gloss_en: the process of working something out using mathematics; the result of doing this.

def_en: an expert at calculation (or at operating calculating machines)
a small machine that is used for mathematical calculations
examples: Students are not allowed to use a calculator during the first part of the maths exam.

gloss_en: a small electronic device used for doing mathematical calculations

def_en: material such as sand or gravel used with cement and water to make concrete, mortar, or plaster
a sum total of many heterogenous things taken together
def_zh: 合计, 总计, 聚集体
examples: In aggregate, the small donations added up to more than a million dollars.

def_en: amount in the aggregate to
gather in a mass, sum, or whole
def_zh: 聚集, 集合, 合计达
examples: The website aggregates news from hundreds of sources into a single feed.
The aggregate demand for housing rose sharply after interest rates fell.

gloss_en: a total formed by combining several separate amounts or things; as a verb, to gather into a total; as an adjective, formed by adding parts together.

def_en: a component of a mixture that has been separated by a fractional process
a small part or item forming a piece of a whole
the quotient of two rational numbers
def_zh: 小部分, 破片, 分数
examples: The new laptop costs only a fraction of the price of the older professional model.
collocations: for a fraction of a second

def_zh: 部分, 成分, 分散

gloss_en: a small part or amount of something; also, a number that is not a whole number, such as 1/2.

examples: The professor pointed out that pi's digits continue infinitely without ever repeating in a pattern.

gloss_en: Any of the numerals from 0 to 9; a single position in a written number.

def_en: the mathematics of generalized arithmetical operations
examples: Students learn to solve for an unknown value when they first study algebra.

gloss_en: A branch of mathematics in which letters and symbols are used to represent numbers in equations and formulas.

def_en: a mathematical statement that two expressions are equal
the act of regarding as equal
def_zh: 相等, 等式, 平衡
examples: Students were asked to solve the equation and check their answers.

def_zh: 方程式; 等式; 反应式; 公式

gloss_en: a mathematical statement showing that two amounts or expressions are equal.

def_en: a group of symbols that make a mathematical statement
a conventionalized statement expressing some fundamental principle
a representation of a substance using symbols for its constituent elements
a liquid food for infants
def_zh: 客套语, 公式, 准则
examples: You won't need to memorize every formula; the important ones will be printed on the exam sheet.
collocations: the formula for the area of a circle

gloss_en: A mathematical rule or relationship expressed in symbols, used to calculate a value.

def_en: a four-sided polygon
examples: A square is a special kind of quadrilateral in which all four sides are equal.

def_en: having four sides
examples: The architect designed the courtyard with a quadrilateral floor plan enclosed by four walls.

gloss_en: a flat shape with four straight sides and four angles.

def_en: (geometry) a plane rectangle with four equal sides and four right angles; a four-sided regular polygon
the product of two equal terms
something approximating the shape of a square
someone who doesn't understand what is going on
def_zh: 正方形, 街区, 广场, 平方, 直角尺
examples: To find the area of a square, you simply multiply the length of one side by itself.

gloss_en: A flat shape with four equal sides and four right angles; also the result of multiplying a number by itself.

def_en: a three-sided polygon
something approximating the shape of a triangle
any of various triangular drafting instruments used to draw straight lines at specified angles
a percussion instrument consisting of a metal bar bent in the shape of an open triangle
def_zh: 三角形, 三个一组, 三角关系
examples: The students learned how to calculate the area of a triangle.

gloss_en: a flat shape with three straight sides and three angles

def_en: the space between two lines or planes that intersect; the inclination of one line to another; measured in degrees or radians
a member of a Germanic people who conquered England and merged with the Saxons and Jutes to become Anglo-Saxons
examples: The interior angles of any triangle always add up to exactly 180 degrees.

gloss_en: The figure formed by two lines meeting at a point, measured in degrees.

def_en: a particular geographical region of indefinite boundary (usually serving some special purpose or distinguished by its people or culture or geography)
a subject of study
a part of an animal that has a special function or is supplied by a given artery or nerve
a part of a structure having some specific characteristic or function
def_zh: 区域, 面积, 范围, 空地
examples: The area around the park is very quiet and safe.

gloss_en: a particular part of a country, city, or landscape

def_en: the pure mathematics of points and lines and curves and surfaces
examples: In geometry class, we learned how to calculate the area of a triangle.

gloss_en: the branch of mathematics concerned with points, lines, shapes, and space.

def_en: characterized by simple geometric forms in design and decoration
of or relating to or determined by geometry
def_zh: 几何学的, 几何学上的, 几何学图形的
examples: The tiles were arranged in a bold geometric pattern of triangles and squares.

gloss_en: relating to geometry, or made up of regular shapes and lines such as circles, squares, and triangles.

def_en: a parallelogram with four right angles
examples: The gardener marked out a neat rectangle for the new vegetable bed.

gloss_en: a flat shape with four straight sides and four right angles, where opposite sides are equal in length.

def_en: a closed plane figure bounded by straight sides
examples: A hexagon is a polygon with six equal sides and six equal angles.

gloss_en: a flat shape with three or more straight sides, such as a triangle or hexagon.

examples: The word 'square' is polysemous: in one lecture it may mean a shape, in another a number multiplied by itself.

gloss_en: Having multiple related meanings, as a single word that can be understood in several senses depending on context.

def_en: a hexahedron with six equal squares as faces
the product of three equal terms
any of several tropical American woody plants of the genus Lonchocarpus whose roots are used locally as a fish poison and commercially as a source of rotenone
a block in the (approximate) shape of a cube
examples: She dropped a single cube of ice into her glass of water.

gloss_en: a solid or three-dimensional shape with six equal square sides.

def_en: a particular environment or walk of life
any spherically shaped artifact
the geographical area in which one nation is very influential
a solid figure bounded by a spherical surface (including the space it encloses)
def_zh: 球, 球面, 球体, 天体, 地球仪, 范围
examples: The Earth is not a perfect sphere but is slightly flattened at the poles.

def_zh: 包围, 使成球体, 放入球内
examples: In the old poem, mist seemed to sphere the moon in a soft glowing ring.

gloss_en: a round, ball-shaped object; also, a particular area of activity or knowledge.

def_en: ellipse in which the two axes are of equal length; a plane curve generated by one point moving at a constant distance from a fixed point
something approximating the shape of a circle
a curved section or tier of seats in a hall or theater or opera house; usually the first tier above the orchestra
any circular or rotating mechanism
def_zh: 圆周, 社交圈, 循环, 范围
examples: The children sat in a circle on the floor during story time.
The vultures began to circle above the stranded animal.

gloss_en: A round plane figure whose boundary (the circumference) consists of points equidistant from a fixed central point.

examples: Kepler showed that each planet moves around the sun in an ellipse rather than a perfect circle.

gloss_en: A regular oval shape, the closed curve traced when the sum of distances from two fixed points is constant; the shape of planetary orbits.

def_en: any cone-shaped artifact
a shape whose base is a circle and whose sides taper up to a point
cone-shaped mass of ovule- or spore-bearing scales or bracts
a visual receptor cell in the retina that is sensitive to bright light and to color
examples: Orange traffic cones were placed along the road to warn drivers about the repairs ahead.
Workers coned off the damaged lane so that cars could pass safely on the other side.

gloss_en: a solid or hollow shape with a round flat base that narrows to a point at the top.

def_en: the size of something as given by the distance around it
the boundary line encompassing an area or object
the length of the closed curve of a circle
def_zh: 圆周, 周缘, 胸围
examples: The engineers measured the circumference of the pipe before ordering the fittings.

gloss_en: the distance around the edge of a circle or other round object.

def_en: the length of a straight line passing through the center of a circle and connecting two points on the circumference
a straight line connecting the center of a circle with two points on its perimeter (or the center of a sphere with two points on its surface)
examples: The old tree trunk measured almost a metre in diameter.

gloss_en: a straight line passing through the centre of a circle from one side to the other; its length

def_en: the length of a line segment between the center and circumference of a circle or sphere
a straight line from the center to the perimeter of a circle (or from the center to the surface of a sphere)
a circular region whose area is indicated by the length of its radius
the outer and slightly shorter of the two bones of the human forearm
def_zh: 半径, 范围, 辐射光线
examples: The shop delivers free of charge to any address within a five-mile radius.

gloss_en: the distance from the center of a circle to its edge; also, a circular area measured out from a central point.

examples: You'll need first-semester calculus before enrolling in the introductory physics sequence.

gloss_en: The branch of mathematics dealing with rates of change and accumulation, using derivatives and integrals.

examples: Differential calculus lets physicists describe exactly how a falling object's speed changes at every instant.

gloss_en: The branch of calculus concerned with derivatives, which measure how quantities change from moment to moment.

examples: In today's lecture we'll see how integral calculus lets us compute the total area under a curve rather than just its slope at a point.

gloss_en: The branch of mathematics concerned with integrals, used to calculate areas, volumes, and accumulated quantities.

def_en: (mathematics) a mathematical relation such that each element of a given set (the domain of the function) is associated with an element of another set (the range of the function)
what something is used for
the actions and activities assigned to or required or expected of a person or group
a relation such that one thing is dependent on another
def_zh: 官能, 职务, 功能, 函数
examples: The function was attended by many important guests and dignitaries.

def_zh: 活动, 运行, 行使职责
examples: The new software will function properly and meet all the requirements.

gloss_en: a formal or official social event

def_en: a measure of how likely it is that some event will occur; a number expressing the ratio of favorable cases to the whole number of cases possible
the quality of being probable; a probable event or the most probable event
def_zh: 可能性, 或然率, 几率, 概率
examples: There is a high probability that the experiment will need to be repeated.

gloss_en: how likely it is that something will happen, often expressed as a number between 0 and 1.

def_en: a branch of applied mathematics concerned with the collection and interpretation of quantitative data and the use of probability theory to estimate population parameters
examples: Recent statistics show that unemployment has fallen for the third year in a row.

gloss_en: the science of collecting and analysing numerical data; also, a set of such numbers.

def_en: any animal lacking a backbone or notochord; the term is not used as a scientific classification
def_zh: 无脊椎动物, 无骨气的人
examples: Insects, spiders, and snails are all invertebrates, together making up the vast majority of animal species.

def_en: lacking a backbone or spinal column
def_zh: 无脊椎的, 无骨气的
examples: The pond teemed with invertebrate life, from tiny water fleas to darting beetles.

gloss_en: an animal that has no backbone, such as an insect, worm, or jellyfish; lacking a backbone.

def_en: a plant that lives in or on water
def_zh: 水生动物, 水生植物
examples: The pond was home to frogs, fish, and many other aquatics.

def_en: relating to or consisting of or being in water
operating or living or growing in water
def_zh: 水生的, 水中的, 水上的
examples: Frogs are aquatic animals that spend much of their early life in water.

gloss_en: living or growing in, on, or near water; relating to water.

def_en: small air-breathing arthropod
examples: Bees and ants are among the most social insects found in nature.

gloss_en: a small animal with six legs, a body divided into three parts, and usually two pairs of wings.

examples: Bears are omnivorous, so their diet shifts from berries in summer to fish and small mammals as the seasons change.

gloss_en: Describing an animal that eats both plants and other animals.

def_en: feeding only on plants
examples: Herbivorous dinosaurs such as the diplodocus stripped leaves from tall trees with their long necks.

gloss_en: feeding only on plants.

def_en: relating to or characteristic of carnivores
(used of plants as well as animals) feeding on animals
examples: Lions and wolves are carnivorous animals that rely on hunting for their food.

gloss_en: feeding mainly on the flesh of other animals.

def_en: relatively small placental mammals having a single pair of constantly growing incisor teeth specialized for gnawing
examples: Owls play an important role in controlling the rodent population on farmland.
Traps and poison are common methods of rodent control in old warehouses.

gloss_en: a small mammal such as a rat, mouse, or squirrel that has strong, continuously growing front teeth for gnawing.

examples: The professor explained that a mollusk like the clam filters food particles directly from the surrounding seawater.

gloss_en: A soft-bodied invertebrate animal, such as a snail, clam, or octopus, often protected by a shell.

def_en: make less rigid or softer
def_zh: 平息, 缓和, 减轻, 使变软
examples: The manager tried to mollify the angry customer by offering a full refund.

gloss_en: to make someone feel less angry or upset; to calm someone down.

def_zh: 熔化, 熔化物, 溶解
examples: The spring melt swelled the mountain rivers and flooded the fields in the valley below.

def_en: reduce or cause to be reduced from a solid to a liquid state, usually by heating
lose its distinct outline or shape; blend gradually
become less intense and fade away gradually
def_zh: (使)熔化, (使)溶解, (使)消散, (使)变软
examples: The ice cream began to melt in the sun before the children could finish eating it.

gloss_en: to change or make something change from a solid into a liquid, usually by heating it.

def_en: a group of cattle or sheep or other domestic mammals all of the same kind that are herded by humans
a group of wild mammals of one species that remain together: antelope or elephants or seals or whales or zebra
def_zh: 兽群, 人群, 牧人
examples: A herd of elephants moved slowly across the dry African plain.

def_en: cause to herd, drive, or crowd together
move together, like a herd
def_zh: 群集, 使成群, 放牧
examples: The guides herded the tourists onto the bus before it began to rain.

gloss_en: a large group of animals of one kind that live and feed together; as a verb, to gather or drive animals or people into a group.

def_en: a group of many things in the air or on the ground
def_zh: 群, 大群, 蜂群, 分蜂群
examples: A swarm of bees settled on the branch of the apple tree in the garden.

def_zh: 分群, 群集, 聚集一块, 云集, 攀
examples: Fans swarmed around the stage as soon as the band appeared.

gloss_en: a large group of insects, especially bees, moving together; also (as a verb) to move somewhere in a large crowd.

def_en: a church congregation guided by a pastor
a group of birds
a group of sheep or goats
def_zh: 兽群, 鸟群, 一群人, 大量, 棉束
examples: A flock of geese flew overhead in a perfect V formation.

def_en: move as a crowd or in a group
examples: Tourists flock to the coastal town every summer to enjoy its beaches.

gloss_en: a group of birds or sheep, or a large crowd of people; also, to gather or move somewhere in large numbers

def_zh: 一夫一妻的，一雄一雌的
examples: Many swan species are monogamous, with a single pair returning to the same nesting site year after year.

gloss_en: Describing an animal that has only one mate, often for a breeding season or for life.

def_zh: 一夫多妻的，一雄多雌的
examples: In polygamous species such as elephant seals, a single dominant male may control a large group of females on the beach.

gloss_en: Describing an animal in which one male mates with several females.

def_zh: 一妻多夫的，一雌多雄的
examples: The jacana is a polyandrous bird, and it is the male who is left to incubate the eggs of multiple partners.

gloss_en: Describing an animal in which one female mates with several males.

examples: Because mammals are homeotherms, they must burn a great deal of energy to keep their body temperature constant in cold climates.

gloss_en: An animal that maintains a stable internal body temperature regardless of its environment; a warm-blooded animal.

def_en: a special variety of domesticated animals within a species
a special type
examples: This breed of dog is known for being gentle and patient with children.

def_en: copulate with a female, used especially of horses
cause to procreate (animals)
def_zh: 养育, 引起, 饲养, 繁殖
examples: The farm breeds horses for both racing and riding.

gloss_en: a particular type within a species of animal or plant; also to keep animals for the purpose of producing young.

def_en: a minute life form (especially a disease-causing bacterium); the term is not in technical use
examples: Certain soil microbes break down dead plants and return nutrients to the earth.

gloss_en: an extremely small living thing, such as a bacterium, that can only be seen with a microscope.

def_en: an animal or plant that lives in or on a host (another animal or plant); it obtains nourishment from the host without benefiting or killing the host
def_zh: 寄生生物, 寄生虫, 食客
examples: The vet found that the dog was covered in tiny parasites feeding on its blood.

gloss_en: an animal or plant that lives on or inside another living thing and gets its food from it.

examples: A visiting botanist will lead the field trip and show us how to identify native flowering plants along the trail.

gloss_en: A scientist who studies plants.

def_en: the transparent covering of an aircraft cockpit
the umbrellalike part of a parachute that fills with air
a covering (usually of cloth) that serves as a roof to shelter an area from the weather
def_zh: 天篷, 遮篷, 苍穹
examples: Sunlight filtered softly through the dense canopy of the rainforest.

def_en: cover with a canopy
examples: Tall trees canopied the narrow path, shading the walkers from the midday heat.

gloss_en: a cover fixed above something to provide shelter or shade; also the uppermost layer of leaves and branches in a forest.

examples: In autumn the dense foliage of the maples turns a brilliant shade of red and gold.

def_en: n the main organ of photosynthesis and transpiration in higher plants
n (architecture) leaf-like architectural ornament

gloss_en: the leaves of a plant or tree, considered as a group.

def_en: part of a compound leaf
examples: Volunteers handed out leaflets about the new recycling scheme outside the station.

gloss_en: a printed sheet of paper that gives information or advertises something, usually given out free.

examples: Notice how the leafstalk twists so that each leaf can turn toward the sunlight without shading the others.

gloss_en: The slender stalk that attaches a leaf blade to a plant's stem; the petiole.

def_en: especially a leaf of grass or the broad portion of a leaf as distinct from the petiole
a dashing young man
something long and thin resembling a blade of grass
a cut of beef from the shoulder blade
def_zh: 叶片, 刀锋, 刀口, 剑
examples: He sharpened the blade of the knife until it could slice through paper.

def_zh: 页[片], 叶片, 刀片, 刀刃, 刀口

gloss_en: the flat, sharp-edged part of a knife, sword, or tool used for cutting; also a long thin leaf of grass.

def_en: a new branch
the act of shooting at targets
def_zh: 射击, 狩猎, 芽, 射伤, 发射, 发芽, 急流, 推力, 摄影, 急送, 滑运道, 浪费
examples: The photographer waited for the perfect shoot to capture the sunset.

def_en: hit with a missile from a weapon
kill by firing a missile
def_zh: 射击, 射中, 损毁, 拍摄, 喷出, 投射, 挥出, 飞速行进, 挥霍, 给...注射
射出, 射击, 发出, 拍电影, 射门, 发芽
examples: The hunter will shoot the deer if it comes close enough.

gloss_en: to send a bullet or other projectile from a gun

def_en: a field of cultivated and mowed grass
def_zh: 草地, 草坪, 上等细麻布
examples: Every Sunday he mowed the lawn and trimmed the hedges around the house.

gloss_en: an area of ground covered with short, regularly cut grass, especially in a garden or park.

examples: In spring the meadow is filled with wildflowers and the hum of bees.

def_en: n a field where grass or alfalfa are grown to be made into hay

a United States 44-gun frigate that was one of the first three naval ships built by the United States; it won brilliant victories over British frigates during the War of 1812 and is without doubt the most famous ship in the history of the United States Navy; it has been rebuilt and is anchored in the Charlestown Navy Yard in Boston
