def_en: a coming into being

the first book of the Old Testament: tells of Creation; Adam and Eve; the Fall of Man; Cain and Abel; Noah and the flood; God's covenant with Abraham; Abraham and Isaac; Jacob and Esau; Joseph and his brothers
def_zh: 发生, 起源, 创世记
examples: The genesis of the idea can be traced to a chance conversation years earlier.

gloss_en: the origin or beginning of something.

examples: The peaceful protest quickly degenerated into a violent riot.
Critics dismissed the film as a degenerate imitation of earlier classics.

def_en: n a person whose behavior deviates from what is acceptable especially in sexual behavior
v grow worse
s unrestrained by convention or morality

gloss_en: to become worse in quality or condition.

examples: The professor recalled how the poorly planned experiment turned into a public fiasco.

gloss_en: a complete and often embarrassing failure

examples: The history lecture discussed how eugenics was misused in the early twentieth century to justify harmful policies.

gloss_en: the discredited belief in improving the human population by controlled selective breeding

def_en: call forth
examples: A fair and open process can engender trust between management and workers.

gloss_en: to cause a feeling, situation, or condition to develop or come into being.

examples: The anthropology lecture traced the tribe's traditions back to a single legendary primogenitor.

gloss_en: the earliest ancestor or original founder of a family or line

def_zh: 子孙, 后裔, 成果
examples: The prize-winning bull's progeny were sold to farms across the country.

def_en: n the immediate descendants of a person

gloss_en: the children or descendants of a person, animal, or plant

def_zh: 本土的, 国产的, 固有的
examples: The kangaroo is indigenous to Australia and is found nowhere else in the wild.

def_en: s originating where it is found

gloss_en: originating naturally in a particular place; native to a region.

def_en: a general kind of something
(biology) taxonomic group containing one or more species
examples: The lion and the tiger belong to the same genus, Panthera.

gloss_en: a group of related plants or animals in biological classification, ranking above species.

def_en: suitable to your needs
(used of plants) capable of cross-fertilization or of being grafted
def_zh: 协调的, 一致的, 适意的, 趣味相同的
examples: We spent the evening in the congenial company of old friends.

gloss_en: pleasant and friendly; suited to one's taste or nature.

def_en: consisting of elements that are not of the same kind or nature
def_zh: 异种的, 异质的, 由不同成分形成的
examples: The study drew on a heterogeneous group of participants from many backgrounds and age ranges.

def_zh: 不均匀的, 多相的, 异质的

gloss_en: Made up of parts or elements that are different in kind; varied and not uniform.

examples: The psychology professor noted that under stress some patients regress to childlike behavior.

gloss_en: to return to an earlier, usually worse or less developed, condition

def_en: lose clarity or turn aside especially from the main subject of attention or course of argument in writing, thinking, or speaking
def_zh: 走向岔道, 离题, 扯到枝节上
examples: The lecturer tended to digress into personal anecdotes before returning to the topic.

gloss_en: to move away from the main subject when speaking or writing.

def_en: (of animals) tending to form a group with others of the same species
instinctively or temperamentally seeking and enjoying the company of others
(of plants) growing in groups that are close together
examples: A naturally gregarious person, he thrived at parties and made new friends effortlessly.

gloss_en: (of a person) fond of company and enjoying being with others; (of animals) living in groups.

def_en: a tiny grain
def_zh: 小粒, 细粒, 颗粒
examples: The fertilizer comes as small granules that dissolve slowly in the soil.

gloss_en: a small, hard piece of something; a tiny grain

examples: The soil in this region is coarse and granular, allowing water to drain away quickly.

def_en: s composed of or covered with particles resembling meal in texture or consistency
a having a granular structure like that of chondrites

gloss_en: made of or resembling many small grains or particles.

def_en: death of a person
a place for the burial of a corpse (especially beneath the ground and marked by a tombstone)
def_zh: 墓穴, 坟墓, 终结, 死亡
examples: Every spring, the family visits the grave of their grandfather to lay fresh flowers.
The names of the fallen soldiers were graved deep into the stone monument.

def_en: dignified and somber in manner or character and committed to keeping promises
of great gravity or crucial import; requiring serious thought
def_zh: 庄重的, 严肃的, 重大的, 低沉的
examples: The doctors were worried because the patient was in a grave condition after the accident.

gloss_en: a hole dug in the ground where a dead person is buried, or the place of burial itself.

def_zh: 使恶化, 使更严重, 加重
examples: Cutting the education budget will only aggravate the shortage of qualified teachers.

def_en: v make worse
v exasperate or irritate

gloss_en: to make a bad situation, problem, or illness worse.

def_en: find repugnant
def_zh: 憎恶, 痛恨, 回避, 拒绝
examples: Most citizens abhor the use of violence to settle political disputes.

gloss_en: to hate or dislike something very strongly, often for moral reasons

def_zh: 可怕的, 遭透的, 极讨厌的
examples: The survivors described the horrible conditions they had endured after the earthquake.

def_en: s provoking horror

gloss_en: extremely unpleasant, bad, or causing fear and shock.

def_en: great merriment
examples: The comedian's clumsy entrance was greeted with hilarity from the audience.

gloss_en: great amusement and loud laughter.

def_en: making lively and cheerful
def_zh: 令人喜欢的, 使人愉快的, 爽快的
examples: Skiing down the steep slope for the first time was an exhilarating experience.

gloss_en: making you feel very happy, excited, and full of energy.

def_en: marked by or causing boisterous merriment or convulsive laughter
def_zh: 喜不自禁的, 欢闹的, 引人发笑的
examples: The comedian's story about his first job was so hilarious that the whole audience was in tears.

gloss_en: extremely funny, causing a lot of laughter.

examples: The lecturer praised the scientist's heterodox ideas that eventually overturned the standard theory.

gloss_en: holding beliefs or opinions that differ from what is generally accepted or orthodox

def_en: occurring or coming into existence after a person's death
examples: The author received a posthumous award for her final novel.

gloss_en: happening, appearing, or continuing after a person's death.

def_en: cause to be unpretentious
def_zh: 使...卑下, 降低...地位, 贬抑
examples: The unexpected defeat humbled the champion, who had assumed victory was certain.

def_en: low or inferior in station or quality
marked by meekness or modesty; not arrogant or prideful
used of unskilled work (especially domestic work)
def_zh: 低下的, 谦逊的, 粗陋的
examples: Despite her fame, she remained humble and always thanked her team first.

gloss_en: not proud; having a modest opinion of your own importance. As a verb, to make someone feel less important

def_en: cause to feel shame; hurt the pride of
def_zh: 使丢脸, 使蒙羞, 屈辱
examples: He felt the coach was trying to humiliate him in front of the whole team.

gloss_en: to make someone feel ashamed or foolish, especially in front of others.

examples: The essay criticized the leader as hypocritical for demanding sacrifices he never made himself.

gloss_en: pretending to have moral standards or beliefs that one does not actually hold

def_en: a proposal intended to explain certain facts or observations
a tentative insight into the natural world; a concept that is not yet verified but that if true would explain certain facts or phenomena
examples: The experiment was designed to test the hypothesis that sleep improves memory.

gloss_en: an idea or explanation that is suggested to account for certain facts and can then be tested to see whether it is true.

examples: The professor warned that being hypercritical of every draft can discourage young writers.

gloss_en: excessively and unreasonably critical, finding fault with small details

examples: In the poetry lecture, the professor pointed out the hyperbole in the line 'I've told you a million times.'

gloss_en: deliberate exaggeration used for emphasis or effect, not meant to be taken literally

def_en: a primate of the family Hominidae
examples: The fossil suggested that this early hominid walked upright at least three million years ago.

gloss_en: a member of the biological family that includes humans and their extinct close relatives, and (in broader use) the great apes.

examples: The sociology lecture focused on intra-cultural differences among regions of the same country.

gloss_en: occurring or existing within a single culture

examples: The biology professor explained how intracellular structures produce the energy a cell needs.

gloss_en: located or occurring inside a cell

examples: The history lecture described how the empire sought to subjugate neighboring territories.

gloss_en: to bring a people or group under complete control or domination, especially by force

def_zh: 力量巨大的事物（机构/官僚制度等）
examples: The economics professor called the corporation a juggernaut that smaller firms could not compete with.

gloss_en: a huge, powerful force or institution that crushes whatever is in its path

def_en: (of handwriting, print, etc.) capable of being read or deciphered
examples: Please write your address in legible handwriting so the letter can be delivered.

gloss_en: clear enough to be read easily.

def_en: music that precedes a fugue or introduces an act in an opera
def_zh: 前奏, 序幕, 序诗, 前奏曲
examples: The border skirmishes proved to be merely a prelude to full-scale war.

def_en: serve as a prelude or opening to
play as a prelude
examples: A tense silence preluded the announcement that the factory would close.

gloss_en: an event or action that comes before and introduces a more important one; also, a short piece of music that introduces a larger work.

def_en: make a more or less disguised reference to
examples: In her speech she alluded to the scandal without naming any of the officials involved.

gloss_en: to mention or refer to someone or something in an indirect way.

examples: Do not delude yourself into thinking that success will come without hard work and sacrifice.

def_en: v be false to; be dishonest with

gloss_en: to make someone believe something that is not true; to deceive.

def_en: derive by reason
def_zh: 引出, 推导出, 引起
examples: The teacher asked open questions to elicit ideas from even the quietest students in the class.

gloss_en: to draw out a response, answer, or reaction from someone.

def_en: variety of sweet eating apples
examples: She picked a shiny Delicious from the bowl, one of her favorite kinds of apple.
The soup smelled so delicious that everyone asked for a second bowl.

gloss_en: having a very pleasant taste or smell.

examples: The business lecture explained how a small company can leverage social media to reach new customers.

gloss_en: the use of something, such as resources or influence, to achieve a desired result

examples: In genetics, a lethal is a factor that causes the death of the organism carrying it.
The snake's venom is lethal enough to kill an adult within hours.

def_en: s of an instrument of certain death

gloss_en: able to cause death; extremely dangerous or deadly.

def_en: a state of comatose torpor (as found in sleeping sickness)
def_zh: 昏睡, 瞌睡, 无力气
examples: A poor diet and lack of sleep left him in a state of constant lethargy.

gloss_en: a lack of energy and enthusiasm that makes you feel tired and unwilling to act.

def_en: expressing yourself readily, clearly, effectively
def_zh: 雄辩的, 有口才的, 有说服力的
examples: The lawyer gave an eloquent speech that convinced the entire jury.

gloss_en: able to express ideas clearly, fluently, and persuasively in speech or writing.

def_zh: 卖弄辞藻的；言辞浮夸的
examples: The writing instructor criticized the essay's grandiloquent style for hiding a weak argument.

gloss_en: using pompous, showy language intended to impress rather than communicate clearly

def_zh: 言简意赅的；不爱说话的
examples: The professor described the philosopher as pauciloquent, saying more in one sentence than others did in a page.

gloss_en: using few words; speaking briefly and sparingly

examples: During the interview she said she wanted to commit herself to environmental research.

gloss_en: to dedicate oneself firmly to a course of action, cause, or promise

def_en: bordering on or living or characteristic of those near the sea
def_zh: 海的, 海上的, 海事的, 沿海的, 海员的
examples: The city's wealth was built on centuries of maritime trade with distant ports.

def_zh: 海事的, 海上的, 海员的

gloss_en: connected with the sea, especially in relation to trade, shipping, or sailing.

def_en: a member of the United States Marine Corps
a soldier who serves both on shipboard and on land
def_zh: 舰队, 水兵, 海景画
examples: The young marine had trained for months before his first overseas deployment.

def_en: of or relating to the sea
of or relating to military personnel who serve both on land and at sea (specifically the U.S. Marine Corps)
def_zh: 海的, 海产的, 海底的, 船舶的, 海运的
examples: Pollution is a serious threat to marine life along the coast.

gloss_en: connected with the sea and the creatures and plants that live in it; also a soldier serving in a naval infantry force.

examples: The forensic science lecture described how bodies are stored in a refrigerated morgue.

gloss_en: a place where dead bodies are kept before burial or examination

examples: The student was mortified when he realized he had cited the wrong source in his presentation.

gloss_en: to make someone feel deeply ashamed or humiliated

examples: In the myth, the goddess falls in love with an ordinary mortal.

def_en: subject to death
unrelenting and deadly
def_zh: 不免一死的, 人类的, 临终的
examples: The story reminds us that all living creatures are mortal and will one day die.

gloss_en: certain to die eventually and unable to live forever; also a human being, as opposed to a god.

examples: The lecturer's monotonous voice soon put half the class to sleep.

def_en: s tediously repetitious or lacking in variety; all work and no play"
s sounded or spoken in a tone unvarying in pitch

gloss_en: boring because it stays the same and never changes

examples: The medieval history lecture noted that manuscripts were carefully copied by monks in the monastery.

gloss_en: a building or community where monks live and practice their religious life

examples: The geology lecture explained that marble is a metamorphic rock formed from limestone.

gloss_en: of rock, formed or changed by great heat and pressure over time

examples: The manufacture of steel requires enormous amounts of energy and raw materials.

def_en: put together out of artificial or natural components or parts; He manufactured a popular cereal"
produce naturally
create or produce in a mechanical way
examples: The factory manufactures thousands of car parts every single day for export.

gloss_en: to make goods in large quantities, usually in a factory using machinery.

def_en: influence or control shrewdly or deviously
hold something in one's hands and move it
control (others or oneself) or influence skillfully, usually to one's advantage
treat manually, as with massage, for therapeutic purposed
def_zh: 操纵, 利用, 操作, 巧妙地处理, 假造
examples: The politician was accused of trying to manipulate public opinion through the media.

gloss_en: to control or influence someone or something cleverly or dishonestly to get what you want.

def_en: proceed or issue forth, as from a source
def_zh: 散发, 发出, 发源
examples: A faint smell of jasmine emanated from the open garden gate.

gloss_en: to come out from a source; to be produced by or spread out from something

examples: The literature professor analyzed the artists' manifesto that launched the new movement.

gloss_en: a public written statement of the aims and principles of a person or group

def_en: a transparent variety of beryl that is blue green in color
examples: The ring was set with a sparkling aquamarine the color of shallow tropical water.

gloss_en: a pale blue-green precious stone; also this pale blue-green color.

examples: The oceanography lecture described how mariculture is used to raise oysters and seaweed along the coast.

gloss_en: the farming of marine plants and animals in the sea or in seawater tanks

def_en: (genetics) any event that changes genetic structure; any alteration in the inherited nucleic acid sequence of the genotype of an organism
a change or alteration in form or qualities
def_zh: 变化, 突变, 元音变化
examples: A single mutation in the gene was enough to make the bacteria resistant to the drug.

gloss_en: a change in the genetic material of a living thing, which can be passed on to its offspring.

examples: The physics professor stressed that the speed of light is an immutable constant of the universe.

gloss_en: unable to be changed or altered

def_en: feeling a need to see others suffer
def_zh: 恶意, 蓄意害人, 怨恨

def_zh: 预谋, 蓄意, 恶意

def_en: a failure to function normally
def_zh: 故障, 失灵, 疾病

def_en: fail to function or function improperly
def_zh: 发生故障, 不起作用

gloss_en: a failure of a machine or part of the body to work properly; also, to fail to work properly.

def_en: evil or harmful in nature or influence
def_zh: 有害的, 恶性的, 有恶意的

gloss_en: to say false and unpleasant things about someone; harmful or evil in nature.

def_en: a document giving an official instruction or command
a territory surrendered by Turkey or Germany after World War I and put under the tutelage of some other European power until they are able to stand by themselves
the commission that is given to a government and its policies through an electoral victory
def_zh: 命令, 指令, 要求

Federal law mandates that all vehicles be fitted with seat belts before they are sold.

gloss_en: an official order or authority to do something, or the power given to an elected government to act.

def_en: an authoritative direction or instruction to do something
a military unit or region under the control of a single officer
the power or authority to command
availability for use
def_zh: 命令, 指挥, 控制, 部队, 司令部
examples: The military history lecture examined how the general took command of the troops before the battle.

gloss_en: an authoritative order, or the authority and control to direct others

def_en: relating or belonging to or characteristic of a municipality
of or relating to the government of a municipality
def_zh: 市政的, 自治区的, 内政的

gloss_en: relating to a town or city and its local government.

def_en: characteristic of a mother
relating to or derived from one's mother
def_zh: 母亲的, 母系的, 母方的

def_en: the ceremony or sacrament of marriage
def_zh: 结婚, 婚姻生活, 婚礼

def_en: of or relating to the economic system of mercantilism
profit oriented
relating to or characteristic of trade or traders
examples: The port city grew wealthy through its extensive mercantile activity in the sixteenth century.

gloss_en: relating to trade, merchants, or commerce

def_en: connected with or belonging to or used in a navy
def_zh: 海军的, 军舰的, 有舰队的

def_zh: 海军的, 军舰的, 船的

gloss_en: relating to a country's navy or to warships

gloss_en: to sail or travel all the way around something, especially the globe

def_en: a person trained to travel in a spacecraft
def_zh: 太空旅行者, 宇航员
examples: The astronaut spent six months aboard the space station conducting scientific experiments.

gloss_en: a person who is trained to travel and work in a spacecraft.

examples: The chemistry professor said the substance is innocuous in small amounts but dangerous when concentrated.

gloss_en: not harmful or offensive; unlikely to cause damage

def_en: belonging to or active during the night
of or relating to or occurring in the night
examples: Owls are nocturnal hunters, using sharp hearing to catch prey in total darkness.

gloss_en: active at night rather than during the day.

examples: In the music history lecture, the professor played a nocturne to show its calm, dreamy character.

gloss_en: a short musical composition, typically for piano, evoking the mood of night

def_en: speak out against
announce the termination of, as of treaties
give away information about somebody
def_zh: 告发, 抨击, 谴责
examples: Several leaders denounced the attack as an act of pure terrorism.

def_zh: 谴责, 斥责, 告发

gloss_en: to criticize someone or something strongly and publicly

examples: The teacher asked the student to enunciate each word so that the whole class could understand.

def_en: v speak, pronounce, or utter in a certain way
v express or state clearly

gloss_en: to pronounce words clearly and carefully; also, to state an idea or principle clearly.

def_en: speak, pronounce, or utter in a certain way
pronounce judgment on
def_zh: 发音, 宣告, 断言
examples: Many learners find it hard to pronounce the 'th' sound correctly.

gloss_en: to make the sound of a word or letter in a particular way.

def_en: make known; make an announcement
announce publicly or officially
give the names of
foreshadow or presage
def_zh: 宣布, 声称, 显示, 预告
当报幕员, 宣布参加竞选
examples: The company is expected to announce its new product line next month.

gloss_en: To make something known publicly or officially.

examples: In this clause the word functions as a nominal rather than as a true verb.

def_en: relating to or constituting or bearing or giving a name
insignificantly small; a matter of form only (`tokenish' is informal)
pertaining to a noun or to a word group that functions as a noun
of, relating to, or characteristic of an amount that is not adjusted for inflation
def_zh: 名义上的, 名字的, 有名无实的, 稍许的
examples: She was the nominal head of the firm, though her deputy made all the real decisions.

gloss_en: existing in name only and not in reality; also very small in amount compared with the real value.

examples: The biology lecture introduced the Latin nomenclature scientists use to classify living things.

gloss_en: a system of names or terms used in a particular field of study

def_en: propose as a candidate for some honor
put forward; nominate for appointment to an office or for an honor or position
def_zh: 提名, 任命, 命名
examples: The committee decided to nominate her for the leadership role.

def_zh: 提名...为候选人, 指定, 推荐

gloss_en: to formally suggest someone for a position, award, or honour.

examples: In older writing, a poet might describe the eye as the optic through which the soul sees the world.

def_zh: 眼睛的, 视觉的, 光学的
examples: Damage to the optic nerve can cause a serious loss of vision.

def_en: n the organ of sight
a of or relating to or resembling the eye
a relating to or using sight

gloss_en: relating to the eye or to the sense of sight.

def_en: a person skilled in testing for defects of vision in order to prescribe corrective glasses
examples: The optometrist tested my vision and recommended glasses for reading.

gloss_en: a person whose job is to examine people's eyes and prescribe glasses or contact lenses.

examples: The economics professor called the policy myopic because it ignored long-term consequences.

gloss_en: short-sighted; unable to see distant objects clearly, or lacking foresight

examples: The literature lecture discussed how an omniscient narrator can reveal every character's thoughts.

gloss_en: knowing everything; having unlimited knowledge

examples: The philosophy lecture examined the idea of an omnipotent being that controls all events.

gloss_en: having unlimited or very great power; able to do anything

examples: Colorful lanterns adorned the streets during the festival.

def_en: v make more attractive by adding ornament, colour, etc.
v be beautiful to look at
v furnish with power or authority; of kings or emperors

gloss_en: to add something to make a person or thing more attractive or beautiful.

examples: The art history professor pointed to the ornate carvings covering the cathedral's façade.

gloss_en: elaborately or heavily decorated with complex detail

def_en: two words that can be interchanged in a context are said to be synonymous relative to that context
examples: The word 'big' is a synonym for 'large', so you can often use either one.

gloss_en: a word that has the same or nearly the same meaning as another word in the same language

examples: The linguistics lecture used 'bank' as a homonym meaning both a riverside and a financial institution.

gloss_en: a word that is spelled or pronounced the same as another but has a different meaning

def_en: a fictitious name used when the person performs a particular social role
examples: The journalist published her early articles under a pseudonym to protect her identity.

gloss_en: a false name used by a writer or artist instead of their real name.

examples: The robotics lecture showed a humanoid machine designed to walk and grasp objects like a person.

gloss_en: having a form or appearance resembling that of a human

examples: The astronomy professor explained that the earth is not a perfect sphere but a slightly flattened spheroid.

gloss_en: a body that is approximately but not perfectly spherical in shape

def_en: urge or force (a person) to an action; constrain or motivate
def_zh: 推动, 激励, 驱使, 迫使

gloss_en: to force or strongly urge someone to do something

def_en: cause to move back by force or influence
be repellent to; cause aversion in
force or drive back
def_zh: 逐退, 抵制, 使厌恶, 抗御

gloss_en: to drive or push something back; also, to cause a feeling of strong dislike.

def_en: force somebody to do something
necessitate or exact
examples: Financial difficulties compelled the family to sell their home.

gloss_en: to force someone to do something, or to make something happen.

def_en: a propelling force
the act of propelling
examples: Engineers are developing a new form of propulsion to send spacecraft farther into space.

gloss_en: the force that pushes something forward, or the process of driving something forward.

def_en: required by rule
def_zh: 被强制的, 强迫的, 义务的
examples: In most countries, education is compulsory for all children up to a certain age.

gloss_en: required by a law or rule; something you must do.

def_en: the rhythmic contraction and expansion of the arteries with each beat of the heart
the rate at which the heart beats; usually measured to obtain a quick evaluation of a person's health
edible seeds of various pod-bearing plants (peas or beans or lentils etc.)
def_zh: 脉冲, 脉搏, 情绪, 意向, 拍子, 豆类
examples: The nurse checked the patient's pulse before recording it on the chart.

def_en: produce or modulate (as electromagnetic waves) in the form of short bursts or pulses or cause an apparatus to produce pulses
def_zh: 使跳动, 用脉冲调制
examples: Music pulsed through the club as the crowd began to dance.

gloss_en: the regular beating of the heart as felt through the arteries, for example at the wrist.

def_en: the act of imposing something (as a tax or an embargo)
an uncalled-for burden
def_zh: 征收, 课税, 强加, 欺骗
examples: The imposition of new taxes on imports angered local businesses.

gloss_en: the act of officially forcing a rule, tax, or punishment on people; also an unfair demand on someone's time

examples: The environmental studies lecture criticized the huge amount of waste created by disposable cups.

gloss_en: designed to be used once or a few times and then thrown away

examples: The genetics lecture explained how certain genes can predispose people to particular illnesses.

gloss_en: to make someone or something more likely to develop a condition or behave in a certain way

def_en: put down by force or intimidation
block the action of
def_zh: 镇压, 抑制, 压制
examples: She tried to repress her anger and speak calmly to the customer.

gloss_en: to stop yourself from showing a feeling or reaction; also to control a group of people by force.

def_en: be a mystery or bewildering to
def_zh: 使困惑, 使复杂化, 使为难
examples: The sudden change in the readings continued to perplex the scientists for weeks.

def_zh: 困惑, 使复杂化, 使纠缠不清

gloss_en: to confuse someone or make them feel puzzled because something is hard to understand.

def_en: a conceptual whole made up of complicated and related parts
a compound described in terms of the central atom to which other atoms are bound or coordinated
(psychoanalysis) a combination of emotions and impulses that have been rejected from awareness but still influence a person's behavior
def_zh: 综合体, 情结, 络合物
examples: The new shopping complex includes a cinema, several restaurants, and a large supermarket.

def_en: complicated in structure; consisting of interconnected parts
examples: The instructions for assembling the furniture were so complex that I had to ask for help.

gloss_en: Consisting of many different and connected parts, making it difficult to understand or deal with.

examples: The coach warned the team not to become complacent after their early success.

gloss_en: self-satisfied and unaware of possible dangers or shortcomings

def_en: (of a body of water) free from disturbance by heavy waves
def_zh: 平静的, 温和的, 沉着的, 平稳的
examples: The placid lake mirrored the mountains perfectly, its surface undisturbed by any breeze.

gloss_en: calm and peaceful, with little movement or excitement.

examples: Volunteers handed out a pamphlet explaining how residents could reduce their water use during the drought.

def_en: n a small book usually having a paper cover
n a brief treatise on a subject of interest; published in the form of a booklet

gloss_en: a small, thin book with a paper cover that gives information about or argues a particular subject.

examples: The librarian, a lifelong bibliophile, showed students the rare first editions in the collection.

gloss_en: a person who loves or collects books

examples: The professor dismissed astrology as pseudoscience because its claims cannot be tested.

gloss_en: a set of beliefs or practices claimed to be scientific but not based on proper scientific method

examples: The essay warned against pseudointellectual arguments that sound impressive but lack real substance.

gloss_en: a person who pretends to have deep knowledge or intelligence they do not really possess

def_zh: 完成, 达到, 实现, 使完美
examples: With dedication and hard work, you can accomplish your goals.

def_en: v put in effect
v to gain with effort

gloss_en: achieve or complete successfully

def_en: a quantity added (e.g. to make up for a deficiency)
def_zh: 补充物, 增刊, 补充, 补遗, 补编, 附录, 补角
examples: Many older people take a vitamin supplement to keep their bones strong.

def_en: add as a supplement to what seems insufficient
serve as a supplement to
examples: She works part-time in the evenings to supplement her income from teaching.

gloss_en: something added to a thing to make it complete or to make up for a lack

def_en: fill something that had previously been emptied
def_zh: 把...重新补足, 把...再装满
examples: We stopped at a village to replenish our supplies of food and water.

gloss_en: to fill something up again or make it complete again after it has been used or emptied.

def_en: a full supply
def_zh: 充分, 很多, 丰富
examples: The advisor reassured her that there was plenty of time to finish the application.

gloss_en: a large or more than sufficient amount of something

def_zh: 请愿, 诉状, 陈情书, 申请, 祈求, 祷文
examples: Thousands of residents signed a petition asking the council to save the local library.

def_en: write a petition for something to somebody; request formally and in writing
def_zh: 正式请求, 恳求, 请愿
examples: The workers decided to petition the government for better safety regulations in factories.

gloss_en: a formal written request, usually signed by many people, asking those in authority to do something

def_en: a feeling of craving something
def_zh: 食欲, 欲望, 爱好
examples: After the long hike, the children came home with a huge appetite for dinner.

gloss_en: the desire for food; also, a strong wish or liking for something.

def_zh: 永久的, 不断的, 无期限的, 没完没了的, 四季开花的
examples: The two neighbors lived in a state of perpetual disagreement over the fence.

def_en: s continuing forever or indefinitely
s uninterrupted in time and indefinitely long continuing
def_zh: 永远的, 终身的, 持续的

gloss_en: continuing forever or happening again and again without stopping

def_en: cause to continue or prevail
def_zh: 使永存, 使不朽, 保持
examples: Such films only perpetuate harmful stereotypes about people from other cultures.

gloss_en: to make something, especially a bad situation or belief, continue for a long time.

def_en: spread or diffuse through
def_zh: 弥漫, 渗透, 充满, 影响, 感染
examples: The smell of fresh coffee permeated the entire house.

gloss_en: to spread throughout something; to pass into or through every part of something.

def_en: carry out or perform an action
perform a function
give a performance (of something)
def_zh: 进行, 履行, 完成, 执行, 表演
行动, 工作, 执行, 演出
examples: The surgeon performed a complex operation with great skill.

gloss_en: To carry out, accomplish, or fulfill an action, task, or function, or to entertain an audience.

def_en: cause somebody to adopt a certain position, belief, or course of action; twist somebody's arm
def_zh: 劝, 使相信, 恳求, 敦促, 说服
examples: The student tried to persuade her professor to extend the deadline for the essay.

gloss_en: to cause someone to believe or do something through reasoning or argument

examples: In English grammar, the present perfect tense is used to describe actions that started in the past and continue to the present.

def_en: make perfect or complete
def_zh: 使完美, 修改, 使精通, 改善, 使熟练
examples: She spent years perfecting her technique as a classical pianist.

def_en: being complete of its kind and without defect or blemish
precisely accurate or exact
def_zh: 完美的, 完好的, 理想的, 熟练的, 精确的, 完成式的
examples: The weather was absolutely perfect for a picnic in the park.

gloss_en: Having all the required or desirable elements, qualities, or characteristics; as good as it is possible to be.

examples: The librarian suggested she peruse the journal articles before choosing a research topic.

gloss_en: to read or examine something carefully and in detail

def_en: apprehension about what is going to happen
an uncertain cognitive state
excited anticipation of an approaching climax
def_zh: 悬疑, 焦虑, 悬念, 悬而不决
examples: The film keeps the audience in suspense right up to the final scene.

gloss_en: a feeling of nervous excitement or anxiety about what is going to happen next.

def_en: an adornment that hangs from a piece of jewelry (necklace or earring)
def_zh: 垂饰, 下垂物, 补充的东西, 补编
examples: She wore a silver pendant shaped like a tiny star.

gloss_en: A piece of jewelry that hangs from a chain worn around the neck.

def_zh: 即将发生的, 迫切的, 悬空的
examples: Dark clouds and strong winds warned the sailors of an impending storm.

def_en: v be imminent or about to happen
s close in time; about to occur
def_zh: 就要来的, 逼在眼前的, 紧急的

gloss_en: about to happen very soon, especially something unpleasant or worrying.

examples: For any serious hiker, a good map is one of the indispensables you should never leave behind.

def_en: not to be dispensed with; essential
unavoidable
def_zh: 不可缺少的, 责无旁贷的, 绝对必要的
examples: A reliable assistant soon became indispensable to the busy director.

gloss_en: absolutely necessary and impossible to do without; also, a person or thing that is essential.

def_en: high in price or charging high prices
def_zh: 贵的, 奢华的, 费用浩大的, 乱化钱的
examples: The hotel room was surprisingly inexpensive, considering its prime location in the city center.

def_zh: 高价的, 昂贵的, 浪费的

gloss_en: costing a lot of money

examples: The anthropology lecture examined how a patriarchic society passes property through the male line.

gloss_en: relating to a social system in which men hold the primary power and authority

def_en: inspired by love for your country
def_zh: 爱国的, 有爱国心的
examples: Crowds waved flags and sang patriotic songs during the national day celebrations.

def_zh: 爱国的, 有爱国心的

gloss_en: having or showing great love and loyal support for your country.

examples: The pharmacology lecture explained how storage conditions affect a drug's potency.

gloss_en: the strength, effectiveness, or power of something

def_en: the inherent capacity for coming into being
def_zh: 潜在性, 可能性, 潜力, 潜能, 势, 位
examples: The young athlete showed great potential to become a world champion.

def_en: existing in possibility
def_zh: 有潜力的, 可能的, 潜在的
examples: The company is looking for potential investors to fund its expansion.

gloss_en: The possibility of something happening or being developed in the future.

examples: The professor argued that without funding the new law would be impotent.

gloss_en: lacking the power or ability to take effective action

def_en: belonging to or inherited from one's father
characteristic of a father
def_zh: 父亲的, 父系的, 似父亲的, 得自父亲的, 家长式统治的
examples: He inherited the family farm from his paternal grandfather.

gloss_en: Relating to a father or fatherhood; or inherited or received through one's father.

def_en: the outside boundary or surface of something
def_zh: 外围, 边缘地区, 圆周
examples: New housing developments have sprung up on the periphery of the city.

gloss_en: the outer edge or boundary of an area, or a position away from the center of importance.

examples: The art history lecture used a pictorial timeline to show how painting styles evolved.

gloss_en: expressed in or relating to pictures or illustration

examples: The mural depicts workers building the city's first railway line.

def_en: v show in, or as in, a picture
v give a description of
v make a portrait of

gloss_en: to show or represent something in a picture, or to describe it in words.

examples: The history lecture described how the state moved to expropriate large private estates.

gloss_en: to take property from its owner for public use or by authority, often without full consent

examples: The proprietor of the small hotel greeted every guest by name.

def_en: n (law) someone who owns (is legal possessor of) a business
def_zh: 所有人, 资本主, 业主

gloss_en: a person who owns a business, especially a shop, hotel, or similar establishment

def_en: something owned; any tangible or intangible possession that is owned by someone;
a basic or essential attribute shared by all members of a class
a construct whereby objects or individuals can be distinguished
any movable articles or objects used on the set of a play or movie
def_zh: 财产, 所有权, 性质, 属性
examples: The chemical properties of this new material make it ideal for construction.

gloss_en: A thing or things belonging to someone; an attribute, quality, or characteristic of something.

def_en: generally agreed upon; not subject to dispute
def_zh: 无可争辩的, 无异议的
examples: By the end of the decade she was the undisputed leader of the reform movement.

def_zh: 无需争论的, 无疑问的, 确然无疑的

gloss_en: accepted by everyone and not questioned or argued about

examples: Training the model required far more compute than the lab could afford.
The software can compute the total cost of the project in seconds.

def_en: v make a mathematical calculation or computation

gloss_en: to calculate an amount or figure, especially using a machine or mathematics.

examples: The medical lecture introduced pathology as the study of how disease changes the body's tissues.

gloss_en: the scientific study of the causes and effects of diseases

examples: The psychology lecture cautioned that the term psychopath is often misused in popular media.

gloss_en: a person with a serious mental disorder marked by antisocial and often aggressive behavior

def_en: any disease-producing agent (especially a virus or bacterium or other microorganism)
examples: Washing your hands regularly removes many of the pathogens that spread common illnesses.

gloss_en: a tiny organism, such as a virus or bacterium, that causes disease.

examples: The student implored the committee to reconsider his rejected application.

gloss_en: to beg someone earnestly or desperately to do something

def_en: express strong disapproval of
regret strongly
def_zh: 悲悼, 哀叹, 对...深感遗憾
examples: The organization issued a statement to deplore the use of violence against protesters.

gloss_en: to strongly disapprove of and condemn something as bad or wrong.

def_zh: 易弯的, 柔顺的, 圆通的
examples: Soaking the wood in water makes it pliable enough to bend into a curve.

def_en: s susceptible to being led or directed
s capable of being shaped or bent or drawn out
s able to adjust readily to different conditions
s capable of being bent or flexed or twisted without breaking

gloss_en: easily bent without breaking; also, easily influenced or persuaded.

def_en: combine by multiplication
combine or increase by multiplication
examples: If you multiply seven by eight, you get fifty-six.

def_en: in several ways; in a multiple manner
examples: The report was multiply reviewed, checked by several editors before publication.

gloss_en: to increase greatly in number or amount; or to add a number to itself a given number of times.

def_zh: 教育学, 教授法, 教学工作
examples: Her research explores how digital tools are reshaping pedagogy in university classrooms.

def_en: n the principles and methods of instruction
n the profession of a teacher
n the activities of educating or instructing; activities that impart knowledge or skill

gloss_en: the methods and practice of teaching, especially as an academic subject.

def_en: a person who travels by foot
examples: A new crossing was built to help pedestrians cross the busy road safely.

def_en: lacking wit or imagination
def_zh: 人行的, 徒步的, 呆板的, 通俗的, 平淡无奇的
examples: The critics found the novel dull and its plot rather pedestrian.

gloss_en: a person who is walking, especially in a town or city rather than travelling in a vehicle

def_en: a trite or obvious remark
def_zh: 单调, 平凡, 陈腐, 陈词滥调
examples: Instead of offering real solutions, the politician fell back on tired platitudes about hope and change.

gloss_en: a remark or statement that has been used so often that it is boring and meaningless.

def_en: a picture (or series of pictures) representing a continuous scene
def_zh: 全景, 全貌, 概论, 范围
examples: From the summit, hikers enjoyed a stunning panorama of the entire valley.

gloss_en: a wide, unbroken view of a large area; also a broad overview of a subject.

def_en: (Greek mythology) the goddess of healing; daughter of Aesculapius and sister of Hygeia
hypothetical remedy for all ills or diseases; once sought by the alchemists
examples: Technology alone is no panacea for the deep problems facing modern education.

gloss_en: something believed to be a solution or cure for all problems or diseases.

examples: The public health lecture examined how a pandemic can overwhelm hospitals within weeks.

gloss_en: an outbreak of a disease that spreads across many countries or the whole world

def_en: having or showing feelings of unwarranted importance out of overbearing pride
examples: His arrogant manner annoyed his colleagues, who felt he never listened to anyone else's ideas.

gloss_en: behaving in a proud, unpleasant way, believing you are better or more important than other people.

def_en: math: determine the length of
set straight or right
convert into direct current
def_zh: 订正, 矫正, 改正, 调正, 校正, 精馏, 整流
examples: The bank promised to rectify the error and refund the money to my account within a week.

gloss_en: to correct something that is wrong.

def_zh: 使竖立, 使直立, 树立, 建立
examples: The workers erected a temporary stage in the park for the summer concert.

def_en: upright in position or posture
def_zh: 直立的, 竖立的, 笔直的
examples: The soldier stood erect and motionless as the general inspected the troops.

gloss_en: to build or set up a structure; also, standing straight up in a vertical position.

def_en: a mild rebuke or criticism
disgrace or shame
examples: Her calm acceptance of the loss was beyond reproach and earned the respect of her rivals.

def_en: express criticism towards
def_zh: 责备, 申斥, 使丢脸
examples: The coach gently reproached the players for arriving late to practice.

gloss_en: to express disapproval of or blame toward someone for something they have done; also, criticism or blame directed at someone.

def_en: `Johnny' was applied as a nickname for Confederate soldiers by the Federal soldiers in the American Civil War; `greyback' derived from their grey Confederate uniforms
examples: The rebels seized the radio station and announced that the government had been overthrown.

def_en: take part in a rebellion; renounce a former allegiance
break with established customs
def_zh: 造反, 反抗, 抵抗, 反感
examples: As a teenager he began to rebel against the strict rules his parents had set at home.
Rebel troops advanced steadily toward the capital during the final weeks of the war.

gloss_en: a person who fights against or refuses to obey a government or an authority.

def_en: a coming to or returning home
the occurrence of a change in direction back in the opposite direction
the act of going back to a prior location
the income or profit arising from such transactions as the sale of land or other property
def_zh: 回来, 返回, 来回票, 归还, 报答, 利润率, 报告书
examples: The company's return on investment was higher than expected.

def_zh: 返回, 归还, 回来
归还, 还, 回报, 产生, 反射, 报告, 申报, 退回
examples: I will return to my hometown next month.

def_zh: 返回的, 回程的, 报答的, 反向的, 重现的, 复原的
examples: The return journey was long and tiring.

gloss_en: to come or go back to a place

def_en: an essay or article that gives a critical evaluation (as of a book or play)
(accounting) a service (less exhaustive than an audit) that provides some assurance to interested parties as to the reliability of financial data
a periodical that publishes critical essays on current affairs or literature or art
(law) a judicial reexamination of the proceedings of a court (especially by an appellate court)
def_zh: 检讨, 复习, 回顾, 检阅, 评论
examples: The company conducts an annual performance review for all employees.

def_zh: 温习, 检讨, 评论, 再检察, 复审
examples: Before submitting the report, I need to review all the data carefully.

gloss_en: A formal assessment of something with the intention of instituting change if necessary; or a critical appraisal of a book, play, or film.

def_en: push for something
make attractive or acceptable
def_zh: 推荐, 介绍, 劝告, 使受欢迎, 托付
examples: The travel agent recommended a quiet beach resort for our vacation.

gloss_en: to suggest that someone or something would be good or suitable for a particular purpose or job

def_en: express approval of
present as worthy of regard, kindness, or confidence
give to in charge
express a good opinion of
def_zh: 嘉奖, 委托给, 推荐
examples: The firefighters were commended for their bravery in rescuing the family from the burning house.

gloss_en: to praise someone or something formally or publicly.

def_zh: adj. 必不可少的；n. 必需品
examples: The advisor explained that a strong essay is a requisite part of the application.

gloss_en: required or necessary for a particular purpose; a thing that is needed

def_zh: 政权, 当权期间, 政体, 社会制度, 体制, 情态
examples: The new democratic regime faced significant challenges in rebuilding the country's infrastructure.

def_en: n the organization that is the governing authority of a political unit
n (medicine) a systematic plan for therapy (often including diet)

gloss_en: a system of government or a period when a particular government is in power

examples: The history lecture described how each regiment was trained to move in tight formation.

gloss_en: a large organized military unit; also, to control something in a strict, systematic way

def_en: sturdy and strong in form, constitution, or construction
strong enough to withstand or overcome intellectual challenges or adversity
rough and crude
def_zh: 健康的, 强健的, 要用力气的, 坚定的, 粗野的
examples: The company reported robust sales despite the difficult economy.

gloss_en: strong and healthy, or able to withstand difficult conditions.

def_en: support with evidence or authority or make more certain or confirm
examples: Two witnesses corroborated the driver's account of the accident.

def_zh: 确证, 确定, 证实

gloss_en: to support or confirm a statement or theory with additional evidence.

def_en: a symptom consisting of the involuntary expulsion of air from the nose
examples: A loud sneeze echoed across the silent library.

def_en: exhale spasmodically, as when an irritant entered one's nose
examples: The dust made her sneeze several times before she left the room.

gloss_en: To suddenly and involuntarily force air out through the nose and mouth; the act of doing this.

def_en: a person who is regarded as underhanded and furtive and contemptible
def_zh: 鬼鬼祟祟的人, 偷偷摸摸的行为, 帆布胶底运动鞋
examples: Everyone considered him a sneak because he was always secretly reporting his classmates to the teacher.

def_en: to go stealthily or furtively
put, bring, or take in a secretive or furtive manner
examples: The children tried to sneak downstairs to see the presents before their parents woke up.
The company launched a sneak attack on its rival by cutting prices overnight.

gloss_en: to move quietly and secretly in order to avoid being noticed

examples: The professor warned that a sneaky attempt to copy sources would count as plagiarism.

gloss_en: behaving in a secretive, deceitful, or underhanded way

examples: The archaeology lecture used stratigraphy to date the artifacts by the layer they were buried in.

gloss_en: the branch of geology that studies rock layers and their order of formation

examples: The guests were treated to a sumptuous banquet of rare dishes and fine wines.

def_en: s rich and superior in quality

gloss_en: impressively rich, luxurious, and expensive.

def_en: (law) an inference of the truth of a fact from other facts proved or admitted or judicially noticed
audacious (even arrogant) behavior that you have no right to
a kind of discourtesy in the form of an act of presuming
def_zh: 专横, 放肆, 假定, 推测
examples: The legal system rests on the presumption that a person is innocent until proven guilty.

def_zh: 假定, 推测, 设想

gloss_en: something that is believed to be true because it is very likely, even without proof; also behaviour that is too bold or confident

examples: The literature professor described the character's decline into a dissolute lifestyle.

gloss_en: lacking moral restraint; given to immoral or self-indulgent behavior

examples: In the negotiations he played the resolute, refusing to yield on any point.

def_en: firm in purpose or belief; characterized by firmness and determination
def_zh: 坚决的, 刚毅的, 毅然的
examples: Despite the setbacks, the team remained resolute in their commitment to finish the project on time.

gloss_en: firmly determined to do something and not easily discouraged.

def_en: crushed by grief
examples: After the mine closed, the town became a desolate stretch of empty streets and boarded-up shops.

gloss_en: (of a place) empty and bare in a way that feels bleak and lonely.

def_en: feel bitter or indignant about
def_zh: 愤恨, 憎恶, 怨恨
examples: She began to resent the long hours she had to work while her colleagues left early.

gloss_en: to feel bitter or angry about something because you think it is unfair.

def_en: the form of a literary work submitted for publication
handwritten book or document
def_zh: 手稿, 原稿, 底稿
examples: The novelist sent her completed manuscript to three different publishers.
The library keeps several manuscript letters written by the poet himself.

gloss_en: the original text of a book or document, especially before it is printed

examples: Historians ascribe the empire's collapse to a combination of war and famine.

def_en: v attribute or credit to

gloss_en: to say or believe that something is caused by, belongs to, or comes from a particular person or thing.

def_en: draw a line around
to draw a geometric figure around another figure so that the two are in contact but do not intersect
examples: The new regulations sharply circumscribe the power of local officials.

gloss_en: to limit or restrict something within certain boundaries

def_en: a condition that accompanies or influences some event or activity
information that should be kept in mind when making a decision
formal ceremony about important occasions
def_zh: 环境, 状况, 事件
examples: Given the difficult circumstances, we were pleased to achieve such a positive result.

gloss_en: a fact or condition connected with or relevant to an event or action

examples: During the expedition, the explorers had to subsist on dried meat and melted snow for weeks.

def_en: v support oneself

gloss_en: to stay alive, especially with very little food or money.

examples: The professor clarified the policy with respect to late assignments.

gloss_en: concerning or in relation to a particular matter

def_en: an example regarded as typical of its class
a bit of tissue or blood or urine that is taken for diagnostic purposes
def_zh: 样品, 标本, 试料
examples: The museum displays a rare specimen of a butterfly found only in the rainforest.

gloss_en: a single example of something, especially one studied or collected as a scientific sample.

examples: The instructor praised the report for its perspicuous explanation of a difficult theory.

gloss_en: clearly expressed and easy to understand

examples: The chemistry lab instructor showed students how to light the spirit burner safely.

gloss_en: a small lamp that burns alcohol to provide a flame for heating in a laboratory

examples: The biology lecture explained how plant cells respire even when they are not carrying out photosynthesis.

gloss_en: to take air into the body and release it; to breathe

def_en: engage in plotting or enter into a conspiracy, swear together
act in unison or agreement and in secret towards a deceitful or illegal purpose
def_zh: 阴谋, 协力, 共谋
examples: The rebels were accused of conspiring to overthrow the government.

gloss_en: to secretly plan with others to do something harmful or illegal; also, of events, to combine and seem to work together toward a result.

examples: Many young musicians aspire to perform one day at a world-famous concert hall.

def_en: v have an ambitious plan or a lofty goal

gloss_en: to have a strong desire or ambition to achieve something.

def_en: a will to succeed
a manner of articulation involving an audible release of breath
def_zh: 热望, 志向, 渴望
examples: Her lifelong aspiration was to become a surgeon and work in rural hospitals.

def_zh: 吸入; 吸[引], 吸引术

gloss_en: a strong hope or ambition to achieve something.

def_en: motivation deriving logically from ethical or moral principles that govern a person's thoughts and actions
conformity to one's own sense of right conduct
a feeling of shame when you do something immoral
examples: Her conscience wouldn't let her keep the extra change the cashier had mistakenly given her.

def_zh: 良心, 道德感, 正义感

gloss_en: the inner sense of what is right and wrong that guides a person's behavior and makes them feel guilty about doing something bad.

def_en: characterized by extreme care and great effort
guided by or in accordance with conscience or sense of right and wrong
def_zh: 有责任心的, 负责的, 本着良心的
examples: A conscientious student, she checked every calculation twice before handing in her report.

def_zh: 凭良心做的, 有道德感的

gloss_en: taking great care to do work or duties thoroughly and correctly

def_en: marked by care and persistent effort
examples: Her assiduous note-taking helped her earn the highest grade in the class.

gloss_en: showing great care, attention, and persistent effort in doing something.

def_en: beguiling but harmful
intended to entrap
working or spreading in a hidden and usually injurious way
def_zh: 隐伏的, 阴险的, 狡猾的
examples: High blood pressure is an insidious condition that often shows no early symptoms.

gloss_en: spreading gradually and harmfully in a way that is not easily noticed until damage is done.

def_en: wear off or die down
sink to a lower level or form a depression
sink down or precipitate
def_zh: 沉淀, 沉降, 平息
examples: The doctor said the swelling would gradually subside within a few days.

gloss_en: to become less strong, active, or intense; to die down.

def_zh: 沉淀, 下沉, 陷没
examples: Heavy pumping of groundwater caused serious subsidence beneath the city.

def_en: n an abatement in intensity or degree (as in the manifestations of a disease)
n a gradual sinking to a lower level
n the sudden collapse of something into a hollow beneath it

gloss_en: the gradual sinking of the ground or a building to a lower level

def_zh: 代替, 取代, 接替
examples: Digital cameras have almost entirely superseded traditional film cameras.

def_en: v take the place or move into the position of
def_zh: 替代, 替换, 充任

gloss_en: to take the place of something older or less effective.

examples: The professor discussed how the printing press aided the dissemination of scientific knowledge.

gloss_en: the act of spreading information or ideas widely

def_en: keep away from others
def_zh: 使隔离, 使孤立, 隔开
examples: The writer chose to seclude herself in a mountain cabin to finish her novel.

gloss_en: to keep someone or something away from other people; to isolate.

def_en: a garment that can be purchased separately and worn in combinations with other garments
examples: The professor handed each student a separate of her latest journal article to read.

gloss_en: a separately printed copy of an article extracted from a larger publication; an offprint

def_en: conduct a prosecution in a court of law
bring a criminal action against (in a trial)
carry out or participate in an activity; be involved in
def_zh: 告发, 起诉, 彻底进行, 执行, 从事
告发, 起诉, 作检察官
examples: The company was prosecuted for dumping toxic waste into the river.

gloss_en: to officially charge someone with a crime and try to prove their guilt in a court of law

def_en: a phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon
the outcome of an event especially as relative to an individual
having important effects or influence
examples: The severe drought had serious consequences for the region's agricultural industry.

def_zh: 结果, 后果, 推断

gloss_en: A result or effect of an action or condition.

examples: In the medieval church, the seculars lived and worked among ordinary people rather than in monasteries.

def_en: of or relating to the doctrine that rejects religion and religious considerations
of or relating to clergy not bound by monastic vows
def_zh: 世俗的, 现世的, 长期的
examples: The country has a secular government that keeps religion separate from the state.

gloss_en: not connected with religious or spiritual matters.

def_zh: 延长, 拖延, 伸出, 绘制
examples: Neither side wished to protract the negotiations, so a settlement was reached within a week.

def_en: v lengthen in time; cause to be or last longer
def_zh: 延迟, 拖延, 延长

gloss_en: to make something last longer than necessary; to prolong or draw out.

examples: The seminar discussed the journal's retraction of a study after its data were found to be flawed.

gloss_en: a formal withdrawal of a previous statement, claim, or published finding

def_en: (law) a formal accusation against somebody (often in a court of law)
statements affirming or denying certain matters of fact that you are prepared to prove
def_zh: 断言, 主张, 申辩
examples: The company firmly denied the allegation that it had polluted the local river.

def_zh: 声明, 事实陈述, 断言

gloss_en: a public statement claiming that someone has done something wrong, usually made without proof.

examples: The history lecture noted that the scientist was of Italian extraction but worked in Britain.

gloss_en: the origin or ancestry a person is descended from

examples: A few small typos do not detract from the overall quality of the report.

def_en: v take away a part from; diminish

gloss_en: to make something seem less valuable or impressive.

def_en: a level shelf of land interrupting a declivity (with steep slopes above and below)
a row of houses built in a similar style and having common dividing walls (or the street on which they face)
def_zh: 阶地, 梯田, 房屋之平顶, 阳台, 沿岸防地, 露台
examples: We had breakfast on the terrace overlooking the sea.

def_en: provide (a house) with a terrace
make into terraces as for cultivation
def_zh: 使成梯田, 使成有平台屋顶
examples: Farmers terraced the steep hillsides so they could grow rice.
They bought a small terrace house on a quiet street in the old town.

gloss_en: a flat, raised area of ground or paving next to a building; also a row of houses joined together.

def_en: an exercise device consisting of an endless belt on which a person can walk or jog without changing place
a mill that is powered by men or animals walking on a circular belt or climbing steps
a job involving drudgery and confinement
def_zh: 踏车, 令人厌倦的工作
examples: She runs on the treadmill for half an hour every morning before work.

gloss_en: an exercise machine with a moving belt on which you walk or run while staying in one place; also, a dull, tiring routine that never seems to change.

examples: A few nails protruded from the old fence, so we walked past it carefully.

def_en: v extend out or project in space
v bulge outward
v swell or protrude outwards

gloss_en: to stick out from a surface or beyond an edge.

def_en: enter uninvited
search or inquire in a meddlesome way
thrust oneself in as if by force
examples: I hope I'm not intruding, but I need to ask you something urgent.

gloss_en: to enter a place or situation where you are not wanted or invited, or to interrupt someone.

def_en: disturb in mind or make uneasy or cause to be worried or alarmed
disturb or interfere with the usual path of an electron or atom
cause a celestial body to deviate from a theoretically regular orbital motion, especially as a result of interposed or extraordinary gravitational pull
def_zh: 扰乱, 使混乱, 使心绪不宁
examples: The sudden change in the data seemed to perturb the researchers.

gloss_en: to make someone anxious or worried, or to disturb the normal state of something.

def_en: unstable flow of a liquid or gas
instability in the atmosphere
a state of violent disturbance and disorder (as in politics or social conditions generally)
def_zh: 混乱, 动荡, 骚乱, 紊流
examples: The pilot warned passengers to fasten their seatbelts as the plane hit turbulence.

gloss_en: violent or unsteady movement of air or water; also a state of confusion or disorder.

def_zh: 狂暴的, 骚乱的, 紊乱的
examples: The country went through a turbulent period after the sudden fall of its government.

def_en: s characterized by unrest or disorder or insubordination
s (of a liquid) agitated vigorously; in a state of turbulence

gloss_en: full of sudden changes, conflict, or violent movement; not calm or steady.

def_en: move deeply
damage as if by shaking or jarring
def_zh: 扰乱, 妨碍, 使不安
examples: Please do not disturb the students while they are taking the exam.

gloss_en: to interrupt someone or interfere with something, or to make someone feel worried or upset.

examples: The professor encouraged the timorous student to speak up more during discussions.

gloss_en: showing a lack of courage or confidence; easily frightened

def_zh: 威慑力量(或因素), 制止物
examples: Heavy fines act as a strong deterrent against illegal parking in the city center.

def_en: tending to deter
def_zh: 制止的, 威慑的, 遏制的
examples: The bright lights had a deterrent effect on burglars looking for an easy target.

gloss_en: something that makes people less likely to do something, especially by making them afraid of the consequences.

examples: The philosophy lecture outlined the central tenets of the ancient school of thought.

gloss_en: a principle or belief firmly held to be true, especially within a system of thought

def_en: cause to be attached
be attached; be in contact with
become attached
def_zh: 附上, 使依附, 使附属, 使喜爱, 系, 缚
附属, 归属, 联系在一起
examples: Please attach the relevant documents to your email before sending it.

def_zh: 挂接服务器命令, 关联, 挂接, 附加

gloss_en: fasten or join (something) to something else

def_en: send from one person or place to another
def_zh: 传输, 传染, 传达, 遗传, 发射, 传播
发射信号, 留传下来
examples: The satellite transmits television signals to millions of homes across the continent.

gloss_en: to send or pass something from one person, place, or thing to another.

def_en: a change from one place or state or subject or stage to another
a musical passage moving from one key to another
a passage that connects a topic to one that follows
def_zh: 转变, 转换, 变迁, 过渡时期, 临时转调
examples: The transition from school to full-time work can be difficult for many young people.

def_en: cause to convert or undergo a transition
examples: The company is gradually transitioning to renewable energy for all of its factories.

gloss_en: the process of changing from one state, condition, or stage to another.

def_en: subject to a mathematical transformation
change or alter in form, appearance, or nature
change in outward structure or looks
convert (one form of energy) to another
def_zh: 使转换, 改变, 改造, 使...变形
改变, 转化, 变换
examples: The new technology has the potential to transform the way we live and work.

gloss_en: to change something completely, especially in a way that improves it

def_zh: 越过, 横贯, 横断物, 屏障, 否认, 反驳
examples: The climbers made a difficult traverse across the icy face of the mountain.

def_en: travel across or pass over
deny formally (an allegation of fact by the opposing party) in a legal suit
def_zh: 横过, 穿过, 经过, 详细研究, 反对, 在...来回移动
横越, 横断, 旋转, 来回移动
examples: The expedition had to traverse hundreds of miles of frozen desert.
A traverse beam was fitted across the frame to hold the two walls in place.

gloss_en: to travel or move across or through an area; as a noun, a route or path that crosses something.

examples: The physics lecture calculated the trajectory of the projectile as it arced through the air.

gloss_en: the curved path that a moving object follows through space

def_en: act in disregard of laws, rules, contracts, or promises
spread over land, especially along a subsiding shoreline
pass beyond (limits or boundaries)
def_zh: 违反, 犯罪, 侵犯
examples: Anyone who transgresses the club's code of conduct may be asked to leave.

gloss_en: to break a law, rule, or moral principle

examples: The seminar tackled the contentious question of how the funds should be divided.

gloss_en: likely to cause disagreement or argument; controversial

def_en: something that acts like a tentacle in its ability to grasp and hold
any of various elongated tactile or prehensile flexible organs that occur on the head or near the mouth in many animals; used for feeling or grasping or locomotion
def_zh: 触须, 触手, 触角
examples: The octopus reached out with one long tentacle and pulled the crab toward its mouth.

gloss_en: one of the long flexible arm-like parts of some animals such as octopuses, used for feeling and grasping.

def_en: making claim to or creating an appearance of (often undeserved) importance or distinction
def_zh: 自负的, 自命不凡的, 夸耀的
examples: The restaurant's pretentious menu used long, fancy words for very ordinary dishes.

gloss_en: trying to appear more important, intelligent, or cultured than you really are.

def_en: a reflex motion caused by cold or fear or excitement
examples: There was a tremble in her voice as she read the sad news aloud.

def_en: move or jerk quickly and involuntarily up and down or sideways
def_zh: 战栗, 忧虑, 摇晃
examples: His hands began to tremble as he opened the envelope with his exam results.

gloss_en: to shake slightly, especially because of fear, cold, or strong emotion.

def_zh: 巨大的, 非常的, 可怕的
examples: The rescue team made a tremendous effort to reach the trapped miners in time.

def_en: s extraordinarily large in size or extent or amount or power or degree; that a whole civilization should be dependent on technology"- Walter Lippman
s extraordinarily good or great; used especially as intensifiers
s extreme in degree or extent or amount or impact

gloss_en: very great in amount, scale, or intensity; extremely large or impressive

def_en: a system of words used to name things in a particular discipline
examples: Before reading the research paper, students must learn the basic terminology of the field.

gloss_en: the special words and expressions used in a particular subject or field.

def_zh: 结束, 终止, 满期
使停止, 使结束, 使终止
examples: The company decided to terminate his contract after the scandal.
The terminate node marks the point where the process comes to a stop.

def_en: v bring to an end or halt
v have an end, in a temporal, spatial, or quantitative sense; either spatial or metaphorical
v be the end of; be the last or concluding part of
v terminate the employment of; discharge from an office or position

gloss_en: to bring something to an end, or to come to an end

def_en: a decrease in size of an organ caused by disease or disuse
any weakening or degeneration (especially through lack of use)
examples: Weeks in a cast led to noticeable muscle atrophy in his injured leg.

def_en: undergo atrophy
examples: Without regular practice, language skills tend to atrophy over time.

gloss_en: the gradual weakening or wasting away of a body part or ability from lack of use; to waste away in this manner.

def_en: practice evasion
use cunning or deceit to escape or avoid
def_zh: (巧妙地)逃脱, 规避, 逃避
examples: The suspect managed to evade the police for several weeks by moving from city to city.

gloss_en: to cleverly escape or avoid someone or something, or to avoid doing something you are supposed to do.

def_en: march aggressively into another's territory by military force for the purposes of conquest and occupation
occupy in large numbers or live on a host
penetrate or assault, in a harmful or injurious way
def_zh: 侵入, 拥入, 侵略, 侵袭
examples: The empire sent its armies to invade the neighbouring kingdom and seize its rich farmland.

def_zh: 强入, 侵犯, 侵略

gloss_en: to enter a country or territory by force, usually with an army, in order to take control of it.

def_en: destroy completely
enjoy avidly
eat immoderately
eat greedily
def_zh: 吞食, 挥霍, 吞没
examples: After the long hike, the children devoured everything on the table within minutes.

gloss_en: to eat something eagerly and quickly; also, to consume or destroy something completely.

def_zh: 贪吃的, 狼吞虎咽的, 贪婪的
examples: As a voracious reader, she often finished two or three novels in a single week.

def_en: s excessively greedy and grasping
s devouring or craving food in great quantities

gloss_en: wanting or eating great amounts of food; also, extremely eager for something such as knowledge.

def_en: active strength of body or mind
examples: Despite his age, he tackled the garden work with surprising vigor.

gloss_en: physical or mental energy, strength, and enthusiasm.

def_zh: 警戒, 警觉, 失眠症
examples: Constant vigilance by the security team prevented any breach of the building.

def_en: n the process of paying close and continuous attention
n vigilant attentiveness

gloss_en: the quality of watching carefully and continuously for possible danger or problems

def_en: the transfer of heat through a fluid (liquid or gas) caused by molecular motion
(meteorology) the vertical movement of heat or other properties by massive motion within the atmosphere
def_zh: 传送, 对流, 传导
examples: Warm air rises by convection, carrying heat to the upper part of the room.

gloss_en: the transfer of heat through a liquid or gas caused by the movement of the warmer parts.

examples: The biology lecture identified the mosquito as the main vector for spreading the disease.

gloss_en: a quantity having both magnitude and direction; also, an organism that carries and transmits a disease

def_en: arrival that has been awaited (especially of something momentous)
the season including the four Sundays preceding Christmas
examples: The advent of the internet transformed the way people communicate.

gloss_en: the arrival or beginning of something important, especially something new.

def_zh: 违反, 触犯, 否认, 反驳, 抵触, 与...冲突
examples: Building a factory here would contravene local environmental regulations.

def_en: v go against, as of rules and laws
v deny the truth of
def_zh: 触法, 违反, 否定

gloss_en: to do something that is not allowed by a rule, law, or agreement.

def_en: meet formally
def_zh: 集合, 召集, 召唤
examples: The committee will convene next week to discuss the proposed budget cuts.

gloss_en: to come together or bring people together for a formal meeting.

examples: It was evident from her tone that she disagreed with the decision.

def_en: s clearly revealed to the mind or the senses or judgment
s capable of being seen or noticed

gloss_en: easily seen or understood; obvious.

def_en: distinctly dissimilar or unlike
examples: The city is home to a diverse population drawn from every continent.

gloss_en: very different from one another; including many different kinds of people or things

def_en: send on a course or in a direction different from the planned or intended one
withdraw (money) and move into a different location, often secretly and with dishonest intentions
examples: Police diverted traffic away from the accident onto a side road.

gloss_en: to change the direction or course of something, or to turn attention away from it.

def_zh: 唤起, 引起, 召(魂)
examples: The smell of fresh bread evoked memories of her grandmother's kitchen.

def_en: v call forth (emotions, feelings, and responses)
v evoke or provoke to appear or occur
v deduce (a principle) or construe (a meaning)
v summon into action or bring into existence, often as if by magic
v call to mind

gloss_en: to bring a feeling, memory, or image into someone's mind.

def_en: cite as an authority; resort to
def_zh: 祈求, 恳求, 实行, 援引, 引起
examples: The lawyer invoked an old statute to defend her client in court.

gloss_en: to refer to a law, rule, or authority as a reason for what you are doing, or to call on something for support

def_en: communicated in the form of words
of or relating to or formed from words in general
of or relating to or formed from a verb
relating to or having facility in the use of words
def_zh: 用言辞的, 言语的, 口头的, 逐字的, 动词的
examples: They had only a verbal agreement, so nothing was written down to prove the terms.

gloss_en: relating to or expressed in words, especially spoken rather than written words.

examples: The professor told us to cut the verbiage from our essays and state our argument in plain, direct sentences.

gloss_en: an excess of unnecessary words; wording that is longer or more complicated than it needs to be

def_en: (law) the findings of a jury on issues of fact submitted to it for decision; can be used in formulating a judgment
def_zh: 裁决, 判决, 判断性意见, 定论, 结论
examples: After three days, the jury finally reached a verdict of not guilty.

def_zh: 定论, 判断, 意见

gloss_en: the official decision made by a jury or judge in a court of law; also, any considered judgment.

def_en: unwillingness to tell lies
def_zh: 说话老实, 老实, 诚实
examples: Historians questioned the veracity of the ancient account of the battle.

gloss_en: the quality of being truthful or accurate.

def_en: confirm the truth of
attach or append a legal verification to (a pleading or petition)
def_zh: 证明, 查证, 证实, 检验, 校验
examples: Before publishing the article, the editor asked the reporter to verify every fact with a second source.

def_zh: 校验, 检验; DOS内部命令:设定写入磁盘时是否验证写入的正确性

gloss_en: to check or prove that something is true, accurate, or correct.

def_en: the quality of having legal force or effectiveness
examples: The scientists questioned the validity of the results because the sample was too small.

gloss_en: the quality of being based on sound reasoning or fact, or of being legally acceptable.

examples: In the economics lecture, the professor explained why a government might deliberately devalue its currency to boost exports.

gloss_en: to reduce the value or worth of something, especially a currency or the importance attached to something

def_en: a carpenter who helps build and launch wooden vessels
examples: The old shipwright ran his hand along the oak hull, checking every joint for signs of weakness.

gloss_en: a skilled worker who builds and repairs wooden ships and boats.

examples: Shakespeare is widely regarded as the greatest playwright in the history of English literature.

def_en: n someone who writes plays

gloss_en: a person who writes plays for the theatre.

examples: According to the lecture on early American industry, a millwright would travel between villages to construct and maintain the water-powered grain mills.

gloss_en: a skilled worker who builds, installs, or repairs mills and mill machinery
